# Heat Trophy: rbk-13-mvp-release-qualification

**Firemark:** ₣BB
**Created:** 260426
**Retired:** 260619
**Status:** retired

## Paddock

## Status — complete; residuals restrung (260618)

The release-qualification **machinery is built and shipped**: `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine,
the gauntlet suite, and the marshal-zero signature gate all landed. This heat's goal is
**complete**.

The actual pristine **runs**, the RELEASE.md **revision**, and the post-success
**cleanup-ceremony documentation** were always gated on "are we actually releasing,"
which is deferred — those live in **₣BU** (stabled, paddock-only), slated fresh when
release time comes.

The ordinary-engineering tail that had collected here — a foundry account-state-invalid
flap fix, an OAuth terminal-failure fail-fast, a build-poll clock split, a shellcheck-coverage
widening, a station-regime relocate, two stale-spec corrections, and a network-diagnostic
repair — was **restrung to ₣Bi** as small pre-MVP loose ends, never release ceremony. The
named RBS0 spec-sync sweep and the fast-tier shellcheck fold both **landed** before the
restring.

Everything below is the design record of the machinery as built — retained as
the authoritative account of what shipped, not as open scope.

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony moved to ₣BU (RELEASE.md revision), where the release-run and doc work now lives.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣Bi — the restrung pre-MVP loose-ends (foundry flap fix, OAuth fail-fast, build-poll split, shellcheck widening, station relocate, stale-spec fixes, scry repair)
- ₣BU — the deferred release runs, RELEASE.md revision, and cleanup-ceremony docs (stabled until release time)
- ₣A_ — surfaced the release-qualification gap
- ₣BC — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition

## Paces

### skirmish-cerebro-standing-run (₢BBABO) [complete]

**[260529-1711] complete**

## Character
Long unattended verification on an operator-independent station (cerebro). Mechanical to launch; exposed to one known transient. Launched out-of-band ahead of the heat's remaining sequence by operator request.

## Goal
Prove the skirmish suite (mini-gauntlet: standing-depot depot→build→crucible chain, no project churn) runs green on cerebro, as the standing-depot counterpart to the full-gauntlet first-run.

## Wrap criterion
Green skirmish tally on cerebro. Not "ran to completion."

## Known exposure
skirmish's canonical-invest + onboarding-sequence ordain path is exposed to the ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID re-invest flap (memo-20260527; repair slated separately in this heat). A failure carrying that signature is the known transient — re-run, don't triage. Any other failure is a real surprise: capture and route.

## On wrap
The wrap summary records the clean skirmish result and explicitly notes it as the green light to retire the rbk- heats now pace-complete: rbk-11-mvp-shellcheck-error-gaps, rbk-12-harmonize-buc-and-buh, rbk-13-mvp-redundancy-cleanup, rbk-09-mvp-regime-sprues-misc (each paddock confirmed shape-complete at retire time).

### foundry-dockerfile-hygiene-module (₢BBABI) [complete]

**[260514-1149] complete**

## Character

Mechanical: a project-wide Dockerfile-syntax constraint, factored into its own BCG library module so kludge and conjure paths share one rule, plus the BCG cleanups in `rbfk_kludge` that BBABH's pristine-state work accumulated.

## Mint

`rbfh` — FoundryHygiene. Library module `Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` (no CLI — utility module per BCG). Public function `rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>` — dies on violation, returns normally on pass. Predicate `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` stays in `rbfk_kludge.sh`; image-presence is not a hygiene concern.

## Hygiene rules

Applied to every line at column 0 starting with `FROM` followed by whitespace, after `#`-column-0 comments are filtered out. Violation → `buc_die` with `{path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line}` precision.

1. No tab character anywhere on the FROM line
2. Second whitespace-delimited token is exactly `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`, or `scratch` (lowercase only)
3. Line does not end with `\`

Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible — the rule constrains token 2 only. Empty Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently; Docker handles its own rejection.

## Callsites

- `rbfk_kludge` in `rbfk_kludge.sh` — after slot resolution, before `docker build`. Checks `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`.
- `rbfd_build` in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — immediately after the existing `test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"` validation, before Cloud Build stitching begins.

Out of scope: `rbfd_graft` — `RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE` is SLSA recipe metadata only, read by `rbfv` for display; graft itself builds from `RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE` (pre-built). Synthesized scratch+COPY Dockerfiles in `rbfd_push_build_context` and Cloud Build pod step scripts (`rbgjv*`, `rbgjb*`, `rbgja*`) are project-controlled, not user-authored.

Each consumer CLI (`rbfk_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`) sources `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` and calls `zrbfh_kindle` in its furnish.

## BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge (BBABH debt)

- Multi-var `local` declarations split one-per-line; `local -r` where assign-once
- `test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0` → `(( ${#z_build_args[@]} ))`
- Extract `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` consolidating the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites; stderr routes to a counter-discriminated forensic temp file under a new `ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX` kindle constant with `z_rbfk_inspect_counter` mutable kindle state.

## Vessel remediation

`rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` currently hardcodes `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` — the exact bypass the hygiene check exists to catch. Declare the upstream via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` in the vessel's `rbrv.env` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing); rewrite Dockerfile head to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`. Lands alongside the hygiene module so the gauntlet remains runnable end-to-end.

## Spec

New subdoc `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` — canonical rule home, follow RBSDS-dns_step shape: short, MCM-annotated (`//axhob_operation` + `//axhos_step` per rule), `{quoin}` references for linked terms. Mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene` for cross-referencing.

- RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with a `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin reference.
- RBSAC ark_conjure — add reference at the build dispatch step.

Update CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: add `RBFH` (module) and `RBSFH` (spec subdoc) entries to the RBK subdirectory section.

## Silks

Current silks `kludge-dockerfile-from-hygiene` no longer fits — work is now project-wide rbfh module with kludge as one of two callsites. Propose `foundry-dockerfile-hygiene-module` or similar via `jjx_relabel` at mount time.

## Verification

- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
- Pristine `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` completes through hallmark publication, hygiene check passes in the conjure path
- `rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter` passes the check post-remediation
- `bash -n` clean on all touched modules

Declare the upstream via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` in the vessel's `rbrv.env` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing); rewrite Dockerfile head to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`. Lands alongside the hygiene module so the gauntlet remains runnable end-to-end.

## Spec

New subdoc `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` — canonical rule home, follow RBSDS-dns_step shape: short, MCM-annotated (`//axhob_operation` + `//axhos_step` per rule), `{quoin}` references for linked terms. Mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene` for cross-referencing.

- RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with a `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin reference.
- RBSAC ark_conjure — add reference at the build dispatch step.

Update CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: add `RBFH` (module) and `RBSFH` (spec subdoc) entries to the RBK subdirectory section.

## Silks

Current silks `kludge-dockerfile-from-hygiene` no longer fits — work is now project-wide rbfh module with kludge as one of two callsites. Propose `foundry-dockerfile-hygiene-module` or similar via `jjx_relabel` at mount time.

## Verification

- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
- Pristine `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` completes through hallmark publication, hygiene check passes in the conjure path
- `rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter` passes the check post-remediation
- `bash -n` clean on all touched modules

**[260513-2148] rough**

## Character

BBABH dropped `--pull=never` because buildx rejects tri-state values (docker/buildx#1889 has been dormant 6 years); forcing `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0` time-bombs against classic-builder deprecation. RBSAK Step 6 currently acknowledges the lost hygiene as an open gap. Project-imposed Dockerfile syntax constraint lets a trivial bash parser restore that hygiene, builder-agnostic and decoupled from Docker flag evolution.

## Scope

Add `zrbfk_dockerfile_hygiene_check` to `rbfk_kludge.sh`, called after slot resolution and before `docker build`. Rules — filter `#`-column-0 comment lines, then for every line starting `FROM ` at column 0:

1. The second whitespace-delimited token must match `${RBF_IMAGE_n}` (n in 1..3) or the literal `scratch`
2. The line must not end with `\` (no continuation)

Violation → line-number-precise diagnostic + buc_die before docker is invoked.

Survey `rbev-vessels/**/Dockerfile` for current compliance and report any non-compliers before the check goes live.

## Constraints

- BCG-clean from the start in rbfk_kludge.sh: split the two multi-var `local` declarations into one-per-line; extract the two `if ! docker image inspect ... >/dev/null 2>&1` sites to a `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` function (consolidates stderr handling); brace+quote the `${#z_build_args[@]}` test
- Project rule applies to vessel Dockerfiles only — hygiene is kludge's concern, not Cloud Build's

## Spec sync

RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with the three rules, the scratch exception, and the rationale.

## Out of scope

- Applying the hygiene check to cloud-build paths
- `FROM --platform=...` accommodation

### rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture (₢BBABJ) [complete]

**[260514-1317] complete**

## Character

Mechanical: mint a thin BCG CLI + two tabtargets for the `rbfh` module landed in `₢BBABI`, then add a ~9-case theurge fixture in the fast tier (lifted into the gauntlet suite) for regression coverage of the Dockerfile hygiene contract. **Test path goes through the new tabtargets** — theurge invokes contract surfaces, not module internals. Synthetic-case patterns follow established `rbtdrf_run_tt` usage with a small sibling helper minted here for expect-fail.

## Mint

- `Tools/rbk/rbfh_cli.sh` — BCG CLI paired with the existing `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` library module (sourced therein). Kindle chain loads buc + rbfc (FoundryCore — for vessel resolution) + rbrr regime (for `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR`) + rbrv regime + rbfh. No credentials, no GCP machinery.
- Public functions:
  - `rbfh_check <dockerfile-path>` — direct path check; wraps `rbfh_dockerfile_check` after sentinel. No-arg → die with usage (parameter is a free-form path, not a listable set).
  - `rbfh_check_vessel <vessel-sigil-or-path>` — calls `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` (which internally lists-and-dies on missing/invalid arg at `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:166-195`), sources resolved vessel's `rbrv.env`, asserts conjure-mode (refuses bind/graft with operator-actionable message), runs check on `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`.
- Tabtargets (both param1 channel; lowercase `h` paralleling `rbw-fpf`/`rbw-fpc`):
  - `tt/rbw-fhc.HygieneCheck.sh` → `rbfh_check`
  - `tt/rbw-fhv.HygieneCheckVessel.sh` → `rbfh_check_vessel`
- Zipper entries in `rbz_zipper.sh`: `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK` and `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL`.
- **New theurge helper `rbtdrf_run_tt_neg`** in `rbtdrf_fast.rs` (~20 lines): mirrors `rbtdrf_run_tt` shape but inverts success-direction — exit 0 → `Err("expected failure, got success")`, exit non-zero → `Ok(())`. Invocation-error (tabtarget not found, launcher failure) still propagates as `Err`. Used only by the 3 negative synthetic cases. Sibling helper preferred over generalizing `rbtdrf_run_tt` with `expect_ok: bool` (which would touch 7 existing call sites).

## Fixture

- New theurge fixture `dockerfile-hygiene` defined in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`.
- **Suite membership** (Option A from mount-time gauntlet-coverage analysis):
  - Add `"dockerfile-hygiene"` to `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` at `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh:48`. Reformat `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` to multiline style matching `ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET` (one fixture per line). The `"${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}"` spread in `ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE`/`_CRUCIBLE`/`_COMPLETE` propagates transitively — fixture flows into all three for free.
  - Add `"dockerfile-hygiene"` explicitly to `ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET` between `"regime-smoke"` and `"hallmark-lifecycle"` (post-§3 slot; the all-vessels case needs `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}` populated). Gauntlet hand-enumerates fast members — load-bearing addition for pristine-tier regression coverage.
- **Synthetic cases (8)**: Dockerfile bodies as inline Rust `&'static str` constants. Each case writes the body to a temp file in the case's `dir`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhc <tempfile>` via either `rbtdrf_run_tt` (positive) or `rbtdrf_run_tt_neg` (negative), maps the helper's `Result` to `rbtdre_Verdict`. Coverage:
  - `dh_accept_parameterized` (positive — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`)
  - `dh_accept_scratch` (positive — `FROM scratch`)
  - `dh_accept_multistage_as` (positive — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} AS builder` coda admissible)
  - `dh_accept_empty` (positive — empty file passes silently)
  - `dh_accept_comments_only` (positive — column-0 `#` lines only)
  - `dh_reject_hardcoded_literal` (negative — `FROM python:3.12-slim`)
  - `dh_reject_tab_in_from` (negative — tab anywhere on FROM line)
  - `dh_reject_trailing_backslash` (negative — line ends with `\`)
- **All-vessels case `dh_all_vessels_pass`** (Rust-led, no bash format-string loop): Rust loop over `read_dir(RBRR_VESSEL_DIR)`. `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR` resolved once via a small one-shot bash that sources rbrr.env + echoes the value (kindle ceremony is unavoidable for derived-path resolution). For each vessel directory: one-shot bash sources `<vessel>/rbrv.env` and echoes `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`; if `conjure`, invoke `tt/rbw-fhv <sigil>` via `rbtdrf_run_tt`. Maintain a Rust counter; end-of-case assertion fails verdict if zero conjure vessels iterated — closes the silent-zero-iteration trap inherited from `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels`. Theurge knows colophons and sigils; not source paths or kindle chains.

## Touches

- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: extend the existing RBFH entry with a parenthetical mention of the CLI partner (`rbfh_cli.sh`). One acronym per module-family — mirrors RBOB pattern (`rbob_bottle.sh` listed; `rbob_cli.sh` not separately).
- Regenerate `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` so the two new tabtargets appear in the Foundry table.
- No spec edits required: `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` describes the rule, not the test surface. `RBSAK`/`RBSAC` references to `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` already landed in `₢BBABI`.

## Verification

- `bash -n` clean on `rbfh_cli.sh`, `rbte_engine.sh`, and any other touched bash modules.
- `cargo build` clean in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/` (the helper mint + fixture registration are Rust changes).
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (case count grows ~98 → ~107).
- Confirm `dockerfile-hygiene` listed in `ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET` by inspection (running gauntlet end-to-end is cost-prohibitive at notch-time; membership-by-inspection is sufficient).
- Sanity-check: temporarily flip one synthetic case's positive/negative expectation, confirm the fixture reports the case as failed (verdict assertion is wired correctly), then revert.

## Out of scope

- Handbook prose for the new tabtargets — operators discover via `ls tt/` plus context. Chivvy a doc itch if drift becomes noticeable.
- Spec subdoc edits to RBSFH for the test surface — fixture is implementation detail, not contract.
- Decomposing `rbfh_check_vessel`'s no-arg behavior into a separate `rbfh_list_vessels` function — premature factoring; `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` already encapsulates list-and-die.
- CLAUDE.md acronym-table relocation — chivvied as cantled pace `claude-md-acronym-table-explode` (`₢BBABK`).
- Migrating the existing 74 enrollment/regime cases off direct-sourcing — chivvied as `₢BJAAB` (theurge-contract-surface-migration) in ₣BJ.
- Architectural change to make `rbq_qualify_pristine` run fast → gauntlet rather than just gauntlet — out of pace scope; the explicit gauntlet hand-add covers BBABJ's regression-coverage purpose.
- Generalizing `rbtdrf_run_tt` to take `expect_ok: bool` (touching 7 existing call sites) — the sibling-helper approach scope-isolates to the 3 new negative-case callsites.

## Silks

Current proposal `rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture` fits — CLI mints two functions + two tabtargets + zipper + helper; fixture adds 9 cases. Both halves load-bearing. Keep unless implementation reveals one collapsing.

## Character

Mechanical: mint a thin BCG CLI + two tabtargets for the `rbfh` module landed in `₢BBABI`, then add a ~9-case theurge fixture in the fast tier (lifted into the gauntlet suite) for regression coverage of the Dockerfile hygiene contract. Patterns follow `regime-validation` for both reach (path-mode tabtarget per synthetic case, single all-vessels case with bash-side iteration) and shape (inline Rust string constants for synthetic Dockerfile bodies, mirroring `rbtdrf_rv_*` setup strings).

## Mint

- `Tools/rbk/rbfh_cli.sh` — BCG CLI paired with the existing `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` library module (sourced therein). Kindle chain loads buc + rbfc (FoundryCore — needed for vessel resolution) + rbrr regime (for `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR`) + rbrv regime + rbfh. No credentials, no GCP machinery.
- Public functions:
  - `rbfh_check <dockerfile-path>` — direct path check; wraps `rbfh_dockerfile_check` after sentinel. No-arg → die with usage (parameter is a free-form path, not a listable set).
  - `rbfh_check_vessel <vessel-sigil-or-path>` — calls `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` (which internally lists-and-dies on missing/invalid arg at `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:166-195`), sources resolved vessel's `rbrv.env`, asserts conjure-mode (refuses bind/graft with operator-actionable message), runs check on `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`. No separate list-and-die handling required — `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` fully encapsulates that behavior.
- Tabtargets (both param1 channel; lowercase `h` since hygiene is read-only/local, paralleling `rbw-fpf`/`rbw-fpc`):
  - `tt/rbw-fhc.HygieneCheck.sh` → `rbfh_check`
  - `tt/rbw-fhv.HygieneCheckVessel.sh` → `rbfh_check_vessel`
- Zipper entries in `rbz_zipper.sh`: `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK` and `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL`.

## Fixture

- New theurge fixture `dockerfile-hygiene` defined in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`.
- **Suite membership** — Option A from mount-time gauntlet-coverage analysis:
  - Add `"dockerfile-hygiene"` to `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` at `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh:48`. Reformat `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` to multiline style matching `ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET` (one fixture per line). The `"${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}"` spread used by `ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE`/`_CRUCIBLE`/`_COMPLETE` is preserved by bash multiline-array syntax — fixture flows transitively into those three.
  - Add `"dockerfile-hygiene"` explicitly to `ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET` between `"regime-smoke"` and `"hallmark-lifecycle"` (post-§3 slot — state-indifferent at the level of synthetic cases, but the all-vessels case needs `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}` populated). Gauntlet hand-enumerates its fast members (per `rbte_engine.sh` comment) — new fast fixtures do NOT propagate automatically; this addition is load-bearing for pristine-tier regression coverage.
- Synthetic cases (~8): Dockerfile bodies as inline Rust `&'static str` constants; each case writes the body to a temp file in the case's `dir`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhc <tempfile>` via `rbtdrf_run_tt`, asserts verdict. Coverage:
  - `dh_accept_parameterized` (positive — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`)
  - `dh_accept_scratch` (positive — `FROM scratch`)
  - `dh_accept_multistage_as` (positive — `AS builder` coda admissible)
  - `dh_accept_empty` (positive — empty file passes silently)
  - `dh_accept_comments_only` (positive — column-0 `#` lines only)
  - `dh_reject_hardcoded_literal` (negative — `FROM python:3.12-slim`)
  - `dh_reject_tab_in_from` (negative — tab anywhere on FROM line)
  - `dh_reject_trailing_backslash` (negative — line ends with `\`)
- One all-vessels case `dh_all_vessels_pass`: bash script iterates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*` (mirroring `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` at `rbtdrf_fast.rs:967`), sources each `rbrv.env` in a subshell to read `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`, skips non-conjure modes, invokes `tt/rbw-fhv <sigil>` per conjure vessel, fails on any non-zero. No hardcoded vessel paths in theurge — discovery flows through the regime's own pointer.

## Touches

- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: extend the existing RBFH entry with a parenthetical mention of the CLI partner (`rbfh_cli.sh`). Convention is one acronym per module-family — mirrors RBOB pattern (`rbob_bottle.sh` listed; `rbob_cli.sh` not separately).
- Regenerate `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` so the two new tabtargets appear in the Foundry table.

  - `rbfh_check_vessel <vessel-sigil-or-path>` — resolves via `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, sources vessel rbrv.env, asserts conjure-mode (refuses bind/graft with operator-actionable message), runs check on `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`. No-arg → list available vessels and die helpfully (match `rbfd_build`'s pattern at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1178`).
- Tabtargets (both param1 channel; lowercase `h` since hygiene is read-only/local, paralleling `rbw-fpf`/`rbw-fpc`):
  - `tt/rbw-fhc.HygieneCheck.sh` → `rbfh_check`
  - `tt/rbw-fhv.HygieneCheckVessel.sh` → `rbfh_check_vessel`
- Zipper entries in `rbz_zipper.sh`: `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK` and `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL`.

## Fixture

- New theurge fixture `dockerfile-hygiene` defined in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, assigned to the fast suite.
- Synthetic cases (~8): Dockerfile bodies as inline Rust `&'static str` constants; each case writes the body to a temp file in the case's `dir`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhc <tempfile>` via `rbtdrf_run_tt`, asserts verdict. Coverage:
  - `dh_accept_parameterized` (positive — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`)
  - `dh_accept_scratch` (positive — `FROM scratch`)
  - `dh_accept_multistage_as` (positive — `AS builder` coda admissible)
  - `dh_accept_empty` (positive — empty file passes silently)
  - `dh_accept_comments_only` (positive — column-0 `#` lines only)
  - `dh_reject_hardcoded_literal` (negative — `FROM python:3.12-slim`)
  - `dh_reject_tab_in_from` (negative — tab anywhere on FROM line)
  - `dh_reject_trailing_backslash` (negative — line ends with `\`)
- One all-vessels case `dh_all_vessels_pass`: bash script iterates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*` (mirroring `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` at `rbtdrf_fast.rs:967`), sources each `rbrv.env` in a subshell to read `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`, skips non-conjure modes, invokes `tt/rbw-fhv <sigil>` per conjure vessel, fails on any non-zero. No hardcoded vessel paths in theurge — discovery flows through the regime's own pointer.

## Touches

- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: extend the RBFH entry to mention the CLI counterpart, or add a sibling `rbfh_cli.sh` line.
- Regenerate `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` so the two new tabtargets appear in the Foundry table.
- No spec edits required: `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` describes the rule itself, not the test surface. `RBSAK`/`RBSAC` references to `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` already landed in `₢BBABI`.

## Verification

- `bash -n` clean on `rbfh_cli.sh` and any touched modules.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (case count grows ~98 → ~107).
- Sanity-check: temporarily flip one synthetic case's `expect_ok` flag, confirm the fixture reports the case as failed (verdict assertion is wired correctly), then revert.

## Out of scope

- Handbook prose for the new tabtargets — operators discover via `ls tt/` plus context. Chivvy a doc itch if drift becomes noticeable.
- Spec subdoc edits to RBSFH for the test surface — fixture is implementation detail, not contract.
- Decomposing `rbfh_check_vessel`'s no-arg list-and-die into a separate `rbfh_list_vessels` function — premature factoring.

## Silks

Current proposal `rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture` fits if `cli` and `fixture` both feel load-bearing at mount time. If just one (probably the fixture) ends up doing most of the work, consider relabeling.

### claude-md-acronym-table-explode (₢BBABK) [complete]

**[260514-1330] complete**

## Character

Mechanical: relocate per-module acronym mappings from project-root `CLAUDE.md` into the existing per-kit `*-claude-context.md` files. Each kit already has a context file `@`-included from CLAUDE.md, so payload is token-neutral — content moves, not duplicates. Blast radius for future per-module acronym mints collapses to domain-local.

## Move

Per-module acronym tables migrate to per-kit context files:

- **RBK** acronyms (~140 lines under `### Tools Directory` / `#### RBK Subdirectory` in CLAUDE.md) → fold into `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` as a new top section, OR mint `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-acronyms.md` if the tabtarget file feels overloaded (mount-time call).
- **BUK** acronyms → `Tools/buk/buk-claude-context.md` (which already has "BUK Concepts").
- **JJK** acronyms → `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/jjk-claude-context.md` (already has "Job Jockey Configuration").
- **CMK** acronyms → `Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/cmk-claude-context.md` (already partially holds CMK file acronyms; consolidate, remove duplicates from CLAUDE.md).
- **GAD** acronyms → CLAUDE.md currently redirects to `Tools/gad/CLAUDE.md`; verify target completeness, remove redirect stub from project-root.
- **VOK** acronyms (RCG, VLS, VOS0) → fold into VOK context file (verify path at mount-time — context above mentions VOK but not its claude-context location).
- **CNMP Lenses** acronyms → fold into the appropriate context file in `../cnmp_CellNodeMessagePrototype/`, or leave if cross-repo coordination required (mount-time call — likely defer).

## Keep in CLAUDE.md

- Directory Permissions
- Project Prefix Registry (top-level prefix→project map)
- Prefix Naming Discipline, Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline, Minting Workflow, Kit Infrastructure Suffixes
- Heredoc Delimiter Selection
- AsciiDoc Linked Terms guidance
- Rust Build Discipline, Test Execution
- Working Preferences
- Common Workflows
- Design Principles (Load-Bearing Complexity)
- `@`-includes to kit context files (unchanged)

The split is: **rules and top-level prefix→project map stay in CLAUDE.md; per-module file listings live with the kit they describe**.

## Verification

- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (handbook-render re-renders full handbook through includes — surfaces broken cross-references).
- Spot-check three acronym lookups (`RBFH`, `BUC`, `MCM`) — each finds its entry in exactly one place across the loaded context.
- Project Prefix Registry in CLAUDE.md still enumerates every top-level prefix (`rb`, `bu`, `jj`, `mcm`/`axl`, `gad`, etc.).
- CLAUDE.md line-count drops materially (current acronym table dominates the file).

## Out of scope

- Adding new acronyms not currently in CLAUDE.md.
- Restructuring kit context files beyond accommodating the moved tables.
- Touching the GAD/CMK redirect entries if those already work end-to-end (verify-don't-fix).
- Cross-repo CNMP coordination if it requires touching a sibling repository.

## Silks

`claude-md-acronym-table-explode` — "explode" chosen over "split"/"decompose" because the work is fan-out across many kit files, not bisection. If RBK ends up minting its own dedicated acronyms file rather than folding into tabtarget context, the silks still fits.

### sentry-default-route-regression (₢BBABE) [complete]

**[260508-1830] complete**

## Character

Linux verification — mechanical. The fix is committed (ed898867) and empirically proven on mac (full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark k260508105337-691339d7); linux side just needs pull, sentry re-kludge, charge, fixture run to close the wrap gate.

## Wrap criterion

- linux: `git pull`, `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` (rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image — re-kludge required), commit nameplate, `tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh`
- if fixture passes 54/54 on linux, wrap

## Mac empirical findings (carry forward for linux mounter)

1. Bug reproduces on mac Docker Desktop 28.x with byte-identical signature: sentry exits 42, `FATAL: Cannot resolve www.internic.net via 8.8.8.8` because Docker installs the default route via enclave gateway.
2. Compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` is empirically inert on both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x — Docker picks the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway regardless of priority value.
3. Spec gap (not a regression): RBS0 + RBSSS specified IP-based interface discovery to handle the don't-trust-Docker-ordering principle but never extended that principle to default-route ownership. BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced the latent fragility — pre-rename the special compose path for `default` happened to land the gateway correctly by accident.
4. Fix shape A implemented in commit ed898867: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink discovery computes gateway as first usable IP of uplink subnet via POSIX sh arithmetic (verified for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Spec deltas in RBS0 (at_sentry_container narrative) and RBSSS (step 2 retitled, two new sub-bullets) extend the don't-trust-Docker discipline to default-route ownership. Compose YAML cleanup removed the inert `priority:` keys and the misleading topology header.
5. Mac verification: full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark `k260508105337-691339d7` — observed `default via 172.19.0.1 dev eth1` (uplink) inside the running sentry; eth0 = enclave (10.242.0.2/24), eth1 = uplink (172.19.0.2/16).

## Out of scope

- Lost-sentry-stderr observability gap — separable; capture as its own pace later.
- DNS resolve-then-freeze brittleness (no retry on transient dig failure) — separable.
- BBABC revert: not needed; BBABC's rename stays, fix lives at sentry-side ownership.

## Cross-pace relationships

- Unblocks BBAAX and BBABA implicitly: both need tadmor charge succeeding. Confirmed on mac; linux closes when fixture passes there.
- BBABC was the suspected regression source; this pace is BBABC's downstream surface. Resolution leaves BBABC in place.

**[260508-1105] rough**

## Character

Linux verification — mechanical. The fix is committed (ed898867) and empirically proven on mac (full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark k260508105337-691339d7); linux side just needs pull, sentry re-kludge, charge, fixture run to close the wrap gate.

## Wrap criterion

- linux: `git pull`, `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` (rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image — re-kludge required), commit nameplate, `tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh`
- if fixture passes 54/54 on linux, wrap

## Mac empirical findings (carry forward for linux mounter)

1. Bug reproduces on mac Docker Desktop 28.x with byte-identical signature: sentry exits 42, `FATAL: Cannot resolve www.internic.net via 8.8.8.8` because Docker installs the default route via enclave gateway.
2. Compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` is empirically inert on both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x — Docker picks the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway regardless of priority value.
3. Spec gap (not a regression): RBS0 + RBSSS specified IP-based interface discovery to handle the don't-trust-Docker-ordering principle but never extended that principle to default-route ownership. BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced the latent fragility — pre-rename the special compose path for `default` happened to land the gateway correctly by accident.
4. Fix shape A implemented in commit ed898867: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink discovery computes gateway as first usable IP of uplink subnet via POSIX sh arithmetic (verified for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Spec deltas in RBS0 (at_sentry_container narrative) and RBSSS (step 2 retitled, two new sub-bullets) extend the don't-trust-Docker discipline to default-route ownership. Compose YAML cleanup removed the inert `priority:` keys and the misleading topology header.
5. Mac verification: full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark `k260508105337-691339d7` — observed `default via 172.19.0.1 dev eth1` (uplink) inside the running sentry; eth0 = enclave (10.242.0.2/24), eth1 = uplink (172.19.0.2/16).

## Out of scope

- Lost-sentry-stderr observability gap — separable; capture as its own pace later.
- DNS resolve-then-freeze brittleness (no retry on transient dig failure) — separable.
- BBABC revert: not needed; BBABC's rename stays, fix lives at sentry-side ownership.

## Cross-pace relationships

- Unblocks BBAAX and BBABA implicitly: both need tadmor charge succeeding. Confirmed on mac; linux closes when fixture passes there.
- BBABC was the suspected regression source; this pace is BBABC's downstream surface. Resolution leaves BBABC in place.

**[260508-1630] rough**

## Character

Diagnose-and-fix structural — design conversation requiring judgment after empirical cross-platform characterization.

Crucible charge fails on linux because the sentry's `dig +short A <domain> @${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` (resolve-then-freeze, `rbjs_sentry.sh` phase RBJp4) cannot reach the configured DNS server from inside its own network namespace, exit 42. Cause is a routing topology mismatch: Docker installs the multi-network sentry's default route via the *enclave* network gateway (alphabetically first attached) rather than the *transit* (uplink) gateway. Sentry's iptables OUTPUT chain ACCEPTs UDP/TCP/53 to `${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` only when egressing on `${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`; packets routed via the enclave interface miss that rule and hit the default-DROP policy.

Empirically confirmed on linux today by attaching a clean alpine to enclave-then-transit (compose's natural attach order) and observing `default via <enclave-gw> dev eth0`. The compose YAML's `priority: 1000` on transit / `priority: 500` on enclave does **not** influence default-route selection in this Docker engine.

Suspected regression source: BBABC's `services.networks.default → transit` rename (commit message: "eliminates compose's implicit default"). Pre-rename, Docker compose treated `default` as the gateway-bearing network specially. Post-rename, default-route assignment falls to a non-priority-aware heuristic.

## Wrap criterion

- mac behavior characterized empirically (does the same default-route bug manifest, or does mac Docker honor `priority` differently?)
- fix selected based on cross-platform evidence
- fix lands; tadmor charge succeeds end-to-end on linux
- (mac re-verification post-fix encouraged but operator-driven, not a hard wrap gate)

## Linux conclusions to carry forward

1. **Failure surface**: `rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh` → sentry container exit 42 inside ~16s. Sentry stderr (captured before any quench): `FATAL: Cannot resolve www.internic.net via 8.8.8.8`. Reproduces deterministically (identical log checksum across attempts).
2. **Why dig fails**: `dig` packet egresses on enclave interface (default route), not uplink. Sentry's OUTPUT-chain ACCEPT rule for UDP/53 to `${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` is `-o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}` qualified, doesn't match.
3. **Why default route is wrong**: compose attaches networks alphabetically (enclave before transit); Docker installs default route via first-attached network's gateway. `priority: 1000` is ignored on this engine.
4. **The host network itself is healthy**: clean alpine on `canrbhl-tadmor_transit` resolves 8.8.8.8 fine; host iptables NAT rules are correctly installed for both `172.18.0.0/16` (transit) and the enclave subnet.

## Mac questions to answer at mount time

1. Does mac Docker honor `services.networks.<name>.priority` for default-route selection? (If yes, mac may not exhibit this bug.)
2. With the same alphabetical attach order, what default route does mac install for a multi-network container?
3. Has BBABC been exercised under crucible charge on mac since landing? If yes, evidence one way or another already exists in mac-side logs.
4. If mac is healthy and linux is broken, is the bug a genuine cross-version Docker behavior gap, or is one platform's behavior the recently-introduced regression?

## Candidate fix shapes (kept open — pick after cross-platform evidence)

- **A. Sentry-side route management**: in `rbjs_sentry.sh` after `RBJ_UPLINK_IF` discovery, explicitly `ip route replace default via <uplink-gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}` (gateway derived from connected route on uplink). Sentry takes ownership of its own routing; survives Docker engine drift across versions and platforms.
- **B. Revert BBABC's `default → transit` rename**: smallest delta; restores compose's special handling of `default`. Undoes BBABC's deliberate "eliminate implicit default" cleanup and ripples into RBSCC / RBSSS spec quoins that BBABC also touched.
- **C. Compose-extended directives**: investigate whether any Docker-version-portable compose field forces default-route ownership onto a specific network. May not exist; even if it does, `priority` already failed under empirical test.

A and C survive future engine changes; B is fragile if Docker ever changes `default`-network handling. Choice depends on what mac evidence reveals about whether `priority` itself has fix-grade reliability.

## Out of scope

- Re-running the full gauntlet here. BBABA already exists for that and remains the structural release-qualification gate.
- Lost-sentry-stderr observability gap (worth its own pace later — fixture / charge should capture sentry container logs to `../logs-buk/` before quench so future failures aren't unobservable).
- DNS resolve-then-freeze brittleness (no retry on transient `dig` failure) — separable concern; surfaces if dig ever times out for non-routing reasons.

## Cross-pace relationships

- Blocks BBAAX and BBABA implicitly: both depend on tadmor charge succeeding. No cross-heat reference; in-heat ordering carries the dependency.
- BBABC is the suspected regression source; this pace is BBABC's first downstream surface.

### charge-invariant-network-scan (₢BBABC) [complete]

**[260506-1313] complete**

## Character

Tightens charge invariant; aligns docker compose YAML with established
quoin vocabulary; fixes pre-existing spec drift in network-name quoins.

## Wrap criterion

Charge refuses to start if any docker network on the host matches
`*{nameplate}_{enclave,transit}` regardless of compose project name.
Refusal message names each offending network plus exact cleanup
commands. RBS0 network-name quoins reflect current compose project
naming (post-ZRBOB_PROJECT fix). YAML key `default` renamed to
`transit` in rbob_compose.yml.

## Scope

- `.rbk/rbob_compose.yml`: rename `default:` to `transit:` (sentry
  service networks list + comment on line 15)
- `RBS0`: update `at_transit_network_name` and `at_enclave_network_name`
  definitions to reflect `${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}_*` naming;
  update body to use `transit` for the YAML key
- `RBSNC`, `RBSNX`: replace remaining `default` references with
  `{at_transit_network}` quoin links
- `RBSCC` step 3: widen scan — enumerate networks by labels
  `com.docker.compose.network ∈ {enclave, transit}` AND
  `com.docker.compose.project` matches `^(.*-)?{nameplate}$`,
  refuse on any match independent of current run's project name
- `rbob_bottle.sh:329`: implement broader scan; refusal diagnostic
  enumerates each offending network and the remove command(s)

## Locked constraints

- Invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system
- Detection keyed on nameplate, not RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX
- Behavior change applies to all charges, not just pristine tier
- No new vocabulary minted — uses existing `at_transit_network` quoin

## Open at mount time

- Whether to keep current step 2 (project-scoped compose-down) as
  fast-path before the broader scan, or drop in favor of the scan
- Defensive label match (require sentry/pentacle/bottle service
  presence) to eliminate false-positive risk on `*-{nameplate}`
  project name collisions, or accept the negligible risk

## References

- RBSCC step 3 — current behavior to widen
- RBS0:2453,2561,2567 — existing quoin definitions to align
- `.rbk/rbob_compose.yml:15,27` — YAML rename surface
- `rbob_bottle.sh:329` — current refusal site
- Surfaced by ₣BB pristine run on this host: pre-prefix-era residue
  (project=tadmor, label-only) blocked tadmor charge after MZ

### burs-tincture-station-namespacing (₢BBABB) [complete]

**[260506-1344] complete**

## Character

Add BURS_TINCTURE per-station prefix to the BURS schema. Test fixtures compose it into every cloud-side name they write so parallel rbw-tP runs from different stations land in disjoint GCP projects on a shared payor manor.

## Wrap criterion

Test-side dual-station dry-run (two distinct BURS_TINCTURE values) shows disjoint depot project IDs, GAR repos, and SA emails before any GCP call would race. Actual parallel mac+linux validation is BBABA's job.

## Scope

- Enroll BURS_TINCTURE in burs_regime.sh alongside BURS_USER and BURS_LOG_DIR
- Compose into test-fixture writes: pristine throwaway cloud/runtime prefixes, canonical family stem, any RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX/RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX value the fixtures install into rbrr.env
- Production code unchanged — it reads rbrr.env and uses whatever the test wrote
- Update the joint-length validator (rbrr_regime.sh GCP project-ID budget) to account for the new term

## Out of scope

- Production-side BURS_TINCTURE consumers — test-only by intent
- Director/Retriever identity prefixing — redundant once depots are disjoint
- BBABA's actual parallel mac+linux execution — separate pace
- Reslating BBABA's docket — its parallel-runs claim becomes accurate once this lands

## Constraints

- Test-only scope is load-bearing; production behavior unchanged
- Default semantics for unset BURS_TINCTURE (empty vs required) decided at mount time

**[260505-1526] rough**

## Character

Add BURS_TINCTURE per-station prefix to the BURS schema. Test fixtures compose it into every cloud-side name they write so parallel rbw-tP runs from different stations land in disjoint GCP projects on a shared payor manor.

## Wrap criterion

Test-side dual-station dry-run (two distinct BURS_TINCTURE values) shows disjoint depot project IDs, GAR repos, and SA emails before any GCP call would race. Actual parallel mac+linux validation is BBABA's job.

## Scope

- Enroll BURS_TINCTURE in burs_regime.sh alongside BURS_USER and BURS_LOG_DIR
- Compose into test-fixture writes: pristine throwaway cloud/runtime prefixes, canonical family stem, any RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX/RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX value the fixtures install into rbrr.env
- Production code unchanged — it reads rbrr.env and uses whatever the test wrote
- Update the joint-length validator (rbrr_regime.sh GCP project-ID budget) to account for the new term

## Out of scope

- Production-side BURS_TINCTURE consumers — test-only by intent
- Director/Retriever identity prefixing — redundant once depots are disjoint
- BBABA's actual parallel mac+linux execution — separate pace
- Reslating BBABA's docket — its parallel-runs claim becomes accurate once this lands

## Constraints

- Test-only scope is load-bearing; production behavior unchanged
- Default semantics for unset BURS_TINCTURE (empty vs required) decided at mount time

### base64-to-openssl-sweep (₢BBAA_) [complete]

**[260505-1139] complete**

## Character

Mechanical sweep — convert any raw `base64` invocation in BUK/RBK shell to
the openssl-standardized form established by commit 385589f4. The
jurisdiction garrison ceremony reintroduced raw calls; potentially others
have leaked since.

## Scope

- Rescan `Tools/buk` and `Tools/rbk` for raw `base64` invocations — do
  not trust prior line numbers, files and call sites may have moved.
  Recipe: `grep -rn '\bbase64\b' Tools/{buk,rbk} --include='*.sh'`,
  filter out `openssl ... base64`, jq's `@base64`, and comment-only
  occurrences.
- Replace encode form: `base64 < FILE` → `openssl enc -base64 -A < FILE`
- Replace decode form: `base64 -d` → `openssl enc -base64 -d -A`
- Decode sites inside SSH heredocs run on the remote shell — same
  substitution applies; remote toolchain is presumed sufficient
  (cygwin/wsl/linux/mac all have openssl available).

## Constraints

- Acceptance for the jurisdiction sites is the existing live garrison
  ceremony — `buw-jpG{b,c,w}` step 6 `ssh ... true` already round-trips
  the planted privkey byte-exact; corrupted decode fails SSH auth.
- No new test fixture needed.
- `-A` flag suppresses line-wrapping. Use it uniformly on encode and
  decode so single-line embedding in heredocs stays valid.
- bujb_jurisdiction.sh encode site has a BCG temp-file capture wrapper
  (`> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT 2> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR || buc_die`) —
  preserve it; sweep swaps tool name only.
- With `-A`, encoded output has no embedded newlines; sweep may drop
  any post-capture newline-strip (`${var//$'\n'/}`) that was defensive
  against raw `base64`'s 76-col wrapping.

## Out of scope

- `Tools/lmci/bundle.sh` — different kit, predates the sweep
- jq `@base64` filter — internal to jq, not a bash dependency

### pristine-folds-unmake-refusals (₢BBAA-) [complete]

**[260505-1049] complete**

## Character

Three deft fixture additions following BBAA9's contract change, plus a probable mount-time fix to pristine-lifecycle's existing unmake step (suspected broken by BBAA9 — invokes `rbw-dU` no-arg, which now hits empty-arg refusal). Then operator runs the live-GCP exercise drafted in BBAA9's wrap conversation to clean leftover canonicals and confirm contract behavior in the wild.

## Scope

- Pristine-lifecycle existing unmake step likely needs project_id passed as $1 (was relying on regime-derived target before BBAA9). Verify first at mount-time. Either anticipatory-fix-with-clue-annotations OR just-add-clue-comments are both acceptable per operator — clues cite BBAA9 as the contract change to consult.

- Empty-arg refusal: new case in fast-suite (no GCP, pure shell). Invoke `rbw-dU` no-arg, require non-zero exit + `rbw-dl` pointer in stderr. Agent picks home fixture at mount-time based on the existing taxonomy.

- Live-disqualify: new StateProgressing case in pristine-lifecycle, slotted between the existing depot-creation step and the existing unmake step. Invoke `rbw-dU $RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` (regime-derived live target), require non-zero exit + recovery diagnostic naming `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` rename or `rbw-MZ`, assert no GCP state mutation occurred. Precondition probe asserts depot exists + rbrr.env aligned per `rbtdrb_Probe` shape.

- After fixture additions land green: operator runs the live-GCP fresh-chat exercise — inventory via `rbw-dl`, smoke empty-arg + live-disqualify against the live regime, then unmake leftover canonicals one by one watching the run-through. Any operator-noticed issues fold back into the pace.

## Constraints

- **No new depot create/destroy steps.** Both GCP-touching additions amortize against pristine-lifecycle's existing depot window. Depot creation is expensive and soft-delete retention is rate-limited.

- DisplayName discriminator coverage stays uncovered — no clean fixture path for a non-depot GCP project under the Payor account. Acknowledged out of scope.

- Clues left in code can stay through the manual exercise to aid that debugging session; cleaned in this pace's wrap OR deferred to a later sweep — operator call at wrap time.

## Out of scope

- DisplayName discriminator fixture coverage (future pace if/when the gap matters).
- Bulk-unmake-leftovers tool (carried over from BBAA9 out-of-scope; manual exercise may tempt it but resist).
- Refactor of pristine-lifecycle structure beyond the deft additions and the suspected-broken-existing-unmake fix.

### unmake-takes-project-id (₢BBAA9) [complete]

**[260505-1156] complete**

## Character

`rbgp_depot_unmake` reads `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` (derived from
`RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` + `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` + literal `"d-"`) instead of
accepting the target as a parameter. RBSDU spec already declares "depot
project ID provided as first argument" — implementation diverged.
Surfaced as spook today: cleaning up leftover canonicals from failed
gauntlet attempts requires editing `rbrr.env` to retarget, polluting
regime state for a transient operation.

Compounding bug: nothing prevents unmaking the live RBRR-selected depot.

Decoupling unmake from RBRR exposes a new bug surface — operator can
route the verb at any GCP project Payor has access to. The structural
defense (post-BBAAM): displayName anchor `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX`,
already used by list as the depot-vs-non-depot discriminator. Unmake
inherits the same consumption-side check.

Adjacent BCG cleanup folds in: the `"d-"` literal in
`rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh` is not lifted to a tinder constant.

## Scope

- `rbgp_depot_unmake()` accepts depot project ID as `$1`; all internal
  references switch from `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` to the parameter
- Empty-arg behavior: print buc_tabtarget pointer to `rbw-dl` for
  candidate discovery, then `buc_die` non-zero. No auto-routing to
  list. Single responsibility preserved.
- New refusal step (early, before authenticate): fail-fast if target
  equals `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`; diagnostic names recovery path
  (rename moniker or `rbw-MZ` before unmake)
- Validate-Target-Depot gains displayName check: response's
  `.displayName` must start with `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX`. Refuse
  otherwise — diagnostic surfaces the mismatch and points operator at
  `rbw-dl` for the candidate list. Symmetric with how list discriminates.
- Zipper enrollment for `RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT`: channel `""` → `"param1"`
- Tabtarget stays single-file (no imprint variant); param flows through
- RBSDU spec adds: live-disqualify step (cheap, pre-authenticate),
  empty-arg refusal language, displayName discriminator sub-bullet
  inside Validate-Target-Depot. Existing argument-from-first-position
  language already correct.
- Lift `"d-"` to `RBGC_depot_project_infix` tinder constant in
  `rbgc_Constants.sh` (top-of-module, lower_snake name per BCG)
- `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh` rewrites the `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`
  derivation to reference the tinder constant

## Constraints

- BCG tinder discipline: `RBGC_depot_project_infix` is pure string
  literal, lowercase name part, top-of-module placement immediately
  after sourced-guard, no runtime dependency
- Spec voice vs implementation voice: RBSDU edits reference
  `{rbrr_*}` / `{rbgc_*}` linked terms (e.g., live-depot derivation
  spelled in terms of RBRR fields + the new infix linked term);
  implementation function compares against the named
  `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` constant directly. Single source of truth in
  each voice.
- Refusal ordering, cheapest first:
  1. Empty-arg refusal (zero traffic)
  2. Live-disqualify (zero traffic, local string compare)
  3. Authenticate
  4. projects.get + state=ACTIVE + displayName discriminator
- Param shape: full project ID (e.g., `canc-d-canest2100000`), not
  moniker — matches what `rbw-dl` displays and what GCP error messages
  surface
- `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX` consumed as-is (currently kindle-cased
  SCREAMING despite being pure literal — pre-existing BCG drift, NOT
  in scope to relift this pace)

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAX (rbw-tP-first-run) does not block on this — today's manual
  rbrr.env-edit dance is acceptable for one cleanup pass — but the
  gauntlet will produce more leftovers, so this lands first

## Out of scope

- Formation helper function (e.g., `rbgp_depot_project_id_for_moniker`)
  — speculative until a second call site needs the rule; current scope
  has only `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` as the consumer
- Lifting `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX` from kindle to tinder — adjacent
  BCG cleanup, separate pace if/when
- Symmetric refactor of other depot operations (levy still kindles
  from RBRR, which is correct since levy IS the act of selecting/
  creating the live depot)
- Bulk-unmake-leftovers tool — separate pace if the need recurs

### yoke-domain-shift-all-vessels (₢BBAA6) [complete]

**[260503-1212] complete**

## Character

Discrete contract reshape — yoke domain shifts vessel→reliquary. Mechanically intricate but design is locked. Bundled orphan vessel cleanup closes the `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` test thread carrying-forward since BBAA3.

## Locked decisions

- `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); operation iterates every vessel under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/` with rbrv.env, writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
- Primitive does **not** git commit — leaves changed files in repo dir
- Orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) **must** commit at end-of-case
- Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
- BCG-compliant unimpeachable error handling — pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; vessel-write failures surface clear diagnostic identifying which vessel failed and which were already written
- Orphan vessel cleanup (bundled): delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`

## Done criteria

- `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26
- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
- Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
- All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent

## Discovery recipes

- Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
- Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
- Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
- Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
- Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)

## Out of scope

- Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
- Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved

- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
- Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
- All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent

## Discovery recipes

- Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
- Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
- Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
- Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
- Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)

## Out of scope

- Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
- Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved

### reliquary-universal-required (₢BBAA4) [complete]

**[260501-0812] complete**

## Character
Multi-layer alignment — implementation, orchestration, spec, and operator-facing handbook prose all express the same fact.

## Locked decision
RBRV_RELIQUARY is required (min=1, non-empty) for every ordain-path vessel: conjure, bind, AND graft. The about/vouch pipeline is mode-uniform and consumes reliquary tool images in every mode (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft); bind also uses reliquary skopeo for image-copy. The current spec/yoke/orchestration/handbook claim that "bind/graft don't use reliquary" contradicts the implementation — three layers (`rbfd_mirror`, `rbfd_graft`, about/vouch step assembly) require it; yoke, the spec, and the bind/graft handbook tracks neither set nor document it.

## Surface
- yoke mode-gate (`rbfl_yoke`): accept all ordain-path modes
- orchestration yoke fan-out (`rbtdro_onboarding.rs` `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS`): include plantuml AND graft-demo
- spec correction in RBSDY: strike "Gate on Vessel Mode" step; broaden NOTE
- spec sweep in RBS0: every site that names conjure as the reliquary-using mode needs broadening — not only the yoke linked-term description. Discovery recipe: `grep -ni "conjure" Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -iE "reliquary|build step|tool"`. Expect at least the gcloud-tool NOTE (says reliquary tools exist for conjure shell convenience — wrong; about+vouch use them too) and the GCB-step-contexts enumeration `(conjure, bind, enshrine)` missing graft.
- preflight gate (`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`): remove empty-RBRV_RELIQUARY tolerance
- graft preflight: ensure `zrbfd_registry_preflight` runs before submission
- vessel kindle schema for RBRV_RELIQUARY: min=1
- procedural specs (RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAG): declare universal reliquary precondition
- handbook tracks `rbhodb_director_bind.sh` and `rbhodg_director_graft.sh`: add inscribe-then-yoke ceremony calling `rbw-dY`, mirroring the existing pattern in `rbhodf` (conjure) and `rbhoda` (airgap). The yoke tabtarget already exists and is the right tool — no new tabtarget.

## Watch for
Validator min=1 against post-`rbw-MZ` state — every vessel has empty RBRV_RELIQUARY by design until inscribe+yoke run. If `rbw-rvv` is exercised against marshal-zero state in any test tier, surface as adjacent fix.

## Cross-pace dependencies
- BBAAu landed.

## Wrap criterion
- `ordain-bind` and `ordain-graft` clear empirically against canonical-established depot.
- `rbw-rvv` enforces RBRV_RELIQUARY min=1 on every ordain-path vessel.
- Spec, orchestration, implementation, AND handbook prose tell one story — no remaining "bind/graft don't use reliquary" claims anywhere.

## Out of scope
- In-pool Cloud-Build-time reliquary preflight — separate pace (BBAA3).
- BBAAs empirical run — downstream pace, mounts after this clears.

### cloud-build-reliquary-preflight (₢BBAA3) [complete]

**[260501-0933] complete**

## Character
Defense-in-depth — every Cloud Build validates reliquary GAR-presence from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.

## Motivation
The host-side preflight (`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`) verifies the Director's host can reach GAR. It does NOT verify what actually matters at build time: the worker pool's view of GAR. Three real divergence sources — airgap pool private VPC routing, IAM split between Director (host) and Mason (build) SAs, and time skew between submission and execution.

## Surface
- New step script (rbgjr* or analogous) — gcloud/alpine HEAD-checks against reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors
- Step-assembly prepend: conjure (`rbfd_build` stitch), bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`), graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`), enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`)
- Specs: RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE — declare the in-pool preflight step
- RBS0:800 (cloud-build-step contexts paragraph) — extend to mention in-pool preflight contract

## Chicken-and-egg note
The preflight step's own image is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Preflight covers the other five tool images plus enshrinements. Document the boundary explicitly in spec.

## Cross-pace dependencies
- Reliquary universalization (this heat's prior pace) lands first.

## Wrap criterion
- Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path Cloud Build job.
- Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) produces a clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message.
- Green case overhead acceptable (~5s).

## Out of scope
- Host-side preflight tightening — covered by prior pace.
- BBAAs empirical run.

### rbrv-anchor-locator-format (₢BBAAz) [complete]

**[260429-1600] complete**

## Character

Spec-driven mechanical refactor. ANCHOR's value shape changes from
content-addressed identifier to domain-blind locator. No new
capabilities; existing flows preserved.

## Goals

Redefine `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` as a `package-path:tag` locator
matching the wrest/jettison domain-blind locator pattern in RBS0.
Today ANCHOR is `<sanitized-origin>-<10-hex>` implicitly under
`enshrines/`; new shape carries its namespace explicitly so it can
name an enshrinement OR (in ₢BBAA0) a hallmark.

## Wrap criterion

- Spec sweep: RBS0 RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR definition, RBSAE enshrine,
  RBSAC conjure preflight + resolve, plus any consumers using the
  short-form anchor.
- Code consumers updated: enshrine writeback, conjure resolution,
  validation patterns.
- Existing populated rbrv.env values migrated to locator shape.
- Fast suite green; previously-green onboarding-sequence cases
  still pass.

## Out of scope

- Hallmark-base support — ₢BBAA0.
- Airgap chain integration — ₢BBAAs verifies.
