### pristine-walkthrough-manual (₢BBAAc) [complete]

**[260428-1218] complete**

## Character

Operator-driven manual walkthrough from a marshal-zeroed state, positioned between canonical-establish (built) and canonical-onboarding-sequence (yet to be built). Two purposes under the gauntlet design:

1. Validate canonical-establish in actually-fresh marshal-zero state — its precondition probes were designed defensively, but only running against a real fresh state surfaces silent first-build assumptions in the underlying tabtargets.
2. Inform canonical-onboarding-sequence case design — walking §3 manually surfaces the case shape, probe specifications, and operator-facing diagnostic ceremony for the next fixture's cases.

Cognitive posture: real-time conversation, surprise-by-surprise. Operator drives commands; agent watches output, reads code on demand, captures dispositions.

## Wrap criterion

§2 (canonical-establish run as a fixture against marshal-zero), §3 (reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain, walked manually), and §4 (crucible suite per RELEASE.md) each completed end-to-end against a fresh marshal-zero. Every surprise captured + dispositioned (inline fix or surfaced as follow-up pace before canonical-onboarding-sequence mounts). Fixed-or-surfaced is sufficient — green tally is BBAAX's guardrail, not this pace's.

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt. Same magnitude as BBAAX, distinct purpose: this pace's bug surface is decomposed step-by-step (manual operator drive) and informs probe design before the next fixture exists; BBAAX validates the fully-stitched suite end-to-end.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Per BBAAN posture: invest is fatal-on-exists; divest deletes production RBRA; unmake absorbs governor cleanup. Any retry path goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery.
- Reuse existing tabtargets exclusively; canonical-establish runs as a fixture; §3 and §4 walked manually.
- No code changes to canonical-establish during this pace (it's already built). Surprises in canonical-establish surface as follow-up paces.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (the §2 fixture exists for run-as-fixture step).

## Out of scope

- §1 (pristine-lifecycle exercises it; not relevant here).
- Canonical-onboarding-sequence implementation (subsequent pace, informed by this walkthrough's findings).
- Suite assembly (subsequent pace).
- Green-tally end-to-end run (BBAAX).
- Fixing every surprise inline — large or design-call surprises surface as follow-up paces.

### rbra-install-advice-locality-sweep (₢BBAAo) [complete]

**[260428-1054] complete**

## Character

Three RBRA install paths emit operator advice: Governor (rbgp_governor_mantle) and Director/Retriever (rbgg_invest_*). Governor still writes to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR with per-mantle timestamp — out of step with the singleton-assay-slot discipline D/R honor and RBS0:806-807 documents. Independently: install advice across all three uses cp where the assay slot's volatile-singleton semantics call for mv; and D/R advice prescribes local install where the operator running invest is typically not the credential consumer (mint-on-behalf-of-identity flow).

Surfaced live by `rbw-aM` transcript review post-BBAAn wrap.

## Wrap criterion

- Governor mantle writes to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE (single-slot, no per-mantle timestamp). No RBRA bytes appear in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR across the mantle path. BBAAn's JWT-bearer-probe call site operates on the new target.
- All three advice blocks use `mv`, not `cp`.
- Governor advice prescribes destination (RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE); the "or, for assay (test) mode" branch deleted.
- Director/Retriever advice frames as operator-discretion ("move to a safe place"), with the local role slot shown as the canonical destination only when invester == consumer.
- RBS0:806-807 updated: Governor added to the singleton-assay-writer enumeration; verb corrected (copy → move); "safe place" framing reflected.
- canonical-establish fixture's assay-probe finds the post-Governor-mantle credential at the assay slot (locality fix validated against a real consumer).

## Locked decisions

- `mv` not `cp` — assay is volatile-singleton-staging; install means relocate, not duplicate.
- Governor: prescribed destination (operator self-mints, self-uses).
- Director/Retriever: operator-discretion framing (mint-on-behalf flow — invester typically delivers, not consumes).
- RBS0:806-807 update folds into this pace.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAn (sa-key-propagation-probe) landed — JWT-bearer probe call site is in scope to re-point at the new write target.

## Out of scope

- Retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs.
- Spec doc touches beyond RBS0:806-807 (RBSGM/RBSDK/RBSRK install-ceremony detail) — separate concern.
- Documenting delivery-channel patterns (encrypted USB, secure transfer) for the "safe place" advice — runbook concern.

**[260428-1024] rough**

## Character

Governor mantle predates the credential-locality discipline applied to Director/Retriever invest. RBRA bytes are written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (transient/disposable) rather than RBRR_SECRETS_DIR; the "Install the RBRA file" advice in `rbw-aM` output copies from there. Director/Retriever already write directly to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE — operator copies stay within secrets dir per the rbgg_Governor.sh comment block ("credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR"). Surfaced live by `rbw-aM` transcript review post-BBAAn wrap.

## Wrap criterion

Governor RBRA bytes never appear outside RBRR_SECRETS_DIR across the full mantle path. `rbw-aM` "Install the RBRA file" advice copies from secrets/, not output-buk/. The "or, for assay (test) mode" branch disappears once the file already IS in assay.

## Locked decisions

- Match Director/Retriever shape: write to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE (single-slot, no per-mantle timestamp on the canonical drop point); operator copies to RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE.
- Code-only pace.

## Out of scope

- Retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs.
- Spec doc updates documenting the install ceremony — separate concern.

### oauth-token-mint-retry-on-signature-race (₢BBAAp) [complete]

**[260428-1100] complete**

## Character

Tight, mechanical change in rbgo_OAuth.sh: shift the propagation-race tolerance from SA-creation-time probe to consumer-side token-mint retry. Single mechanism, uniform treatment for every RBRA consumer.

## Wrap criterion

- `rbgo_get_token_capture` retries on `invalid_grant` + `"Invalid JWT Signature"` only; other errors fail-fast as today.
- `rbgo_probe_jwt_bearer_propagation` and its two call sites (zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key, rbgp_governor_mantle) deleted.
- The three `RBGC_SA_KEY_PROBE_*` constants either deleted or repurposed for the consumer-retry budget. ZRBGO_PROBE_STDERR_FILE deleted or repurposed.
- 30s upper-bound budget preserved (was probe budget; becomes consumer-retry budget).
- bash -n clean, theurge build clean, theurge unit tests pass.
- Live validation: canonical-establish fixture run end-to-end from fresh marshal-zero passes (depot_levy + governor_mantle + retriever_invest + director_invest), demonstrating the consumer-side retry absorbs the same race BBAAn's probe was bounding.

## Constraints

- Error-class discrimination required: parse OAuth response body via jq for `error == "invalid_grant"` AND `error_description == "Invalid JWT Signature."` Other shapes fail-fast as today. Capture body to disk so probe-style forensic file remains available on timeout.
- Progress logging on each retry attempt — operator visibility during retry window (no silent 30s pause).
- Backoff schedule preserved from BBAAn (initial 2s, 2x growth, 15s cap).

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Absorbs BBAAn's mechanism: BBAAn's probe + constants deleted; same constants may be renamed to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_*` family if reused.
- Unblocks canonical-onboarding-sequence (subsequent pace) which sequences SA creation + consumer mints tightly across fixture cases.
- Surfaced by BBAAc walkthrough (canonical-establish fixture's retriever_invest case failed with Invalid JWT Signature ~zero seconds after governor_mantle's probe succeeded — same race, different consumer).

## Out of scope

- Other retry-worthy OAuth error patterns (e.g., transient 5xx, network reset). Capture as itch — not today; today's scope is the propagation-race signature pattern only.
- Changes to non-token RBRA flows (RBRA file write, RBRA validation, etc.).
- Spec changes — no behavioral shift visible at procedural-spec level.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Absorbs BBAAn's mechanism: BBAAn's probe + constants deleted; same constants may be renamed to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_*` family if reused.
- Unblocks canonical-onboarding-sequence (subsequent pace) which sequences SA creation + consumer mints tightly across fixture cases.
- Surfaced by BBAAc walkthrough (canonical-establish fixture's retriever_invest case failed with Invalid JWT Signature ~zero seconds after governor_mantle's probe succeeded — same race, different consumer).

## Out of scope

- Other retry-worthy OAuth error patterns (e.g., transient 5xx, network reset). Capture as itch — not today; today's scope is the propagation-race signature pattern only.
- Changes to non-token RBRA flows (RBRA file write, RBRA validation, etc.).
- Spec changes — no behavioral shift visible at procedural-spec level.

### gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence (₢BBAAf) [complete]

**[260428-1236] complete**

## Character

State-progressing fixture for §3 of the gauntlet. Cases walk the operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks for vessel construction (e.g., rbw-Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg per vessel mode) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly. Establishes handbook-regression coverage as the gauntlet's secondary purpose: ensures handbook prose stays in sync with working code so future operators following the handbooks aren't surprised by drift.

**Build-only.** Cases stop when the vessel hallmark lands in GAR. No charge, no test. The build/run split keeps the run-side fixtures (per-crucible) reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Service-tier — GCP credentials + Cloud Build capacity. Per-vessel mode coverage flows from each vessel's mode field, naturally aligning with the four director-mode handbook tracks. Drift between fixture and handbook is accepted future maintenance.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning at mount time.

## Scope

- Cover §3 via handbook walks: inscribe-reliquary, plus one ordain-* case per vessel, each walking the corresponding handbook track (rbw-Odf conjure, rbw-Oda airgap, rbw-Odb bind, rbw-Odg graft — case-to-vessel mapping verified at mount per regime declarations).
- Each case invokes the handbook's prescribed tabtargets in the prescribed order; asserts post-conditions named in the handbook; produces the expected hallmark in GAR.
- Each case carries a precondition probe per the gauntlet-canonical-establish pattern.
- Disposition: StateProgressing.
- Manifest registration.

## Wrap criterion

- Each case walks its handbook end-to-end and produces the expected hallmark.
- Case structure mirrors the handbook prose closely enough that a reader can match case lines to handbook lines.
- Per-case precondition probes verified.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (precondition-probe pattern set; depot + SAs in place).
- BBAAc landed (manual walkthrough findings folded back).
- Onboarding director handbook tracks (rbw-Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg) stable enough in heat A6 to lean on (operator confirmed).

## Out of scope

- Engine work (gauntlet-engine-foundation).
- Canonical-establish fixture (prior pace).
- Suite assembly (BBAAg).
- Crucible runs of any kind — preserved by build/run split for run-side reusability.
- Run-side handbook coverage (rbw-Ots, rbw-Ofc rack portion). Out of scope for now.
- Live end-to-end validation (BBAAX).

## Character

Second state-progressing fixture for the gauntlet test suite. Wraps §3 of the release-qualification sequence — reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain. Mimics the operator's online onboarding journey under theurge control while establishing the hallmarks needed by §4 crucible fixtures.

Service-tier — needs GCP credentials and Cloud Build capacity. Per-vessel mode coverage (conjure / bind / graft / airgap) flows from each vessel's mode field; fixture is mode-agnostic in structure. Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

Distinct from the existing `four-mode` fixture: ordain-* cases produce real hallmarks for downstream §4 crucible fixtures; `four-mode` validates Cloud Build mode-mixture against test images. Different scope, different artifacts.

## Scope

- New fixture covering §3 cases: inscribe-reliquary, ordain-tadmor, ordain-moriah, ordain-srjcl, ordain-pluml
- Each case carries a precondition probe — refuses cleanly with operator-actionable diagnostic if expected state absent
- Disposition: StateProgressing
- Manifest registration
- Mode coverage encoded per vessel; fixture exercises whatever mode each vessel's regime declares

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (precondition-probe pattern set; depot + SAs in place)
- BBAAc landed (manual walkthrough informs case design + probe specifications)

## Out of scope

- Engine work (gauntlet-engine-foundation)
- Canonical-establish fixture (prior pace)
- Suite assembly (subsequent pace)
- Live end-to-end validation (BBAAX)

### rbw-tP-build (₢BBAAW) [abandoned]

**[260428-0700] abandoned**

## Character

Construction of the canonical entry-point script `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`. Stitches existing tabtargets per RELEASE.md §3 sequence into one orchestrator. No new cloud-mutating logic — every cloud step is a call to an existing tabtarget. The script's value is sequence, entry-contract enforcement, and exit gating, not novel functionality.

Coding-only pace. **Wrap criterion**: script exists, parses cleanly, each tabtarget invocation has the right shape under cheap dry-checks. The gate-on-green discipline lives in the sibling first-run pace.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

New file: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`. Body comprises three responsibilities.

**Entry contract** (per RELEASE.md L46–53): refuse to run unless ALL hold:
- marshal-zero signature on HEAD
- working tree clean
- RBRA files absent
- RBRR fields blank
- hallmark pins blank in every nameplate regime
- depot-scoped fields blank in every vessel regime

**Sequence body** (per RELEASE.md L63–69):
- pristine-lifecycle fixture (gate + simple + credentialed sections — depends on BBAAK landing)
- canonical infrastructure setup — levy canonical depot (`rbw-dL`), mantle canonical governor (`rbw-aM`), invest canonical retriever (`rbw-arI` per BBAAN), invest canonical director (`rbw-adI` per BBAAN), IAM propagation wait
- supply chain — inscribe reliquary (`rbw-dI`), ordain all hallmarks (`rbw-fO` per vessel)
- crucible suite — charge/run-fixture/quench × {tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml}, sequential, per-fixture quench-after to avoid cross-fixture contamination
- cleanup — abjure all hallmarks (`rbw-fA`), divest operational SAs (`rbw-arD`, `rbw-adD` per BBAAN), unmake depot (`rbw-dU`); governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN's settled posture

**Exit semantics**: green tally on success; operator-actionable diagnostic on failure naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence per RELEASE.md L74.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- Reuse existing tabtargets exclusively — no inline cloud-mutating logic
- Real-time review at each step before commit
- Wrap on script-exists + parses + invocation shapes correct; do **not** wrap on "ran end-to-end" — that is the first-run pace's wrap criterion

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAK landed (credentialed fixture cases available)
- BBAAN landed (cult-verb tabtargets `rbw-arI`, `rbw-adI`, `rbw-arD`, `rbw-adD`, `rbw-arr`, `rbw-adr`)

## Out of scope

- First end-to-end run against fresh cloud state (sibling pace)
- Modifications to existing tabtargets to support orchestration — surface as separate paces if needed
- Phase-skip flags or partial re-run flags (single-shot ceremony per RELEASE.md L10–11)
- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH territory)
- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (BBAAH)

### onboarding-sequence-buildout (₢BBAAu) [complete]

**[260429-0847] complete**

## Character

Pure construction. Build out the `onboarding-sequence` fixture with the cases needed to produce every vessel downstream crucibles consume. No empirical run-against-fresh-state here — that's BBAAs's reslated scope.

## Scope

- Rename sweep: `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `onboarding-sequence` across fixture/section/manifest constants and tabtarget filenames; `onboarding-` prefix on all case names (existing five and new four).
- Four new cases: `enshrine-bases`, `kludge-tadmor`, `kludge-ccyolo`, `conjure-srjcl`.
- Expand `inscribe-reliquary` to yoke the stamp into all seven ordain-side vessels in one pass; remove per-ordain-case yoke prelude.
- Centralize hallmark propagation: each ordain case publishes its hallmark to a static list of consuming nameplates (sentry-tether → moriah/srjcl/pluml; airgap-bottle → moriah; plantuml-bottle → pluml; jupyter-bottle → srjcl; graft-demo → none).
- RCG named-string constants for vessel dirs, nameplates, case names, consumer arrays.
- Auto-commit pattern: kludge cases commit between sentry+bottle (clean-tree precondition); ordain cases commit at end (clean-tree for next ordain).

## Case order

inscribe-reliquary → enshrine-bases → kludge-tadmor → kludge-ccyolo → ordain-conjure → conjure-srjcl → ordain-airgap → ordain-bind → ordain-graft

## Wrap criterion

All nine cases compile; fixture invocable; single-case rerun works for each new case (mechanical confirmation, not green-against-fresh-state). Unit tests pass; no fast-suite regression.

## Out of scope

- Empirical run-until-green against fresh canonical state (BBAAs).
- moriah crucible Fixture static (still phantom; separate concern).
- RBS0 spec sync (BBAAq).

### crucible-fixture-gauntlet-readiness (₢BBAAs) [complete]

**[260503-1022] complete**

## Character

Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.

## Iteration loop

Each turn:

1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
3. Recommend a fix.
4. Await guidance.

No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.

## In-flight progress

Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:

1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.

Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally on onboarding-sequence.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAu landed.
- canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.

## Out of scope

- Construction — BBAAu.
- Full gauntlet — BBAAX.
- moriah Fixture static — phantom.

- Reusability demonstrated against at least one non-gauntlet-built vessel source.

### yoke-string-constant-sweep (₢BBAAv) [complete]

**[260503-1049] complete**

## Character

Mechanical RCG sweep. Replace four lowercase "yoke" string literals in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` with a named-string constant so the operation name lives in one place.

## Wrap criterion

A single `RBTDRO_OPERATION_YOKE` (or similarly named) constant declared once, consumed by every yoke-as-string site. Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`; unit tests pass; fast suite no regression.

## Sites (entry points, will drift — discover at mount time)

`grep -n '"yoke\|"yoke-' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdro_onboarding.rs` — currently four hits at error formats, label prefix, commit message. Re-grep at mount time.

## Naming

Per RCG/CLAUDE.md prefix discipline: `RBTDRO_*` for module-scoped constants. Capitalization in the constant value vs. each call site's casing is the design call to make at mount time — one constant with case adaptation at use sites, or distinct constants for distinct cases.

## Out of scope

- Other Rust files. The four-site cluster is in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` only.
- Other operation strings ("inscribe", "enshrine", "ordain", etc.). Yoke is the canary; if the pattern lands cleanly here, the same sweep can roll forward in a separate pace.
- Comments and doc strings — those are explanatory prose, not magic strings.

## Character

Defines the suite as a composition of fixtures, creates the suite tabtarget, and pivots the rbw-tP workbench tabtarget from the originally-planned orchestrator-script shape to entry-contract enforcer + suite tabtarget exec. No new test cases; this pace is composition + entry point only.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

- Gauntlet suite definition: bash array in the engine holding the ordered fixture sequence per paddock's state ladder: `enrollment-validation` → `pristine-lifecycle` → `canonical-establish` → `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `regime-validation` → `regime-smoke` → `four-mode` → `tadmor` → `moriah` → `srjcl` → `pluml`
- Resolver branch in the suite-name resolver
- New TestSuite tabtarget for the gauntlet suite
- Reshape rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh: enforce entry contract (marshal-zero signature, RBRR blank, RBRA absent, regimes blank) then exec the gauntlet suite tabtarget

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence landed (final new fixture exists)

## Out of scope

- First-run end-to-end validation (BBAAX)
- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH)
- Documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs (BBAAI)

### gauntlet-rerun-infrastructure (₢BBAAr) [abandoned]

**[260503-1229] abandoned**

## Character

Verify the iterative-debug surface of the gauntlet suite, sequenced after BBAAg (suite + rbw-tP exist) and before BBAAX (first end-to-end run, green-tally guardrail). BBAAX commits to marshal-zero-between-attempts — without proven single-case rerun against the suite's expensive state-progressing cases (especially §3 ordain, where vessel construction is the costliest step), each debug iteration costs ~1 hour + a GCP project, and the gauntlet's value collapses to "must get it perfect in one shot." That contradicts BBAAX's first-class re-run clause.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning at mount time.

## Scope

- Verify (or add) `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.canonical-onboarding-sequence.sh` handles ordain-* cases against an already-canonical depot, no upstream re-run
- Tamper deliberately — delete a hallmark, blank a regime field, leave a partial reliquary — and verify per-case precondition probes fire with operator-actionable diagnostics naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it
- Confirm §4 crucible fixtures (tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) runnable alone against a §3-completed state, no re-ordain required for crucible debug
- Document the iterative debug workflow into the gauntlet runbook surface BBAAI owns: where to look when X fails, what tabtarget to invoke, how to interpret per-case probe output

## Wrap criterion

- Single-case rerun mechanism demonstrably works for ordain-* cases without marshal-zero, against established canonical state
- Per-case probes verified under deliberately-broken state across at least one ordain case and one crucible fixture
- §4 crucible single-fixture rerun confirmed against §3-completed depot
- Debug workflow referenced from BBAAX's docket so the green-tally pace has tooling on hand

## Cost

~30-45 min wall-clock. Reuses canonical state from a prior end-to-end run; does not require its own marshal-zero + GCP project.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAf landed (canonical-onboarding-sequence fixture + per-case probes exist)
- BBAAg landed (rbw-tP + suite resolver exist)

## Out of scope

- New mechanism design — per-case probe pattern is BBAAf's territory; this pace verifies behavior, not invents it
- BBAAX's first end-to-end green-tally run (uses the tooling proven here)
- Cross-heat debug tooling generalizations

### dissolve-four-mode-and-airgap-preflight (₢BBAA7) [complete]

**[260504-1458] complete**

## Character

Kill four-mode fixture entirely. Absorb its unique per-case verification steps (summon, plumb_full, plumb_compact, layer-DiffID byte-identity round-trip, kludge tag-pair docker-inspect, abjure) into the corresponding onboarding-sequence ordain cases. Closes a current handbook-coverage gap where the ordain stubs walk only the build half but skip the verification steps the handbooks already prescribe. Also tightens foundry preflight against the airgap+empty-anchor latent bug class BBAAX exposed.

## Scope

- four-mode fixture: removed entirely (manifest registration, suite registration, Rust module, dedicated test vessels and constants).
- Onboarding-sequence ordain cases: each grows a verification tail matching the unique steps the deleted four-mode case carried for the same mode. Per-case unique-step audit lives in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs four-mode functions (rbtdrc_fourmode_*_lifecycle); mount-time inspection identifies which steps land in which ordain case.
- rbev-busybox vessel: removed (used only by deleted four-mode conjure_lifecycle). Other four-mode test vessels: ref-check at mount time before deleting (rbev-bottle-plantuml is needed by pluml crucible; rbev-graft-demo refs unaudited).
- Foundry enshrine-preflight loop in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: airgap vessel + non-empty ORIGIN + empty ANCHOR fails-fast via the existing 404-path remediation diagnostic shape. Tether vessels keep the permissive skip (current behavior correct — buildx fetches upstream at build time).

## Constraints

- Onboarding-sequence keeps handbook-walk discipline. The new tail is framed as "complete the handbook walk including post-build verification" — handbooks already include these steps (rbw-Oda explicitly mentions "compare plumb" per tabtarget context) so this closes a coverage gap, not creates one.
- Crucible fixtures stay vessel-source-agnostic per paddock's build/run split. summon/plumb do NOT move into crucible setup; they belong in the build half because the operator does them as part of completing the build cycle.
- Gauntlet sequence shrinks by one fixture (four-mode slot removed from ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET).
- No handbook prose changes — only test code closes the gap to existing handbook content.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAX is the verifier — re-run gauntlet after this pace lands and confirm green tally.

## Out of scope

- Handbook prose updates (paddock framing: handbook = recipe, gauntlet = regression that runs the recipe + verifies the artifact; verification framing stays gauntlet-only).
- Vessel directory deletion beyond rbev-busybox (audit blast radius at mount time before any other vessel delete).
- Tabtarget/spec sweep beyond what rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh regenerates plus RBSFR fundus registry.

### kludge-origin-slot-auto-pull (₢BBABH) [complete]

**[260513-2150] complete**

## Character

`rbfd_kludge`'s presence guard at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1391-1448` lumps anchored-slot and origin-slot misses under one "kludge stays uncredentialed" refusal. The framing is over-broad: anchored slots point into GAR and need GCP auth (wrest stays out-of-band, correct); origin slots are public upstream pulls that need no credentials. The over-broad refusal forces operators and fixtures into an extra step that buys nothing.

Surfaced by ₢BBAAX's first gauntlet run: `onboarding-sequence/rbtdro_onboarding_kludge_tadmor` failed in pristine state because `debian:bookworm-slim` (tadmor sentry's only slot, unanchored pass-through) wasn't cached locally. In any pristine kludge flow every slot is unanchored — anchors don't land until an `ordain conjure` — so the operator-facing diagnostic prescribes `docker pull` for every slot, which kludge itself could just do.

## Scope

- `rbfd_kludge` auto-pulls origin-slot misses inline (announcing each pull at info level for teaching/visibility)
- Anchored-slot miss path unchanged: refusal + wrest remediation, preserving the credentialed-out-of-band property
- `--pull=never` backstop on the build stays as tag-drift defense
- Comment block at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1381-1384` updated to distinguish the two slot types

## Constraints

- No new tabtargets, no handbook edits, no fixture edits
- Origin pull must announce itself — preserve the teaching moment that the current diagnostic provides
- `docker image inspect` re-check after pull stays as a sanity gate before kludge continues

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Blocks ₢BBAAX's next attempt (next `rbw-tP` run after this lands)

## Out of scope

- Anchored-slot ergonomics (separate question, not surfaced by this run)
- `--pull=never` backstop rethink
- Handbook prose update — handbook currently says nothing about pre-pulling, which becomes accurate again once this lands

### docker-login-retry-shape (₢BBAA8) [complete]

**[260530-0926] complete**

## Character

Design conversation requiring judgment. Mechanism choice must follow operator concurrence — load-bearing slate-time instruction.

## Issue evidence

Failed in BBAAX first-run gauntlet attempt at `rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_conjure` verification tail. Conjure cloud build (4m0s) and vouch (1m30s) both succeeded. All GAR artifacts confirmed via gcloud post-failure (image, attest-amd64, attest-arm64, about, vouch tagged correctly). Failure was wrest's `docker login` against `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev`:

    Error response from daemon: Get "https://us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/v2/":
    net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection
    (Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers)

Endpoint probed clean post-failure (curl 134ms, 401 as expected; gcloud listing fast). Documented docker daemon transient per moby/moby#44350: non-configurable Client.Timeout fires before the registry auth backend responds; industry remediation across the corpus is retry. Same case had 4 curl rc=28 warnings during the preceding vouch poll, absorbed by the existing 3-consecutive tolerance.

## Asymmetry to close

`Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` poll loop tolerates this transient class with a 3-consecutive policy (BCG-clean, in production).
`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` `rbfl_wrest`'s docker login is single-shot, fatal on the same transient class. Same daemon, same registry boundary, different fate.

## Scope

Retry shape for transient-vulnerable docker daemon ↔ registry single-shot calls. Immediate surface is wrest's docker login; design surface should include other single-shot daemon calls crossing the same boundary (login, pull, push, inspect with registry round-trip).

## Discuss before implementing — load-bearing

Mount agent surfaces the design space and proposes a recommended shape; operator concurrence precedes landing. No pre-baking at slate-time. Design questions to seat at the table belong in the conversation, not the docket.

## Out of scope

- Curl/gcloud transient audit beyond the docker daemon path — distinct class
- Build-poll loop changes (already tolerant)
- Generic retry framework — only what the surveyed surface requires

## References

- moby/moby#44350 — non-configurable docker daemon Client.Timeout
- BBAAX inline-fix commits 24e24651 (depot Cloud Build URL) and dd5893ba (Mid-execution posture rule) — first-run iteration arc context

### phase-1-depot-lifecycle (₢BBAAC) [abandoned]

**[260427-1233] abandoned**

## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** — `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** — `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** — `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** — `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Roster + verify** — `rbw-arr` (retrievers) and `rbw-adr` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** — `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** — phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)

**[260426-1103] rough**

## Character

First executable phase after the preflight gate. Quota-bearing: GCP project creation has daily quota caps, so this phase is a deliberate discrete operator-aware checkpoint. Re-runs of subsequent phases do NOT re-trigger quota; only Phase 1 does. Operator pauses here consciously.

## Work

Add Phase 1 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Levy depot** — invoke `rbw-dL` PayorLeviesDepot to create the depot; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned
2. **List depots** — invoke `rbw-dl` PayorListsDepots; assert just-created depot appears
3. **Active-depot assertion** — confirm `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the just-created depot
4. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Depot created. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed to Phase 2; preflight will re-validate marshal-zero state." message

Cover create/list/delete via tests:
- **Create test** — depot levy succeeds; verifiable via `rbw-dl`
- **List test** — `rbw-dl` enumerates correctly with the new depot
- **Delete test** — defer to Phase 5 cleanup; deletion mid-tier would break subsequent phases, so it's tested in cleanup context

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after Phase 1 has already created a depot, the preflight still runs (and may fail on RBRA presence if creds got installed mid-stream — design call). If preflight passes and a depot already exists, Phase 1 detects it and skips levy, proceeding to Phase 2. The detector key is "depot exists matching RBRR-derived name."

## Verification

- Phase 1 runs cleanly against post-preflight state
- After Phase 1, depot exists and is the active depot per `RBDC_*`
- Re-running `rbw-tP` after Phase 1 detects already-created depot and proceeds to Phase 2 (subject to preflight passing)
- Quota-aware messaging is clear at phase exit
- Failure during levy surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Service account creation (Phase 2)
- Depot deletion (Phase 5 cleanup)
- Multi-depot scenarios
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier

### phase-2-service-account-lifecycle (₢BBAAD) [abandoned]

**[260426-1126] abandoned**

## Character

Second executable phase. Tests the governor/retriever/director SA lifecycle that every later cloud operation depends on. Adds explicit create/list/delete coverage (the user pointed out this surface had been under-tested).

## Work

Add Phase 2 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Mantle governor** — `rbw-aM` PayorMantlesGovernor; verify governor SA exists in IAM
2. **Charter retriever** — `rbw-aC` GovernorChartersRetriever; verify retriever SA exists with correct roles
3. **Knight director** — `rbw-aK` GovernorKnightsDirector; verify director SA exists with correct roles
4. **List service accounts** — `rbw-aL` GovernorListsServiceAccounts; assert all three present, classified correctly by role
5. **Forfeit cycle test** — within Phase 2, create a throwaway named SA, list it, then `rbw-aF` GovernorForfeitsServiceAccount, list again, assert clean removal — exercises the forfeit machinery without touching the three production SAs
6. **IAM propagation wait** — Phase 2 cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for the production SAs; subsequent phases assume they work

Each step's verification is a tabtarget output assertion, not just exit-code success — confirm the SA's role bindings match the contract documented in RBSCIG (IamGrantContracts).

## Verification

- All three production SAs (governor, retriever, director) present after Phase 2
- `rbw-aL` enumerates all three with correct role classifications
- Throwaway forfeit cycle leaves no residue
- IAM propagation has completed (Phase 3 ordains succeed without retries on auth grounds)
- Failed forfeit (e.g., on a non-existent SA) surfaces with actionable diagnostic

## Out of scope

- Reliquary inscribe (Phase 3)
- Production SA cleanup at end-of-tier (Phase 5)
- IAM grant contract changes (RBSCIG is authoritative; this phase exercises but doesn't modify)

### phase-3-reliquary-and-hallmark-lifecycle (₢BBAAE) [abandoned]

**[260427-1233] abandoned**

## Character

Third executable phase. The largest cloud-side phase by surface area: exercises every producer mode (conjure, bind, graft) plus reliquary inscribe and full consumer flow (vouch, summon, plumb, tally). This is the phase where the recent first-build spook bug class (kludge-aware-charge-prereq, ZRBOB_PROJECT, reliquary integrity) gets caught at construction rather than discovery.

## Work

Add Phase 3 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Inscribe reliquary** — `rbw-dI` DirectorInscribesReliquary; verify reliquary stamp lands under `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}reliquaries/<date>/<tool>` with all canonical tools present (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo)
2. **Conjure small vessel** — `rbw-fO` against a small conjure-mode vessel (suggest `rbev-busybox` or sentry-deb-tether); verify hallmark publication with shape `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/{image,vouch,pouch,about,attest,diags}`
3. **Bind small vessel** — `rbw-fO` against a bind-mode vessel; verify digest pin and hallmark
4. **Graft small vessel** — `rbw-fk` LocalKludge then `rbw-fO` graft path; verify GRAFTED vouch verdict and `<hallmark>/` shape (no pouch for graft)
5. **Vouch all three** — `rbw-fV` mode-aware vouch; verify each verdict shape matches mode contract (SLSA for conjure, digest-pin for bind, GRAFTED for graft)
6. **Summon each** — `rbw-fs` RetrieverSummonsHallmark; verify local image present
7. **Plumb compact + full** — `rbw-fpc` and `rbw-fpf`; verify metadata fetch from new GAR layout
8. **Tally** — `rbw-ft` RetrieverTalliesHallmarks; verify all three hallmarks classified correctly by health state
9. **Rekon** — `rbw-irh` and `rbw-irr`; verify ark basenames match canonical members under hallmark and reliquary subtrees

Implicitly exercises the kludge-aware-charge-prereq fix path: k-prefixed kludge hallmarks classify correctly without false missing-vouch warnings.

## Verification

- All three hallmarks present in GAR with correct shape per mode
- Each vouch verdict matches mode contract
- Summon + plumb succeed against new layout
- Tally counts and classifications correct
- Rekon surfaces all expected canonical members
- No false missing-vouch warnings on k-prefixed paths

## Out of scope

- Crucible charge / security tests (Phase 4)
- Hallmark abjure (Phase 5)
- New vessel creation (uses existing kit-shipped vessels)

### phase-4-crucible-suite (₢BBAAF) [abandoned]

**[260427-1233] abandoned**

## Character

Fourth executable phase. Brings up containers; runs the security and capability fixtures against freshly-built hallmarks. This is where moriah's airgap test cases finally run on a depot whose first-time creation we just validated end-to-end. The user noted: "I've been pretending like I don't need to test depot creation but maybe I do" — this phase is where that decision becomes operational.

## Work

Add Phase 4 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Charge tadmor** — `rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`; verify sentry+pentacle+bottle up cleanly under prefixed names
2. **Run tadmor security suite** — `rbtd-r.Run.tadmor.sh` (or single-case mode against the just-charged crucible); verify 34 security cases pass against a freshly-conjured tadmor hallmark
3. **Quench tadmor** — `rbw-cQ.Quench.tadmor.sh`; verify cleanup
4. **Charge moriah** — airgap nameplate; verify charge with airgap topology against fresh enshrined-base + airgap-conjured vessel
5. **Run moriah airgap suite** — capability fixtures verify airgap discipline holds end-to-end against fresh-cloud-built hallmark
6. **Quench moriah**
7. **Charge srjcl + run srjcl-jupyter (3 cases) + quench**
8. **Charge pluml + run pluml-diagram (5 cases) + quench**

Sequential only — fixtures share regime state and container/network namespaces. Per-fixture quench-after-test discipline so a failure in one fixture doesn't poison the next.

The moriah test specifically validates the airgap path that requires `rbw-dE` enshrine + airgap-mode `rbw-fO` to have completed in Phase 3 against the freshly-created depot. If Phase 3 was wrong, moriah fails here.

## Verification

- All four crucibles charge successfully against fresh hallmarks
- Tadmor security suite green
- Moriah airgap suite green (the test that motivated this whole heat)
- srjcl-jupyter green (3 cases)
- pluml-diagram green (5 cases)
- Charge/quench cycles leave no residual state between fixtures

## Out of scope

- Cleanup of hallmarks/SAs/depot (Phase 5)
- Test infrastructure changes (any test failures here surface in subsequent paces or follow-up heats)
- Adding new fixtures (this phase exercises the existing four)

8. **Charge pluml + run pluml-diagram (5 cases) + quench**

Sequential only — fixtures share regime state and container/network namespaces. Per-fixture quench-after-test discipline so a failure in one fixture doesn't poison the next.

The moriah test specifically validates the airgap path that requires `rbw-dE` enshrine + airgap-mode `rbw-fO` to have completed in Phase 3 against the freshly-created depot. If Phase 3 was wrong, moriah fails here.

## Verification

- All four crucibles charge successfully against fresh hallmarks
- Tadmor security suite green
- Moriah airgap suite green (the test that motivated this whole heat)
- srjcl-jupyter green (3 cases)
- pluml-diagram green (5 cases)
- Charge/quench cycles leave no residual state between fixtures

## Out of scope

- Cleanup of hallmarks/SAs/depot (Phase 5)
- Test infrastructure changes (any test failures here surface in subsequent paces or follow-up heats)
- Adding new fixtures (this phase exercises the existing four)

### phase-5-cleanup-and-first-end-to-end-run (₢BBAAG) [abandoned]

**[260427-1233] abandoned**

## Character

Cleanup phase plus the actual first run. The tier doesn't ship until it's been run end-to-end against fresh state and surprises captured. Failures during the first run are *informative*, not blockers — they document the surprises this whole heat exists to surface.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so divest colophons are available and unmake-absorbs-governor-cleanup is in effect.

## Work

### Cleanup phase implementation

Add Phase 5 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Abjure all hallmarks** — `rbw-fA` for each ordained hallmark; verify clean removal via `rbw-irh`.
2. **Divest operational SAs** — `rbw-arD` for retriever, `rbw-adD` for director (BBAAN cult-verbs). Per BBAAN: divest reads installed RBRA, performs REST DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file. Verify clean removal via `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster (empty observation facts expected).
3. **Unmake depot** — `rbw-dU` PayorUnmakesDepot. Per BBAAN: governor SA cleanup folds into unmake (404-tolerant on absent governor). No standalone forfeit-governor step. Verify quota recovery.
4. **Final tally** — `rbw-dl` shows no depots, `rbw-irh`/`rbw-irr` surfaces clean GAR namespace, `rbw-arr`/`rbw-adr` roster empty.

### First end-to-end run

Execute the FIRST end-to-end run of `rbw-tP` against actual fresh state:

1. Marshal zero, commit (using the new signature from Phase 0)
2. Install RBRAs from secure backup
3. Run `rbw-tP` — full sequence; expect ~1 hour wall-clock
4. Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX — in a notch dedicated to this pace's findings
5. Fix small surprises inline within this pace (one-line bash fixes, message clarity)
6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

### release-ceremony-and-bcg-command-lint (₢BBABM) [complete]

**[260531-1344] complete**

## Character

Was mechanical ceremony repair; now carries a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture with a real design surface. Three mechanical tracks (ceremony retirement cleanup, claimed-done fixes, verification sweep) frame one design-laden track: a BCG command-compliance linter in theurge. The linter needs judgment; the rest is mechanical. The locked decisions below are settled — do not relitigate them; mount-time minting of the new module/fixture name is the open naming call.

## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8)

- **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** The external-command audit becomes a static-analysis fixture in theurge's **fast test suite** — not a bash step in `rbq_qualify_release()`. It rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and, via the `complete` suite, `rbw-tr` (release gate), which the ceremony's Step 4 runs pre-strip. That coverage is what lets ceremony **Step 2 be deleted**.
- **BCG is the single source of truth.** Encode the allowlists from BCG section "Command Dependency Discipline": the POSIX floor, the declared-dependency set (RBS0 inventory + `openssl`), and the **eviction table — enforced**, failing with BCG's prescribed replacement (e.g. `grep` -> `case`/`[[ =~ ]]`). This is stricter than today's Step 2, whose hand-rolled POSIX list wrongly permits evicted commands.
- **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect every locally-defined function across the corpus; then flag each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, POSIX allowlist, declared deps}, and fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on presumed BCG-compliance (shellcheck already gates the corpus in `rbq_qualify_fast`) — a command-position scan suffices; no heredoc/case parser.
- **Checks everything pre-veiling.** Lints ALL `Tools/` bash including `vov_veiled/`. The linter carries **no veil-scope knowledge** — it is a linter, not veiling logic, and must not couple to either packaging path (`vof` kit-parcel collection, or the ceremony strip). The only partition is by **execution environment**: kit-bash (strict BCG) vs GCB-bash (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/` — looser; gcloud et al. present). **Encode the GCB-domain allowlist now**; do not skip those dirs.
- **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Checking veiled bash for the first time may surface real eviction-table violations; fix them in this pace, do not split to a follow-on. Expect the first run not to land green.
- **Track 4 resolved.** `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` pointing at `recipebottle-staging.git` is **intended**. No remote mutation. Ensure no ceremony/doc text asserts the non-staging URL as canonical.

## Tracks

### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup

In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).

### Track 2 — BCG command-lint fixture (locked above)

Build per the locked decisions. Model on the host-side fast-fixture pattern (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`: `dir: &Path -> rbtdre_Verdict`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, added to the `fast` suite). Mint a fresh anchor word — `audit` is taken (image-domain cult verb). Then delete ceremony Step 2.

### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done

Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.

### Track 5 — Verification sweep

After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.

## Verification gates

Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.

## References

- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target
- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` section "Command Dependency Discipline" — allowlist source of truth (POSIX floor, eviction table, declared-dep principle)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fast-fixture shape + suite/manifest registration
- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; shellcheck sibling; Step 2 deletion rationale
- `Tools/vok/vof/` — existing kit-parcel veiling (`vofr_collect` excludes vov_veiled); the linter must NOT couple to it
- Commits b7c1dbdd (retirement), 5dc8dcc6 (README restructure), b63dc6cf (foundry colophon rbw-h* -> rbw-f*)
- Absorbed ₢A8AAA D4+D5; D3 (CLAUDE.consumer glossary) landed on 5dc8dcc6, excluded

**[260531-0746] rough**

## Character

Ceremony repair plus a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture. The fixture (Track 2,
the design-laden track) is **built, tested, and registered** — what remains is one
operator scope decision, the locked first-run cleanup, and the three mechanical
ceremony tracks. Do not relitigate the locked decisions below; the open call is the
cross-kit lint scope named under "Open decision."

## Landed (Track 2 — cupel fixture)

Commits 5d59fdb, 24337f7, a35c5a4. New `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdru_cupel.rs` — a
two-pass function-aware command-position lexer over all `Tools/` bash; anchor word
`cupel` (assay vessel, theurge metallurgy family). `rbtdtu_cupel.rs` unit-tests the
lexer. Registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` (empty required-colophons), `lib.rs`, the
fixtures registry, and the six fast-carrying suites (fast/service/crucible/complete/
gauntlet/skirmish). Lexer handles case/array-literal/arithmetic/heredoc/quote/
comment; `ABANDONED*` dirs excluded (operator decision); GCB partition by `rbgj`
path prefix. 134 theurge tests pass; builds clean under deny(warnings).

## Open decision (mount-time, operator) — cross-kit lint scope

Discovery flags ALL `Tools/` bash, but only the RB-parceled kits answer to RBS0's
Dependency Inventory. Non-RB kits (`lmci`, `apck`, `jjk`, `vvk`, `vslk`, `cmk`)
carry their own dependency realities — e.g. `cargo` in Rust-build bash, `tc`/`ip`
in network diagnostics. Decide one of: (a) a single RBS0 profile governs all
`Tools/` bash (non-RB tools then become declared-dep additions or real violations);
(b) scope cupel to RB kits only (`rbk`/`buk`/`cmk`/`vok`); (c) per-kit profiles.
This sorts each finding into "fix" vs "out of scope." Settle before first-run cleanup.

## Remaining work

1. Resolve the scope decision above.
2. **Encode the GCB allowlist** — `ZRBTDRU_GCB_EXTRA` is still empty. Run the gcb
   case (the kit case fails first, so suite fail-fast stops gcb — run it alone via
   `tt/rbw-tc.FixtureCase.sh cupel rbtdru_gcb_bash` or reorder), enumerate command
   usage under `Tools/rbk/rbgj*`, codify the allowlist.
3. **First-run cleanup (in-pace, locked)** — fix eviction violations in in-scope
   kits. Rediscover via `tt/rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh cupel`, then read
   `cupel-kit-findings.txt` in the run's trace dir. Findings split into eviction-
   table hits (grep/wc/tr/head/ls/base64/awk/cut — BCG-prescribed replacements) and
   unknown-command findings (triage: declared-dep addition vs fix). Includes a real
   bug cupel caught: `Tools/rbk/rbgb_Buckets.sh` calls `buc_warning` (undefined) —
   should be `buc_warn`.
4. Land cupel green, then **delete ceremony Step 2** (the hand-rolled external-
   command audit) from `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`.

## Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup

In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired
`cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase
`readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) —
replace with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10
survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep
`git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add
(`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2.

## Track 3 — Verify claimed-done

Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` ->
`tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C
(Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-
completeness check present) verify-and-close. Track 4 resolved: `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM`
-> `recipebottle-staging.git` is intended; ensure no ceremony/doc asserts the
non-staging URL as canonical.

## Track 5 — Verification sweep

After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/
`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*`
foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify
`tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role
track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.

## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8) — do not relitigate

- **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** Rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and,
  via `complete`, `rbw-tr` (release gate). That coverage lets ceremony Step 2 be deleted.
- **BCG is the single source of truth.** POSIX floor + declared deps (RBS0 inventory
  + `openssl`) + the eviction table, enforced with BCG's prescribed replacement.
- **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect local functions across the corpus,
  then flag command-position tokens not in {builtins, locals, POSIX floor, declared
  deps}; fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. No heredoc/case parser mandated
  (soundness rests on the shellcheck-clean corpus) — though case/array/arithmetic
  handling was added in-build to suppress false positives.
- **Checks everything pre-veiling**, partitioned only by execution environment:
  kit-bash (strict) vs GCB-bash (`Tools/rbk/rbgj*` — looser; gcloud et al. present).
  No coupling to veiling/packaging paths.
- **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Expect the first run not to land green.
- **Track 4 resolved** (staging URL intended; no remote mutation).

## References

- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target
- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` "Command Dependency Discipline" — allowlist source
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` "Dependency Inventory" — declared-dep source
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdru_cupel.rs` / `rbtdtu_cupel.rs` — the fixture
- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; Step 2 deletion rationale
- Commits b7c1dbdd (retirement), 5dc8dcc6 (README restructure), b63dc6cf (foundry colophon rbw-h* -> rbw-f*)

**[260531-0653] rough**

## Character

Was mechanical ceremony repair; now carries a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture with a real design surface. Three mechanical tracks (ceremony retirement cleanup, claimed-done fixes, verification sweep) frame one design-laden track: a BCG command-compliance linter in theurge. The linter needs judgment; the rest is mechanical. The locked decisions below are settled — do not relitigate them; mount-time minting of the new module/fixture name is the open naming call.

## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8)

- **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** The external-command audit becomes a static-analysis fixture in theurge's **fast test suite** — not a bash step in `rbq_qualify_release()`. It rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and, via the `complete` suite, `rbw-tr` (release gate), which the ceremony's Step 4 runs pre-strip. That coverage is what lets ceremony **Step 2 be deleted**.
- **BCG is the single source of truth.** Encode the allowlists from BCG section "Command Dependency Discipline": the POSIX floor, the declared-dependency set (RBS0 inventory + `openssl`), and the **eviction table — enforced**, failing with BCG's prescribed replacement (e.g. `grep` -> `case`/`[[ =~ ]]`). This is stricter than today's Step 2, whose hand-rolled POSIX list wrongly permits evicted commands.
- **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect every locally-defined function across the corpus; then flag each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, POSIX allowlist, declared deps}, and fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on presumed BCG-compliance (shellcheck already gates the corpus in `rbq_qualify_fast`) — a command-position scan suffices; no heredoc/case parser.
- **Checks everything pre-veiling.** Lints ALL `Tools/` bash including `vov_veiled/`. The linter carries **no veil-scope knowledge** — it is a linter, not veiling logic, and must not couple to either packaging path (`vof` kit-parcel collection, or the ceremony strip). The only partition is by **execution environment**: kit-bash (strict BCG) vs GCB-bash (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/` — looser; gcloud et al. present). **Encode the GCB-domain allowlist now**; do not skip those dirs.
- **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Checking veiled bash for the first time may surface real eviction-table violations; fix them in this pace, do not split to a follow-on. Expect the first run not to land green.
- **Track 4 resolved.** `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` pointing at `recipebottle-staging.git` is **intended**. No remote mutation. Ensure no ceremony/doc text asserts the non-staging URL as canonical.

## Tracks

### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup

In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).

### Track 2 — BCG command-lint fixture (locked above)

Build per the locked decisions. Model on the host-side fast-fixture pattern (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`: `dir: &Path -> rbtdre_Verdict`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, added to the `fast` suite). Mint a fresh anchor word — `audit` is taken (image-domain cult verb). Then delete ceremony Step 2.

### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done

Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.

### Track 5 — Verification sweep

After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.

## Verification gates

Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.

## Character

Mechanical ceremony repair + verification sweep, with two embedded judgment calls (Track 2 scope, Track 4 remote). `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` is currently un-runnable on two counts: it copies a retired source file *and* invokes a non-existent tabtarget. This pace brings the ceremony current and hardens the qualify orchestrator.

Absorbed scope: ₢A8AAA D4 (retirement cleanup) and D5 (verification sweep). D3 (CLAUDE.consumer.md glossary) landed on 5dc8dcc6 and is excluded.

## Context

Three upstream changes invalidated parts of the ceremony, none yet reflected in it:

- **b7c1dbdd** — retired `index.html`, `README.consumer.md`, `.nojekyll`.
- **5dc8dcc6** — root `README.md` restructured into the canonical consumer doc; CLAUDE.consumer.md glossary replaced by a trigger directive.
- **b63dc6cf** — foundry colophon restructure `rbw-h*` -> `rbw-f*`; `rbw-h*` now belongs exclusively to Handbook.

The 2026-05-30 grounding pass confirmed the un-runnable premise and surfaced corrections now baked into the tracks.

## Docket

### Track 1 — Retirement artifact cleanup (from A8AAA D4)

In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`:

1. Step 2's "Declared dependencies (index.html promise)" — repoint to the current authoritative dependency source (README Prerequisites, or an RBS0 anchor); decide during execution. Coordinate with Track 2, which may delete Step 2 entirely.
2. Step 8 — delete the `cp .../README.consumer.md README.md` line (source retired). Keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy. Root `README.md` is already the tracked consumer doc, carried by the squash merge + `git add -u`.
3. Lowercase `readme.md` handling — drop it entirely. Git tracks only `README.md`; the lowercase path is a case-insensitive-filesystem alias, not a separate tracked file, so `git rm ... readme.md` is a no-op. Replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html` and `.nojekyll`.
4. Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired).
5. Step 10 staging — `git add CLAUDE.md` stays (Step 8 overwrites it from the consumer template); `README.md` no longer needs an explicit add (`git add -u` covers it).

### Track 2 — Command-audit automation (AUAAR item A) — DESIGN FORK

Add an external-command audit to `rbq_qualify_release()` in `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh`, inserted after `rbq_qualify_fast` (which ends in shellcheck) and before the complete test suite. No audit helper exists in `Tools/buk/buq_qualify.sh` today — this is net-new code.

**Decision required before building.** The current ceremony Step 2 is deliberately an LLM task because a sound allowlist audit ("flag any command not in the declared/POSIX tiers") is hard to do reliably in bash. Operator chooses:

- **Denylist sweep** (recommended) — grep for the known offenders the ceremony already enumerates (gcloud, podman, python, shellcheck, column, openssl, base64, shasum, ...). Tractable; catches the real cases.
- **Full allowlist audit** — faithful to Step 2's intent but brittle in bash.

Scope: `.sh` under `Tools/buk/` and `Tools/rbk/`, excluding cloudbuild dirs (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/`) and `**/vov_veiled/`. Once automated, delete ceremony Step 2 (redundant with pre-strip `rbw-tr`).

### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done items (AUAAR B/C/D)

- **Item B — now a FIX, not a close.** Prior docket said "Step 11 already uses `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` — confirm and close." That tabtarget does not exist; QualifyFast is `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Correct the phantom reference in Step 11 (and in Track 5 step 3 below).
- **Item C — close.** Step 9 `rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` runs before Step 10's strip (which removes the MZ tabtarget). Ordering verified robust.
- **Item D — close.** Step 3 regime-completeness check is present and current.

### Track 4 — OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM remote (AUAAR item E) — OPERATOR CONFIRM

Prior docket assumed the remote should be `git@github.com:scaleinv/recipebottle.git`. Live state points at `git@github.com:scaleinv/recipebottle-staging.git` — plausibly a deliberate staging target for candidate-branch review. Do NOT mutate. Surface the discrepancy and let the operator confirm whether `-staging` is intended (likely) or the remote should be repointed. Shared-state change -> explicit confirmation required before any `git remote set-url`.

### Track 5 — Verification sweep (from A8AAA D5)

After Tracks 1-4 land:

1. Retirement grep — no live refs to `index.html`, `README.consumer.md`, `.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos.
2. Foundry colophon grep — no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible `.sh`/`.md`/`.adoc` (post b63dc6cf, `rbw-h*` is Handbook-only).
3. Fast qualify — `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` (corrected from the phantom `rbw-tf`).
4. Manual smoke — exercise the onboarding entry point (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh`) and at least one role track.
5. Notch remaining changes.
6. Ask operator for go-ahead to wrap. Do NOT auto-wrap.

### Verification gates

- Each track presented before executing.
- Review point before committing the ceremony diff.
- Track 2 design fork resolved before the audit is built.
- Operator confirmation before any remote mutation (Track 4).
- No auto-wrap — operator decides when the pace closes.

## Character

Mechanical ceremony rewrite + verification sweep. The `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` file is currently a foot-gun — running it today would fail at Step 8 because it copies a retired file. This pace consolidates all prep-release fixes under a single owner: retirement cleanup (absorbed from ₢A8AAA D4), original AUAAR refinements, plus the verification sweep (absorbed from ₢A8AAA D5).

Absorbed scope: ₢A8AAA D4 and D5. ₢A8AAA D3 (CLAUDE.consumer.md glossary) already landed on commit 5dc8dcc6 and is excluded.

## Context

The current prep-release ceremony assumes an older architecture. Three upstream changes invalidated parts of it:

- **b7c1dbdd** (₣A8 D1-D3): retired `index.html`, `README.consumer.md`, `.nojekyll`; RBSPH created; RBSCO reshaped.
- **5dc8dcc6** (₢A8AAG): CLAUDE.consumer.md 11-term glossary replaced with trigger directive pointing to README.
- **b63dc6cf** (₢A8AAK): foundry colophon restructure `rbw-h*` → `rbw-f*`; 15 hardcoded `buh_tc` foundry paths converted to `buh_tT` with zipper constants.

None of these are reflected in `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`. The ceremony must be brought current before the first release attempt.

Several original AUAAR items may have been resolved by intervening work; tracks below verify current state before editing.

## Docket

### Track 1 — Retirement artifact cleanup (absorbed from A8AAA D4)

Fix stale references to retired files in `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`:

1. **Step 2 line 35**: replace `(index.html promise)` with something pointing at the current authoritative source for declared dependencies (README Prerequisites section, or an RBS0-anchored statement). Decide during execution.
2. **Step 8 lines 106-115**: delete `cp Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/README.consumer.md README.md` (source file retired). Keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy line. Drop the trailing note about lowercase `readme.md`.
3. **Step 10e line 202**: delete `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch readme.md`. Add defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html` and `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch .nojekyll`.
4. **Step 10 survival list (lines 215-226)**: remove `.nojekyll` (line 220) and `index.html` (line 221) from the "what should survive" enumeration.
5. **Step 10f line 207**: reconsider `git add CLAUDE.md README.md`. Under the new architecture README.md is the canonical consumer doc already tracked in the squash merge; `git add -u` handles its modifications. `git add CLAUDE.md` stays because Step 8 overwrites it from the consumer template.

### Track 2 — Command audit automation (original AUAAR item A)

Add external-command audit to `rbq_qualify_release()` between shellcheck and the `.complete.` test suite.

**Declared dependencies**: bash, git, curl, ssh/scp/ssh-keygen, jq, docker.

**Assumed POSIX/coreutils** (any system with bash): chmod, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, mktemp, date, sleep, cat, grep, sed, awk, sort, head, tail, wc, tee, touch, ln, tr, cut, printf, test, true, false, kill.

Scope: all `.sh` files under `Tools/buk/` and `Tools/rbk/` except cloudbuild scripts (`Tools/rbk/rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/`) and `**/vov_veiled/`.

Once automated in `rbq_qualify_release()`, the ceremony's **Step 2 (external command audit as LLM task) becomes redundant** — pre-strip qualification (Step 4 via `rbw-tr`) covers it. Delete Step 2 as part of this track.

### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done items in current ceremony

Three original AUAAR items may already be resolved by changes that landed after the pace was drafted. Read the current ceremony and either close the item or capture remaining work.

- **Item B (post-strip fast vs full qualify)**: current Step 11 already uses `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh`. Confirm and close.
- **Item C (stripped-tabtarget marshal reset)**: original text cited `rbw-MR.MarshalReset.sh` called from "Step 10"; current Step 9 calls `rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` and runs **before** Step 10's strip. Verify this ordering is robust. If fine as-is, close. If fragile, fix via direct `rblm_reset` invocation from `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh`.
- **Item D (regime variable completeness check)**: current Step 3 implements this. Confirm and close.

### Track 4 — OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM remote (original AUAAR item E)

Ensure the `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` git remote points at `git@github.com:scaleinv/recipebottle.git`.

1. `git remote -v` — inspect current state.
2. If already correct, document as done.
3. Otherwise, set via `git remote add` or `git remote set-url`. **User confirmation required** before any remote mutation — this is a shared-state change.

### Track 5 — Verification sweep (absorbed from A8AAA D5)

After Tracks 1-4 land, run the sweep:

1. **Retirement grep**: confirm no live references to `index.html`, `README.consumer.md`, or `.nojekyll` outside retired gallops and memo artifacts.
2. **Foundry colophon grep**: confirm no stale `rbw-h*` foundry references in consumer-visible files (customer-visible `.sh`/`.md`/`.adoc`). The `rbw-h*` namespace now belongs exclusively to Handbook after b63dc6cf.
3. **Fast qualify**: `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` (or the then-current qualify-fast tabtarget — verify during execution).
4. **Manual smoke**: exercise the current onboarding entry point (check `tt/` for the correct tabtarget — `rbw-o` is listed as "Onboarding start") and one or more role tracks.
5. **Notch** remaining changes.
6. **Ask user for go-ahead to wrap the heat.** Do NOT auto-wrap.

### Verification gates

- Each track presented to user before executing.
- User review point before committing the ceremony diff.
- User confirmation before any remote mutation (Track 4).
- No auto-wrap — user decides when the pace closes.

### A. Add command audit to rbq_qualify_release()

Insert between shellcheck and `.complete.` test suite in `rbq_qualify_release()`. The audit scans surviving .sh files for external command invocations beyond declared dependencies.

**Declared dependencies** (index.html promise): bash, git, curl, ssh/scp/ssh-keygen, jq, docker

**Assumed POSIX/coreutils** (any system with bash): chmod, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, mktemp, date, sleep, cat, grep, sed, awk, sort, head, tail, wc, tee, touch, ln, tr, cut, printf, test, true, false, kill

Excludes cloudbuild scripts (Tools/rbk/rbgja/, rbgjb/, rbgjv/) — those run on GCB, not the user's workstation. Flags anything not in either tier (gcloud, podman, python, shellcheck, column, openssl, base64, shasum, etc.).

### B. Update ceremony post-strip step

Step 9 (post-strip qualification) changes from full `rbw-QR` to fast-only verification (`rbq_qualify_fast` or equivalent). The `.complete.` test suite already passed pre-strip on develop — post-strip just verifies stripping didn't break wiring (tabtargets resolve, colophons match modules).

### C. Fix marshal reset invocation

Step 10 calls `tt/rbw-MR.MarshalReset.sh` which was stripped in Step 8c. Fix: invoke `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` directly (source and call `rblm_reset`), or reorder to run marshal reset before stripping the tabtarget.

### D. Regime variable completeness check

Already in ceremony (Step 3). LLM reads regime enrollment files and corresponding specs, reports gaps. Informational only — doesn't block.

### E. Remote configuration

Update OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM remote to point to scaleinv/recipebottle (git@github.com:scaleinv/recipebottle.git).

- rbq_Qualify.sh — qualification orchestrator
- .claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md — the ceremony
- index.html lines 211-213 — dependency claim
- Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh — lifecycle marshal (direct invocation target)
