Create /rbk-prep-release slash command — the full release ceremony.

## Remote Configuration

Current OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM remote points to bhyslop/recipemuster.git (old repo). Destination is scaleinv/recipebottle (git@github.com:scaleinv/recipebottle.git). First step: add or update remote to point to correct destination.

## Consumer Document Sources

Consumer CLAUDE.md and README.md source templates exist at:
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/CLAUDE.consumer.md` → copy to `CLAUDE.md`
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/README.consumer.md` → copy to `README.md`

These source templates are themselves in vov_veiled/ and will be stripped — only the root copies survive.

## Pre-release Verification

- Beseech command (rbw-DB): removed in ₢AUAAU — no longer a concern.
- Marshal (rbw-MR) is excluded from consumer CLAUDE.md (veiled, private fork only).
- Copyright year in README.consumer.md is 2026 — verify matches at release time.

## Regime Variable Completeness (absorbed from ₢AUAAH)

As part of building the release qualification gate, add a regime variable completeness check:

1. **Discovery**: Survey buv_* enrollment patterns across all *_regime.sh files. Map each regime type (rbrr, rbra, rbrn, rbrp, rbrv, rbro) to its spec document.
2. **Checker**: Add a completeness check to rbq_Qualify.sh release tier (rbw-QR) that verifies every enrolled regime variable has spec treatment in the relevant .adoc document.
3. **Catalogue gaps**: Run the checker and report any missing spec coverage. Fixing gaps is out of scope — those become future paces/itches.

Scope decisions (from AUAAH open questions): 'variable name appears in a spec' is sufficient for release gate — no need to trace producers/consumers.

## Ceremony Sequence

1. Run rbw-QR release qualification gate (now includes regime variable completeness) — fail-fast on develop
2. Verify develop is clean and pushed
3. Sync main with upstream (git fetch/pull from scaleinv/recipebottle)
4. Create candidate branch from main, squash-merge develop
5. On candidate: strip proprietary content (see strip list below)
6. On candidate: copy consumer CLAUDE.md template to CLAUDE.md
7. On candidate: copy consumer README.md template to README.md
8. On candidate: marshal reset (rbw-MR equivalent — blank site-specific config, delete depot credentials)
9. On candidate: delete podman-gateway-proposal.md if present
10. Generate commit
11. Final review — user inspects and pushes manually

## Strip List

Primary mechanism — recursive glob:
- **/vov_veiled/ — all proprietary specs, guides, memos, dormant code (podman scripts, ABANDONED-github) across all kits

Whole directories:
- .claude/ — commands, JJ gallops, agent infrastructure
- lenses/ — should be empty after AUAAO, remove directory
- Memos/ — internal memos
- Tools/ccck/ — Claude Code Container Kit (internal)
- Tools/cmk/ — Concept Model Kit (separate project)
- Tools/gad/ — GAD subsystem (not part of Recipe Bottle)
- Tools/hmk/ — Hard-state Machine Kit (internal)
- Tools/jjk/ — Job Jockey Kit (internal project management)
- Tools/lmci/ — Language Model Console Integration (internal)
- Tools/temp-buk/ — Temp artifacts
- Tools/vok/ — VOK/Rust binary layer (internal)
- Tools/vslf-rbw/ — Visual SlickEdit (internal)
- Tools/vslk/ — Visual SlickEdit Kit (internal)
- Tools/vvc/ — Voce Viva Commands (internal)
- Tools/vvk/ — Voce Viva Kit (internal)

What survives:
- Tools/buk/ (minus vov_veiled/) — tabtarget infrastructure, consumer needs it
- Tools/rbk/ (minus vov_veiled/) — core Recipe Bottle kit
- CLAUDE.md — replaced with consumer template
- README.md — replaced with consumer template
- index.html, rbm-abstract-drawio.svg, LICENSE, .nojekyll — project page assets
- tt/ — tabtargets
- .rbk/ — regime config (blanked by marshal reset)

Decision needed: do Tools/buk/ internals (buts/) need selective stripping beyond vov_veiled/ glob?

### prep-release-integration (₢BBAAH) [abandoned]

**[260601-0503] abandoned**

## Character

Final integration pace. Wires pristine-pass as a precondition for `/rbk-prep-release` so the upstream contribution ceremony cannot proceed without a recent pristine green. Closes the loop: pristine qualification becomes load-bearing on release prep, not optional ceremony.

## Work

Modify `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` to add a precondition step: verify rbw-tP ran green against the current HEAD. If not, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence.

Recency contract is **exact-HEAD-match** — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes. rbw-tP green tally writes an artifact linking pass to HEAD's commit SHA; `/rbk-prep-release` reads it and refuses on mismatch. Artifact is gitignored (deliberately not committed). Artifact mechanism (file location, contents) is a mount-time decision.

Update README "Release Procedure" section to reflect that the prep-release step now enforces rbw-tP had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing. README references the post-success cleanup ceremony documented in RELEASE.md (owned by BBAAI).

## Verification

- `/rbk-prep-release` fails fast when no pristine pass is recorded for current HEAD
- After successful rbw-tP, `/rbk-prep-release` proceeds normally
- After successful rbw-tP followed by an unrelated commit, `/rbk-prep-release` refuses (SHA mismatch)
- Pristine-passed artifact is gitignored
- README precondition documentation references the cleanup ceremony in RELEASE.md

## Out of scope

- Cleanup ceremony documentation in RELEASE.md (owned by BBAAI)
- Restructuring the rest of `/rbk-prep-release` (preserved as-is)
- Multi-pristine-run aggregation
- CI integration
- Recency policies beyond exact-HEAD-match (other policies left for future heats if needed)

**[260428-0718] rough**

## Character

Final integration pace. Wires pristine-pass as a precondition for `/rbk-prep-release` so the upstream contribution ceremony cannot proceed without a recent pristine green. Closes the loop: pristine qualification becomes load-bearing on release prep, not optional ceremony. Adds documentation for the post-success cleanup ceremony operators need given the gauntlet design omits automatic teardown.

## Work

Modify `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` to add a precondition step: verify rbw-tP ran green against the current HEAD. If not, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence.

Recency contract is **exact-HEAD-match** — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes. rbw-tP green tally writes an artifact linking pass to HEAD's commit SHA; `/rbk-prep-release` reads it and refuses on mismatch. Artifact mechanism (file location, contents, gitignore status) is a mount-time decision.

Update README "Release Procedure" section to reflect that the prep-release step now enforces rbw-tP had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing.

Document the post-success cleanup ceremony — since the gauntlet suite omits teardown so operators can peek at final state, document the manual cleanup sequence (`rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`) operators run when ready to release the canonical infrastructure. README and/or RELEASE.md is the placement decision at mount time (coordinate with BBAAI).

## Verification

- `/rbk-prep-release` fails fast when no pristine pass is recorded for current HEAD
- After successful rbw-tP, `/rbk-prep-release` proceeds normally
- After successful rbw-tP followed by an unrelated commit, `/rbk-prep-release` refuses (SHA mismatch)
- Pristine-passed artifact's gitignore status is correct (deliberately not committed)
- Documentation accurately describes the precondition and the post-success cleanup ceremony

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the rest of `/rbk-prep-release` (preserved as-is)
- Multi-pristine-run aggregation
- CI integration
- Recency policies beyond exact-HEAD-match (other policies left for future heats if needed)

## Work

Modify .claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md to add a precondition step at or near step 1:

- Verify a recent rbw-tP run was green
- Recency contract — design call: most recent commit on main? Last 7 days? Tagged release marker on the green run? Suggest "pristine-passed marker file references HEAD's commit SHA exactly" — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes
- If pristine has not run green against the current HEAD, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence

Likely needs a small artifact written by rbw-tP on success — e.g., a .pristine-passed file (gitignored) carrying timestamp + commit SHA. /rbk-prep-release reads it; if SHA doesn't match HEAD, prep-release refuses.

Update README "Release Procedure" section to reflect that the prep-release step now enforces rbw-tP had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing.

Document the post-success cleanup ceremony — since the gauntlet suite omits teardown so operators can peek at final state, document the manual cleanup sequence (rbw-fA per vessel + rbw-arD + rbw-adD + rbw-dU) operators run when ready to release the canonical infrastructure. README and/or RELEASE.md is the placement decision at mount time.

- Recency contract — design call: most recent commit on main? Last 7 days? Tagged release marker on the green run? Suggest "pristine-passed marker file references HEAD's commit SHA exactly" — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes
- If pristine has not run green against the current HEAD, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence

Likely needs a small artifact written by `rbw-tP` on success — e.g., a `.pristine-passed` file (gitignored) carrying timestamp + commit SHA. `/rbk-prep-release` reads it; if SHA doesn't match HEAD, prep-release refuses.

Update README "Release Procedure" section (from Pace 1) to reflect that step 4 (`/rbk-prep-release`) now enforces step 3 (`rbw-tP`) had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing.

## Verification

- `/rbk-prep-release` fails fast when no pristine pass is recorded for current HEAD
- After successful `rbw-tP`, `/rbk-prep-release` proceeds normally
- After successful `rbw-tP` followed by an unrelated commit, `/rbk-prep-release` refuses (SHA mismatch)
- Pristine-passed artifact's gitignore status is correct (deliberately not committed)
- README accurately documents the precondition

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the rest of `/rbk-prep-release` (preserved as-is)
- Multi-pristine-run aggregation
- CI integration
- Recency policies beyond exact-HEAD-match (other policies left for future heats if needed)

- Apply other corrections, clarifications, and restructurings captured during the heat but deferred from those paces — sourced from BB heat commit messages (search for RELEASE.md references) and BBAAc walkthrough findings

### rbs0-sync-sweep-from-bb-changes (₢BBAAq) [complete]

**[260601-0647] complete**

## Character

Read-only **sizing audit**, not a repair pace. Produce a drift ledger comparing
BB-touched veiled specs against current code/behavior. The ledger sizes the
repair effort and gates whether repair finishes inside BB or becomes its own heat.
Deliberate read-and-compare; no spec edits in this pace.

## Wrap criterion

A drift ledger exists (in this pace's landing) with one row per candidate spec:
verdict (`in-sync` / `drifted-minor` / `drifted-major`) plus a one-line note on
what shifted. Every candidate carries an explicit verdict — silence is not a row.
Operator and agent read it together and decide the repair container.

## Candidate derivation

Candidates are veiled specs (`rbk/vov_veiled/*.adoc`, anchored by RBS0) implicated
by BB's changes. The heat's file-touch bitmap is the starting seed, but a touch is
not drift: a pace that changed code+spec together may have left the spec in-sync.
The audit's job is to distinguish already-synced from drifted, and to catch the
inverse — specs whose behavior shifted but which the responsible pace did NOT touch.

## Constraints

- Strictly read-only. No spec edits, no code edits in this pace.
- Scope is BB's changes, not a full RBS0 audit.
- Seeded row: RBSAJ JWT-SA probe semantic (see prior docket detail — propagation-
  absorbing retry replacing all-iterations-must-pass).

## Out of scope

- Performing any repair. Repair is a follow-on decision the ledger drives.

**[260428-1134] rough**

## Character

Reflective audit pace at the tail of BB. Walk the changes landed across BB's paces and determine which RBS0 (and adjacent veiled-spec) sections fell out of sync with code/behavior, then update them.

Cognitive posture: deliberate read-and-compare. Source of truth is the code/handbook surface as it stands at mount-time; RBS0 is being reconciled to it.

## Wrap criterion

RBS0 (and any directly-implicated sibling spec files under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) reflect the behavior shipped during BB. Each touched section either updated or explicitly judged still-correct. No silent drift left behind for the next heat.

## Constraints

- Read-only against code; spec edits only.
- Scope is BB's changes — not a full RBS0 audit.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring RBS0 beyond what BB's changes require.
- Spec touches outside the veiled-spec family unless directly implicated.

## Known sync points (running list — append as paces land)

- **`RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc`** (from BBAAc inline fix on the JWT-SA probe — IAM-policy-read propagation race absorption). The procedural spec describes the OLD semantic: "all iterations must pass; HTTP 403 → fatal access denied; HTTP 401 → fatal token rejected." New code semantic in `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`: bounded propagation-absorbing retry — first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries within `max_attempts × delay_ms` budget, all other codes (including 401) fall to a single fail-fast catch-all with body-summary diagnostic via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture`. Sections needing rewrite: §"Execute Probe Iterations" (rename concept to *attempts*; rewrite "Evaluate Response" sub-section to reflect 2xx-wins / 403-retry / else-fatal); §"Display Summary" ("succeeded after «I» attempt(s)" instead of "«N»/«N» iterations passed"); the top NOTE block (intent shift from sustained-access verification to propagation-window absorption + verification); §"Validate Input Parameters" (`ITERATION_COUNT` description shifts to "max attempts — first 2xx wins"; `DELAY_MS` to "between retries"). Companion call-site change: `rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe` defaults shifted to 60 × 3000ms (≈180s budget) for SA roles only; Payor probe defaults and semantic unchanged.

**[260428-1103] rough**

### access-probe-spec-sync (₢BBABQ) [complete]

**[260601-0652] complete**

## Character

Spec→code reconciliation for the two access-probe procedural specs. RBSAJ is a
structural rewrite; RBSAO is a small handler delta. Mechanical once the shipped
semantic is stated.

## Source of truth

`rbk/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` — `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe` (JWT SA) and the Payor OAuth
probe (`rbgv_payor_oauth_probe` / `zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once`).

## RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe — rewrite

Replace the old all-iterations-must-pass model (403→fatal, 401→fatal, "N/N
iterations passed") with the shipped propagation-absorbing bounded retry: first
2xx (200/206) wins and returns; HTTP 403 retries within the attempt-count budget
then dies "propagation budget exhausted" with a jq body summary; all other codes
(including 401) fall to one fail-fast catch-all with body summary. `--count` is
max-attempts (not iteration-count); `--delay-ms` is between retries. Rewrite the
Execute-Iterations / Evaluate-Response / Display-Summary sections and the top
NOTE (intent shifts from sustained-access verification to propagation-window
absorption). The 60×3000ms SA-role defaults are set at the tabtarget/dispatch
call-site, NOT in rbgv (rbgv defaults count=1/delay_ms=0) — describe accordingly.
Payor OAuth probe semantic is unchanged.

## RBSAO-access_oauth_probe — small fix

Spec carries a dedicated HTTP 404 ("Payor project not found") handler the code
lacks; code folds 404 into the generic catch-all. Reconcile spec to that.

## Wrap criterion

Both specs describe the shipped probe behavior. No reference to the removed
all-pass/iteration model or the phantom 404 handler remains.

**[260601-0645] rough**

### governor-sa-lifecycle-spec-sync (₢BBABR) [complete]

**[260601-0704] complete**

## Character

Spec→code reconciliation for the governor service-account lifecycle specs. One
conceptual correction (singleton-per-role → identity-parameterized) applied across
four invest/divest specs, plus a small roster fact-content fix in two.

## Source of truth

`rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh` (`rbgg_invest_*`, `zrbgg_divest_role`, `zrbgg_roster_role`),
zipper channel param1, governor tabtargets `tt/rbw-a{r,d}{I,D,r}*.sh`. The command
reference (folio = param1 per identity) confirms identity-parameterized is the
intended shipped model — this is doc reconciliation, not a design question.

## invest/divest — model correction (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSDD, RBSRD)

Specs describe a singleton-per-role operation: no argument, identity derived as
`<role>-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}`, and (divest) SA email read from the installed RBRA
`client_email` with the RBRA always removed. Shipped code is identity-parameterized:
required identity folio composing `<role>-<identity>`; divest synthesizes the email
from `<role>-<identity>@…` and removes the RBRA only on match. Reconcile all four.

## roster fact content (RBSDR, RBSRL)

Specs say the emitted fact value is "" (presence-as-fact); code writes the SA email
as the fact content, and file_root is the bare identity (not a composed
`<roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY»` path). Reconcile to code.

## Wrap criterion

Four lifecycle specs describe the identity-parameterized model; two roster specs
describe email-as-fact-content. No singleton-per-role or empty-value-fact language
remains.

## Out of scope

Whether parameterized vs singleton is the right design — settled; code + command
reference are authoritative.

### rbs0-scattered-constant-sync (₢BBABS) [complete]

**[260601-0725] complete**

## Character

Low-risk cleanup of small, independent spec drifts surfaced by the BB audit —
stale constant names, one-line cross-refs, one missing field, and one obsolete
RBS0 orchestration pattern. Each edit is local and independent; this is a
checklist pace.

## Items (source-of-truth code in parentheses)

- **RBSDL-depot_list**: fact-ext constants `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT*` → `RBCC_fact_ext_depot*`; emit step omits the per-cloud_prefix subdir (`<cloud_prefix>/<moniker>.depot`). (`rbgp_depot_list`)
- **RBSDI-depot_inscribe**: substitution `_RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX` → `_RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT`. (inscribe mirror script)
- **RBSIA-image_audit, RBSIR-image_rekon**: stale kindle prose "`RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX` + hallmarks"; actual tokens are `rbi_hm`/`rbi_rq`/`rbi_es` from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_*`. Display-text linked-terms already correct — only the inline prose claim is stale. (`rbgl_GarLayout.sh`, `rbgc_Constants.sh`)
- **RBSTB-trigger_build**: cites `RBGC_RUBRIC_TRIGGER_PREFIX`; confirm the live constant name and correct. (`rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`)
- **RBSNX-network_connect**: remove the compose `priority 1000/500` route-selection claim; sentry-side default-route override is load-bearing. (`rbob_compose.yml`, `rbjs_sentry.sh`)
- **RBSRV-RegimeVessel**: add the required `RBRV_RELIQUARY` field (Tool Image Reliquary group, 1–14 chars). (`rbrv_regime.sh`)
- **RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts**: the "Current Code vs Actual API Contract" discrepancy table is pre-convergence; code now uses 409 (not 412), requires non-empty etag, GET+v3 everywhere. (`rbgi_IAM.sh`)
- **RBS0 §rbtoe_depot_list_update**: replace the obsolete mechanism (Cloud-Billing-v1 enumerate + Mason-SA inspect, flat facts, payor_install invoker) with the shipped CRM-v3 projects:search displayName-anchor, cloud-prefix-namespaced facts, levy/unmake-only invokers. (`zrbgp_depot_list_update`)
- **RBS0 §rbgc_depot_project_infix**: fix the cross-ref mis-attributing the displayName-discriminator role to the project-ID infix (discriminator is `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX`).

## Out of scope

RBSRR, RBSAA, RBSAP — pre-existing drift independent of BB's changes; not swept here.

## Wrap criterion

Each listed item corrected against its source-of-truth code; the three
out-of-scope specs untouched.

### charge-folds-readiness-wait (₢BBAAt) [complete]

**[260511-1539] complete**

## Character

Convert post-charge readiness-wait from a theurge-side matcher into a
nameplate-borne fact, with the wait folded into the charge tabtarget itself.
After charge returns, the case is ready — theurge holds no readiness concern.

## Scope

- Add `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` to the nameplate regime
  (tadmor=0, srjcl/pluml=30 currently).
- Charge tabtarget consults the fact and waits internally before returning.
- Theurge: `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` matcher and post-charge wait block
  both removed.
- Three colluding pieces collapsed: bash const
  `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`, Rust mirror
  `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS`, Rust matcher
  `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay`.

## Wrap criterion

- Charge honors per-nameplate readiness delay; theurge has no readiness code.
- tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAs landed (fixture lifecycle is the right shape for this hand-off).

## Out of scope

- Service-readiness probe contract (poll port-open or curl health endpoint
  until truly ready). Bigger contract upgrade; blind-sleep-by-nameplate-fact
  is sufficient. Surface as itch.

## References

- Discovery clue: grep `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL` to find the locality the new field
  needs plumbing through — per-nameplate `.rbk/*/rbrn.env`, `rbrn_regime.sh`,
  `rbrn_cli.sh`, `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`, and runtime consumers.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — current post-charge wait block in theurge
  orchestration.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs::rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` —
  current matcher.
- `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — bash const definition.

After charge returns, the case is ready — theurge holds no readiness concern.

## Scope

- Add `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` to the nameplate regime
  (tadmor=0, srjcl/pluml=30 currently).
- Charge tabtarget consults the fact and waits internally before returning.
- Theurge: `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` matcher and post-charge wait block
  both removed.
- Three colluding pieces collapsed: bash const
  `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`, Rust mirror
  `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS`, Rust matcher
  `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay`.

## Wrap criterion

- Charge honors per-nameplate readiness delay; theurge has no readiness code.
- tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAs landed (fixture lifecycle is the right shape for this hand-off).

## Out of scope

- Service-readiness probe contract (poll port-open or curl health endpoint
  until truly ready). Bigger contract upgrade; blind-sleep-by-nameplate-fact
  is sufficient. Surface as itch.

## References

- Discovery clue: grep `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL` to find the locality the new field
  needs plumbing through — per-nameplate `.rbk/*/rbrn.env`, `rbrn_regime.sh`,
  `rbrn_cli.sh`, `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`, and runtime consumers.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — current post-charge wait block in theurge
  orchestration.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs::rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` —
  current matcher.
- `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — bash const definition.

### handbook-render-param1-coverage (₢BBAAw) [complete]

**[260513-1436] complete**

Drafted from ₢A-AAS in ₣A-.

## Character
Future follow-up to the handbook-render fixture landed in ₣A6 (₢A6AAt). Design decision pace — likely requires some discussion of how to represent per-colophon parameterization in Theurge's test fixtures before any code moves.

## Docket
The handbook-render fixture in ₣A6 exercises 15 of the 16 handbook colophons. The one skipped is `rbw-HWdw` (`RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE`), which uses the `param1` channel — it requires a WSL-target argument to run, and the fixture's uniform "invoke and check exit 0" pattern does not supply one.

Decide how the handbook-render fixture should handle this (and any future param1-channel handbooks). Candidate directions:

- **Per-colophon argument lookup**: extend the Rust-side handbook colophon registry with a `default_args` slice per entry. Most entries get `&[]`; `rbw-HWdw` gets a known-safe dummy. Keeps the case loop uniform, localizes the exception.
- **Dedicated subsection in the fixture**: a second section listing only param1 handbooks with their dummy values. Preserves the "one case per colophon" symmetry without threading argument tables through the uniform case.
- **Split fixture**: `handbook-render-simple` (no args) + `handbook-render-parameterized` (args explicit). Heavier and probably not worth it for one offender.

This pace picks a direction, implements it, and extends handbook-render coverage to `rbw-HWdw`.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAt (handbook-render-fixture) must be complete — this pace patches a known scope deferral from that work.

### Wrap criterion
- handbook-render fixture exercises all 16 handbook colophons (including `rbw-HWdw`)
- Pattern generalizes to any future param1 handbooks added via `rbz_zipper.sh`
- Test asserts the chosen dummy param1 value(s) produce a rendering exit (not an argument-missing exit)

### Notes
- Known exit 0 is not the only valid verdict here. The test's purpose is to catch display breakage in handbook rendering — the dummy argument must steer toward a rendered output path, not an early-exit-due-to-bad-arg path.
- `rbw-HWdw` is currently the only param1 handbook. If a second one lands before this pace runs, reassess whether an inline table is still the right shape.

### handbook-render-imprint-coverage (₢BBAAx) [abandoned]

**[260531-0746] abandoned**

Drafted from ₢A-AAT in ₣A-.

## Character
Imprint-channel sibling to ₢A-AAS. Design decision pace — the core question is how `buh_tt` and the handbook-render fixture should treat imprint-channel colophons when the handbook is host-agnostic. Likely needs discussion before code lands.

## Docket
`buh_tt` delegates to `buyy_tt_yawp`, which globs `${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}/${colophon}.*` when no imprint arg is supplied and picks the first alphabetical match. For imprint-channel colophons with multiple host variants, the first match is arbitrary.

### Concrete trigger
Surfaced during ₢A6AAn. In `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_top.sh:52`, the Windows handbook teaches Phase 3 with:
```bash
buh_tt "     Phase 2 (clone repos):              " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2}"
```
`JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is enrolled with channel `imprint`; the tabtargets are `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.cerebro.sh` and `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.localhost.sh`. The handbook is host-agnostic (it's about "a Windows host" in the abstract), so the glob picks `cerebro` — arbitrary and potentially misleading for a learner setting up a different host.

The prior hardcoded string was `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` — explicit about the placeholder. The `buh_tt` replacement (prescribed in ₢A6AAn's docket) loses that teaching signal in exchange for a clickable link.

### Why this isn't caught by the fixture today
`handbook-render` checks exit 0, not rendered content correctness. The Windows handbook rendering with `cerebro.sh` baked in is a green case — the fixture catches display *breakage* but not display *drift*.

### Candidate directions
- **Suffix annotation idiom**: document the standard `buh_tt ... "${FOO}" "" " (pick your host)"` pattern for imprint-channel colophons in host-agnostic handbooks. Cheapest; no primitive change. Accepts that the rendered link points at an arbitrary variant.
- **Multi-variant rendering in `buyy_tt_yawp`**: when called with no imprint on an imprint-channel colophon, render something like `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` (synthetic placeholder) instead of globbing. Needs a way to detect "colophon is imprint-channel" — likely requires `buz_exec_lookup` or a sibling query on the zipper registry.
- **Require explicit imprint**: make `buh_tt` on an imprint-channel colophon without an imprint arg a hard error. Forces every caller to make a choice. Breaking change; audit scope unclear without a grep sweep.
- **Companion fixture assertion**: extend `handbook-render` (coordinating with ₢A-AAS) to snapshot rendered content and diff against expected per-colophon output. Catches drift, not just breakage. Heavier but pairs naturally with the param1 coverage work.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAn complete — it produced the concrete trigger.
- Likely best sequenced after ₢A-AAS so the fixture's parameterization mechanism is known before deciding whether to leverage it for content-snapshot testing.

### Wrap criterion
- Decision recorded on imprint-channel handling in handbooks (one of the directions above, or another).
- If a primitive change: `buh_tt`/`buyy_tt_yawp` updated, call sites audited, fixture updated if behavior is observable.
- If an idiom only: documented in BCG or the handbook-building guide, and `rbhw0_top.sh:52` updated to apply it.
- Windows handbook (`rbw-hw`) renders in a way that doesn't silently commit a learner to the wrong host variant.

### Notes
- Currently `JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is the only known imprint-channel colophon used from a host-agnostic handbook. `JJZ_FUNDUS_SCENARIO` and `JJZ_FUNDUS_SINGLE` are also imprint-channel but aren't referenced from handbooks at time of slate.
- Pairs thematically with ₢A-AAS — both address the gap where channeled colophons meet uniform handbook/fixture shapes. May be worth groom-time discussion of whether to merge.

### rbtd-fixture-nomenclature-collapse (₢BBAAy) [complete]

**[260511-1557] complete**

## Character

Two-step nomenclature simplification in the rbtd fixture engine.
Mechanical refactor with one careful read of test-coverage impact.

## Goals

1. Eliminate "case name string" as a parallel surface to function name.
   Today `case!()` already sets name from `stringify!($func)`, so case
   names ARE function names — but operator-facing surfaces (commit
   prose, dockets, error messages) treat the kebab form as if it were
   a key. Make canonical = function name; either drop kebab from
   user-facing output entirely, or accept kebab as an alias resolver.

2. Eliminate `rbtdre_Section` abstraction. Replace with flat
   `cases: &[Case]` directly on `rbtdre_Fixture`. Fixture-level
   fail-fast vs keep-going (already controlled by disposition) is the
   only granularity actually wanted. Multi-section fixtures
   (enrollment-validation, regime-validation) absorb their grouping
   into case-name prefixes if helpful for display.

## Wrap criterion

- All sections collapsed; `rbtdre_Section` struct removed.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` inter-section coverage retired or repurposed
  (it currently exists to test the abstraction being removed).
- All fixtures still pass under their existing tabtargets.

## Out of scope

- Renaming individual case functions for prose readability.
- Changes to fixture lifecycle (setup/teardown), dispositions,
  or run/list tabtarget surfaces.
- Anything in rbtw or upstream of the engine API.

### single-case-imprint-collapse (₢BBAA1) [complete]

**[260511-1619] complete**

## Character

Tabtarget-surface refactor. Mechanical except for one judgment call:
the colophon name and channel.

## Goals

Replace the imprint-channel `rbtd-s.FixtureCase.<fixture>.sh [case]`
pattern with a single non-imprint, two-param colophon: param1 is the
fixture name, param2 is the rust test case function name (e.g.
`rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels`). The new tabtarget covers ALL
manifest-registered fixtures, not just crucible ones.

The tabtarget is self-documenting — invocation with missing or invalid
arguments fails with a usage listing instead of an opaque error:

- No params: list all valid param1 values (fixture names from the
  manifest); exit nonzero.
- Invalid param1: same — list all valid param1 values; exit nonzero.
- Valid param1, missing or invalid param2: list valid param2 values
  (case names registered for that fixture); exit nonzero.
- Both valid: run the case.

## Wrap criterion

- Single new tabtarget routes by fixture-name lookup against the
  rbtd manifest; rejects unknown fixture names.
- Helpful-failure UX: missing/invalid args produce a list of valid
  values for the offending position and a nonzero exit.
- Per-fixture FixtureCase.<fixture>.sh files retired.
- Crucible-fixture single-case semantics preserved: still operates on
  already-charged state, never charges/quenches.
- BUK channel discipline honored — the new colophon enrolls under the
  param1 channel, not imprint; zipper updated; CLAUDE.md tabtarget table
  reflects the change.
- Fast suite green; canonical onboarding-sequence cases unchanged.

## Out of scope

- FixtureRun (whole-fixture) tabtargets — unchanged.
- Charge/quench tabtargets — unchanged.
- Adding new fixtures or new manifest fields.

## References

- Tools/buk/buk-claude-context.md — channel semantics (imprint vs param1)
- BBAAz onboarding-sequence-buildout pattern — precedent for
  manifest-driven dispatch
- rbtd manifest: rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs

### http-helper-callsite-port (₢BBAA5) [complete]

**[260513-1420] complete**

## Character

Mechanical DRY port: replace inline curl-with-stderr-capture blocks across the codebase with calls to `rbgu_http_request` (helper landed in BBAAs, lives in `Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh`). Mount-time discovery — do not assume the surface is what it was when this was slated.

## Discovery recipe

At mount, scan the curl callsites:

```
grep -n 'curl' Tools/rbk/*.sh | grep -v 'rbgu_http_request\|--head\|rbgj'
```

For each hit, decide: compatible or not?

A callsite is **compatible** with the helper when its curl shape matches all of:
- Method-parameterized HTTP request (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Bearer-token auth passed via a separate token variable
- Accept and Content-Type headers (when present) are `application/json`
- Optional JSON body file via `-d @body`
- Caller wants response body, HTTP status code, and stderr captured to files (rather than discarded or piped)

A callsite is **incompatible** (leave inline; possibly sibling-helper in a future pass) when any of:
- HEAD-only existence probe (`--head`)
- Form-encoded body (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`)
- Runs in a different shell environment (e.g., alpine sh inside Cloud Build step scripts)
- Other Content-Type or unusual header set the helper doesn't speak

## Wrap criterion

- Every callsite found by the recipe has been classified compatible or incompatible
- Every compatible callsite calls the helper instead of inlining the curl flags
- No new inline curl block matching the compatible shape has appeared in the working tree
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` green; `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` baseline preserved
- At least one empirical run exercises a ported callsite end-to-end

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAs landed (helper available)

## Out of scope

- Tolerance/timeout tuning of any retry or polling loop
- New helper variants (form-encoded, HEAD-predicate, etc.) — those are separate paces if warranted
- Restructuring caller-side retry/policy logic — only the curl-flag block is portable; the loop around it stays as-is

### iam-grant-propagation-tolerance-share (₢BBABF) [complete]

**[260513-1434] complete**

## Character

Mechanical. Shape is locked; resist loop-skeleton extraction (bash higher-order is ugly — deferred).

## Source

`Memos/memo-20260513-iam-propagation-race-director-invest-gar.md` — fresh-depot `rbgg_invest_director` failed at AR `getIamPolicy` HTTP 403 because the governor's `roles/owner` grant had not yet reached the AR-resource-scope IAM cache. Existing `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` recognizes only class A/B (HTTP 400 + body match for "does not exist" / "is not deleted"). Class C — HTTP 403 because the *caller* is recently-empowered — has no rule today.

## Locked decisions

- **403 retry is time-bounded only — no body fingerprinting.** The bound itself is the discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. Same model already proven in `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe`.
- **Single IAM-grant budget profile.** Mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC` / `_MAX_DELAY_SEC` / `_DEADLINE_SEC` in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (values 3 / 20 / 420 per RBSCIP). Every IAM-grant retry reads from there — no more inline literals.
- **Predicate parameterized by per-site tolerance list.** `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` takes (code, body-glob) pairs; empty glob = match any body. Each grant site declares its own tolerance up front. Resource-scope sites add (403, ""). Other sites retain just the (400, "*does not exist*") / (400, "*is not deleted*") pair.
- **Loop skeleton stays per-site.** Each `rbgi_*` grant function keeps its own nested GET-modify-SET loop. JWT probe (180s budget) untouched — different cost-of-waiting profile, separate constant if standardized later.

## Scope

Code:
- `rbgc_Constants.sh` — mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_*` constants
- `rbgi_IAM.sh` — generalize predicate; thread constants through all grant fns; update each site's tolerance list (resource-scope sites add 403)
- `rbgg_Governor.sh` — apply same harness to inline GAR retry in `rbgg_invest_director`; add 403 tolerance

Specs (Tier A, load-bearing):
- `RBSCIP-IamPropagation.adoc` — document class C; replace inline 3/20/420 with constant references
- `RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc` — add 403 row to propagation-error catalog with time-bound rationale
- `RBSDK-director_invest.adoc` — mention 403 tolerance at AR step

Specs (Tier B, coherence cross-refs):
- `RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc`, `RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc`, `RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc`

Tier C drift (RBS0 mapping, RBSHR closure, RBSAJ cross-refs) deferred to BBAAq's running-list mechanism.

## Wrap criterion

Fresh-depot PayorMantle → InvestRetriever → InvestDirector succeeds in one shot, no divest-reinvest workaround. Spec and code coherent on class C across Tier A+B documents.

### fast-qualify-shellcheck-fold-cover-zero (₢BBABG) [complete]

**[260601-0820] complete**

## Character

Mechanical. Fold the two fast-codebase-check entry points into one tier, parallelize the slow one, expand its coverage, bring its baseline to zero, and wire it into marshal-zero as an early structural gate.

## Locked decisions

- **One fast-tier shellcheck entry.** `rbq_qualify_fast` absorbs `buq_shellcheck`; standalone `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` retires. The primitive function stays; only the standalone tabtarget retires. `rbw-tr` drops its now-redundant shellcheck phase.
- **Parallelize via xargs -P.** Replace the single shellcheck invocation with xargs-driven parallel invocation; aggregate exit code into one verdict. Target ~1–2s wall-clock for the file set (down from ~9s).
- **Coverage expansion.** `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh` joins the shellcheck file set. Mount-time call on scope-generalization shape (multi-root parameter vs explicit outside-Tools list).
- **Baseline zero.** Every current shellcheck finding either fixed in-place or moved to `busc_shellcheckrc` with BCG-structural rationale. Fast-tier exits zero on clean tree after wrap.
- **MZ early gate.** `rblm_zero` runs the parallelized shellcheck primitive in its pre-check block (alongside tree-clean / upstream / pushed). Non-zero shellcheck fails marshal-zero before it touches anything. Gauntlet entry-state cannot accept a shellcheck-dirty codebase by construction.

## Wrap criterion

`tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` exits zero, runs in ~2s wall-clock, covers `Tools/` plus `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh`. `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` no longer exists. `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` no longer makes a separate shellcheck call. `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` fails fast on any shellcheck finding before mutating anything.

## Out of scope

- `tt/*.sh` shellcheck coverage (`buq_tabtargets` shape-check is the appropriate gate for generated shims)
- `.buk/launcher.*.sh` (3–4 line trivial shims; capture as itch if ever produces a finding)
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/` subtree — operator call at mount (exempt vs fix)

### foundry-and-cloud-step-trust-audit (₢BBABN) [abandoned]

**[260531-0745] abandoned**

Drafted from ₢AUAA5 in ₣AU.

## Character

Research-heavy with judgment — produces a ranked findings document; no code changes in this pace. Parallels the prior `oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep` review method (₢A_AAN) but widened to the foundry surface, including the step scripts deployed INTO Cloud Build workers (a distinct trust boundary from the on-curia orchestrating bash). Remediation is explicitly deferred: each finding cluster that the operator elects to pursue gets its own follow-up pace, slated by the operator after reviewing the findings.

## Scope

### On-curia orchestrating bash (rbf* family)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` | Shared foundry primitives (~52 KB) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` | Director build orchestration — conjure/bind/graft/enshrine/inscribe dispatch (~72 KB, largest file in the family) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` | Hallmark ledger — abjure/tally/jettison/rekons/wrest (~36 KB) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh` | Retriever operations — summon, plumb (~12 KB) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` | Verify — vouch emit and consume (~39 KB) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfc_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`, `rbfl_cli.sh`, `rbfr_cli.sh`, `rbfv_cli.sh` | CLI dispatchers |

### Cloud Build step scripts (deployed into Cloud Build workers — distinct trust boundary)

These scripts run INSIDE Cloud Build workers with Cloud Build's identity, not the operator's identity. They receive parameters via `_RBG*` substitutions that the director renders into jq-composed build step bodies. They are a distinct trust surface from the orchestrating bash: quoting hazards land differently (step scripts run in a minimal container); error handling in-builder affects what exit codes the builder surfaces; step scripts see only what the director sends.

| Directory | Count | Role |
|-----------|------:|------|
| `Tools/rbk/rbgja/` | 4 | About assembly — discover-platforms.py, syft-per-platform.sh, build-info-per-platform.py, assemble-push-about.sh |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjb/` | 6 | Buildx — derive-tag-base, qemu-binfmt, buildx-push-image, per-platform-pullback, push-per-platform, push-diags |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgje/` | 1 | Enshrine — copy |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgji/` | 1 | Inscribe — mirror |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjm/` | 1 | Mirror-image |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/` | 3 | Vouch/verify — download-verifier, verify-provenance.py, assemble-push-vouch |

Three of these are Python (`rbgja01`, `rbgja03`, `rbgjv02`) — audited with the same trust-foment lens adapted to Python idioms.

## Method

Apply the same tiered trust-foment framework used in the OAuth review (₢A_AAN), adapted to foundry concerns:

### Tier 1 — visible-in-30-seconds signals

- Shellcheck cleanliness across all foundry bash and step-script bash
- `set -euo pipefail` presence and placement
- Quoting and expansion discipline (especially for parameters that flow into jq, curl, skopeo, docker buildx, oras, etc.)
- Python: lint (ruff/pyflakes if available), `shell=True` subprocess use, unvalidated input
- TODO/FIXME/XXX density, especially undated or in supply-chain-adjacent paths
- Dead code, commented-out blocks, unused constants

### Tier 2 — second-look signals

- **Error handling asymmetry across the rbf* family** — do rbfc, rbfd, rbfl, rbfr, rbfv fail the same way for the same class of problem?
- **Duplication across step scripts** — rbgjb03/rbgjb05 buildx paths, rbgja04/rbgjv03 assemble-push variants
- **Stale references after recent structural work** — with `₢A_AAB` RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX threading and `₢A_AAK` categorical layout changes landing in A_, check for drift, half-migrated paths, or comments that contradict current code
- **TOCTOU / race-adjacent patterns** — temp file creation, permission tightening order
- **Error-swallowing patterns** — `|| true` placements, subshell failure masking, pipefail gaps

### Tier 3 — foundry-specific trust surfaces

1. **Supply-chain chain of custody** — ordain → vouch → summon → plumb. What does each ark promise, and does the consumer actually verify what was promised? Specifically:
   - SLSA provenance: `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` is the verifier. Does the vouch artifact it checks against actually carry what the director claimed to emit?
   - Vouch gate: can a conjure/bind/graft succeed without a matching vouch? Does `rbfr` refuse to summon an un-vouched hallmark?
   - Cross-mode (conjure vs. bind vs. graft): each has different provenance guarantees. Does the mode-aware vouch logic in `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` correctly distinguish, or are there paths where a graft verdict can leak into a conjure-mode verification?

2. **Multi-platform build integrity** — buildx per-platform pullback, digest handling, and platform-specific SBOM/about emission. Does the per-platform pipeline cross-contaminate? Can a push-per-platform step silently push an image built for the wrong platform?

3. **Cloud Build step-script input validation** — step scripts receive `_RBG*` substitutions composed by the director via jq `--arg` and `--argjson`. jq quoting is correct by construction, but:
   - Any place the director uses raw string interpolation into a build YAML/JSON?
   - Any step script that interpolates its own environment into a shell command without quoting?
   - Any step script that accepts an argv parameter without validating its shape?

4. **Cloud Build posture verification** — the step container environment. What's in it? What can it reach? RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc is the stated posture; does step-script behavior match, or does any step script make assumptions incompatible with the stated posture (e.g., expecting network reachability that airgap pool denies)?

5. **Secret handling in step scripts** — step scripts should see only what they need. Check for:
   - Secret Manager refs correctly scoped per step
   - No secrets logged to Cloud Build logs (Google mirrors these; log leaks are persistent)
   - No secrets written to intermediate build artifacts (e.g., baked into image layers)
   - No secrets embedded in step-script source itself

6. **Container-registry write authority** — which step pushes to which path? Can a graft step push into a hallmark path meant for conjure? (The AAK categorical layout tightens this, but worth auditing once it lands.)

7. **Verifier/verified symmetry** — the vouch schema and the provenance-verify schema must stay in lockstep. If `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` emits field X and `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` checks field Y, divergence is a silent trust gap.

8. **Dependency pins inside step scripts** — step scripts pull tools (syft, skopeo, oras, cosign if present, docker buildx, qemu-binfmt, gcloud-within-builder). Are these pinned by digest? By tag? Not at all? The RBSRG (RegimeGcbPins) spec is the reference contract; does reality match?

9. **Failure modes visible to operator** — when a cloud build step fails, what does the operator see? Cryptic exit codes, clean structured errors, or a wall of unattributed output? This is a trust signal for the operator experience more than for an external auditor, but both matter.

10. **Python ↔ bash boundary** — `rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`, `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py`. These read bash-set environment, emit JSON to stdout or files, and signal failure via exit code. The boundary is where most cross-language bugs live. Check: JSON emission discipline (one doc per invocation, no stray stdout), exit-code fidelity, schema stability.

## Deliverable

A findings report written to the chat (not committed to disk unless operator requests) matching the format produced for ₢A_AAN:

- **"What builds trust here"** section — specific patterns worth preserving (setting the floor)
- **Tier 1 / 2 / 3 findings** — each numbered (F1, F2, …), each naming file:line, describing the trust-foment signal, and proposing a specific fix or clarification
- **Priority list** — findings ranked by trust-yield-per-line-of-diff so the operator can pick which to pursue first

The operator then reviews and elects which clusters to remediate. Each elected cluster becomes its own follow-up pace, either in AU (if pre-release scope) or in A_ (if structural enough to block depot regen). F-numbering is internal to this audit and does not carry across pace boundaries.

## Relation to other work

- **₢A_AAN** (oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep) already covers the OAuth surface (`rbgo_OAuth.sh`, `rbgp_Payor.sh` OAuth portion, `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`, `rbro_regime.sh`, `rbro_cli.sh`). This foundry audit does NOT revisit those files.
- **F7 follow-up** (factor three token-exchange code paths, still to be slated in A_) is also OAuth-scoped and not revisited here.
- **AAK / AAL / AAB** (GAR categorical layout and prefix threading in A_) are actively reshaping paths the foundry constructs. The audit acknowledges this: findings about path construction should be cross-referenced against AAK's helper-module intent (`rbgl_GarLayout.sh`) to avoid flagging things AAK already plans to fix. Ideally this audit runs AFTER AAK lands, so the audit reviews the post-migration state rather than transitional code.
- **Recent AU paces** touched `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (lanes S/A/J/K/F — build provenance, multiplatform, vouch preflight, digest extraction) and `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` (lane F). The audit reads the current state; it does not revisit decisions those paces made, only flags if the implementations embody trust-foment signals visible at the code surface.

## Out of scope

- Implementing any fixes (separate per-cluster paces — operator's decision)
- Auditing OAuth code (covered by ₢A_AAN and its forthcoming F7 sibling)
- Auditing non-foundry / non-step-script RBK code (governor, IAM, access-probe — separate audits if wanted)
- Auditing JJK, BUK, CMK, VVK — all out-of-kit scope
- Pen-testing the Cloud Build environment itself (that's an external-service posture question — see RBSCB; audit only compares script behavior to stated posture)
- Changing the stated Cloud Build posture (RBSCB is a design decision; audit observes, doesn't propose)
- Regenerating pinned digest references (RBSRG is a contract; audit checks adherence, doesn't rotate)

## Verification (for the audit pace itself)

This is a research pace; the "verification" is that the deliverable exists, names real file:line references, is internally consistent, and the operator reads it and either accepts it as-is or asks for clarifications before deciding on follow-ups.

- Every finding names `file:line` (verifiable by inspection)
- Every finding proposes a specific remediation OR explicitly marks "observation-only"
- Findings are ranked (not a flat list)
- Priority table clusters related findings so follow-up paces can be scoped cleanly
- "What builds trust here" section present — audits that only find faults foment their own kind of distrust

**[260527-1740] rough**

## Scope

### On-curia orchestrating bash (rbf* family)

| `Tools/rbk/rbfc_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`, `rbfl_cli.sh`, `rbfr_cli.sh`, `rbfv_cli.sh` | CLI dispatchers |

### Cloud Build step scripts (deployed into Cloud Build workers — distinct trust boundary)

These scripts run INSIDE Cloud Build workers with Cloud Build's identity, not the operator's identity. They receive parameters via `_RBG*` substitutions that the director renders into jq-composed build step bodies. They are a distinct trust surface from the orchestrating bash: quoting hazards land differently (step scripts run in a minimal container); error handling in-builder affects what exit codes the builder surfaces; step scripts see only what the director sends.

| Directory | Count | Role |
|-----------|------:|------|
| `Tools/rbk/rbgja/` | 4 | About assembly — discover-platforms.py, syft-per-platform.sh, build-info-per-platform.py, assemble-push-about.sh |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjb/` | 6 | Buildx — derive-tag-base, qemu-binfmt, buildx-push-image, per-platform-pullback, push-per-platform, push-diags |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgje/` | 1 | Enshrine — copy |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgji/` | 1 | Inscribe — mirror |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjm/` | 1 | Mirror-image |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/` | 3 | Vouch/verify — download-verifier, verify-provenance.py, assemble-push-vouch |

Three of these are Python (`rbgja01`, `rbgja03`, `rbgjv02`) — audited with the same trust-foment lens adapted to Python idioms.

## Method

Apply the same tiered trust-foment framework used in the OAuth review (₢A_AAN), adapted to foundry concerns:

### Tier 1 — visible-in-30-seconds signals

- Shellcheck cleanliness across all foundry bash and step-script bash
- `set -euo pipefail` presence and placement
- Quoting and expansion discipline (especially for parameters that flow into jq, curl, skopeo, docker buildx, oras, etc.)
- Python: lint (ruff/pyflakes if available), `shell=True` subprocess use, unvalidated input
- TODO/FIXME/XXX density, especially undated or in supply-chain-adjacent paths
- Dead code, commented-out blocks, unused constants

### Tier 2 — second-look signals

- **Error handling asymmetry across the rbf* family** — do rbfc, rbfd, rbfl, rbfr, rbfv fail the same way for the same class of problem?
- **Duplication across step scripts** — rbgjb03/rbgjb05 buildx paths, rbgja04/rbgjv03 assemble-push variants
- **Stale references after recent structural work** — with `₢A_AAB` RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX threading and `₢A_AAK` categorical layout changes landing in A_, check for drift, half-migrated paths, or comments that contradict current code
- **TOCTOU / race-adjacent patterns** — temp file creation, permission tightening order
- **Error-swallowing patterns** — `|| true` placements, subshell failure masking, pipefail gaps

### Tier 3 — foundry-specific trust surfaces

1. **Supply-chain chain of custody** — ordain → vouch → summon → plumb. What does each ark promise, and does the consumer actually verify what was promised? Specifically:
   - SLSA provenance: `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` is the verifier. Does the vouch artifact it checks against actually carry what the director claimed to emit?
   - Vouch gate: can a conjure/bind/graft succeed without a matching vouch? Does `rbfr` refuse to summon an un-vouched hallmark?
   - Cross-mode (conjure vs. bind vs. graft): each has different provenance guarantees. Does the mode-aware vouch logic in `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` correctly distinguish, or are there paths where a graft verdict can leak into a conjure-mode verification?

2. **Multi-platform build integrity** — buildx per-platform pullback, digest handling, and platform-specific SBOM/about emission. Does the per-platform pipeline cross-contaminate? Can a push-per-platform step silently push an image built for the wrong platform?

3. **Cloud Build step-script input validation** — step scripts receive `_RBG*` substitutions composed by the director via jq `--arg` and `--argjson`. jq quoting is correct by construction, but:
   - Any place the director uses raw string interpolation into a build YAML/JSON?
   - Any step script that interpolates its own environment into a shell command without quoting?
   - Any step script that accepts an argv parameter without validating its shape?

4. **Cloud Build posture verification** — the step container environment. What's in it? What can it reach? RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc is the stated posture; does step-script behavior match, or does any step script make assumptions incompatible with the stated posture (e.g., expecting network reachability that airgap pool denies)?

5. **Secret handling in step scripts** — step scripts should see only what they need. Check for:
   - Secret Manager refs correctly scoped per step
   - No secrets logged to Cloud Build logs (Google mirrors these; log leaks are persistent)
   - No secrets written to intermediate build artifacts (e.g., baked into image layers)
   - No secrets embedded in step-script source itself

6. **Container-registry write authority** — which step pushes to which path? Can a graft step push into a hallmark path meant for conjure? (The AAK categorical layout tightens this, but worth auditing once it lands.)

7. **Verifier/verified symmetry** — the vouch schema and the provenance-verify schema must stay in lockstep. If `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` emits field X and `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py` checks field Y, divergence is a silent trust gap.

8. **Dependency pins inside step scripts** — step scripts pull tools (syft, skopeo, oras, cosign if present, docker buildx, qemu-binfmt, gcloud-within-builder). Are these pinned by digest? By tag? Not at all? The RBSRG (RegimeGcbPins) spec is the reference contract; does reality match?

9. **Failure modes visible to operator** — when a cloud build step fails, what does the operator see? Cryptic exit codes, clean structured errors, or a wall of unattributed output? This is a trust signal for the operator experience more than for an external auditor, but both matter.

10. **Python ↔ bash boundary** — `rbgja01-discover-platforms.py`, `rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py`, `rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py`. These read bash-set environment, emit JSON to stdout or files, and signal failure via exit code. The boundary is where most cross-language bugs live. Check: JSON emission discipline (one doc per invocation, no stray stdout), exit-code fidelity, schema stability.

## Deliverable

A findings report written to the chat (not committed to disk unless operator requests) matching the format produced for ₢A_AAN:

- **"What builds trust here"** section — specific patterns worth preserving (setting the floor)
- **Tier 1 / 2 / 3 findings** — each numbered (F1, F2, …), each naming file:line, describing the trust-foment signal, and proposing a specific fix or clarification
- **Priority list** — findings ranked by trust-yield-per-line-of-diff so the operator can pick which to pursue first

The operator then reviews and elects which clusters to remediate. Each elected cluster becomes its own follow-up pace, either in AU (if pre-release scope) or in A_ (if structural enough to block depot regen). F-numbering is internal to this audit and does not carry across pace boundaries.

## Relation to other work

- **₢A_AAN** (oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep) already covers the OAuth surface (`rbgo_OAuth.sh`, `rbgp_Payor.sh` OAuth portion, `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`, `rbro_regime.sh`, `rbro_cli.sh`). This foundry audit does NOT revisit those files.
- **F7 follow-up** (factor three token-exchange code paths, still to be slated in A_) is also OAuth-scoped and not revisited here.
- **AAK / AAL / AAB** (GAR categorical layout and prefix threading in A_) are actively reshaping paths the foundry constructs. The audit acknowledges this: findings about path construction should be cross-referenced against AAK's helper-module intent (`rbgl_GarLayout.sh`) to avoid flagging things AAK already plans to fix. Ideally this audit runs AFTER AAK lands, so the audit reviews the post-migration state rather than transitional code.
- **Recent AU paces** touched `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (lanes S/A/J/K/F — build provenance, multiplatform, vouch preflight, digest extraction) and `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` (lane F). The audit reads the current state; it does not revisit decisions those paces made, only flags if the implementations embody trust-foment signals visible at the code surface.

## Out of scope

- Implementing any fixes (separate per-cluster paces — operator's decision)
- Auditing OAuth code (covered by ₢A_AAN and its forthcoming F7 sibling)
- Auditing non-foundry / non-step-script RBK code (governor, IAM, access-probe — separate audits if wanted)
- Auditing JJK, BUK, CMK, VVK — all out-of-kit scope
- Pen-testing the Cloud Build environment itself (that's an external-service posture question — see RBSCB; audit only compares script behavior to stated posture)
- Changing the stated Cloud Build posture (RBSCB is a design decision; audit observes, doesn't propose)
- Regenerating pinned digest references (RBSRG is a contract; audit checks adherence, doesn't rotate)

## Verification (for the audit pace itself)

This is a research pace; the "verification" is that the deliverable exists, names real file:line references, is internally consistent, and the operator reads it and either accepts it as-is or asks for clarifications before deciding on follow-ups.

- Every finding names `file:line` (verifiable by inspection)
- Every finding proposes a specific remediation OR explicitly marks "observation-only"
- Findings are ranked (not a flat list)
- Priority table clusters related findings so follow-up paces can be scoped cleanly
- "What builds trust here" section present — audits that only find faults foment their own kind of distrust

### rbw-tP-first-run (₢BBAAX) [abandoned]

**[260601-0531] abandoned**

## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property)

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property and for the pre-flight manifest's tincture line to be meaningful)

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

**[260428-0710] rough**

## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

### rbw-tP-branched-rerun (₢BBABA) [abandoned]

**[260601-0532] abandoned**

## Character

Validate BBAAX's repairs by re-running rbw-tP end-to-end on a branch named after this pace's coronet. Branch isolation lets parallel attempts on different machines avoid the commit-trail race on main; with BBABB landed, parallel mac+linux execution against a shared payor manor is now first-class — distinct BURS_TINCTURE values give every RBDC-derived name (depot project_id, GAR repo, GCS bucket, SA emails) per-station disjointness by construction.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally end-to-end. Same guardrail as BBAAX — partial completion is not pass.

## Cost

~90m wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt.

## Scope

- Create a branch named after this pace's coronet, off current main
- Run rbw-tP from marshal-zero on the branch
- Operator selects which machine the pace mounts on (mac or linux); pace does NOT use foray
- Triage surprises per BBAAX rules: tiny inline fix + re-run, larger → follow-up pace

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Locked decisions

- Branch name == pace coronet (identity-stable; no human-meaningful name)
- Branch lives until pace wraps; deletion is operator territory

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; the parallel-station property depends on it)

### ordain-poll-progress-heartbeat (₢BBABP) [complete]

**[260529-1740] complete**

## Character
Short, mechanical observability fix. One judgment call (where the emission belongs); the rest is rote.

## Goal
During the silent cloud-build poll window, the operator gets periodic console progress — answering "is it progressing?" — without spelunking rbfc_poll_response_*.json. Wanted live for dogfight, skirmish, gauntlet, any ordain-bearing fixture.

## The silence, two layers
- rbfc_FoundryCore.sh zrbfc_wait_build_completion emits per-poll status via buc_log_args — log-only, never console.
- The theurge engine rbtdre_run_cases emits only terminal PASSED/FAILED per case; the case closure is an opaque blocking call, so nothing surfaces while ordain waits on the build.

## The one judgment call
Confirm theurge captures/redirects the case closure's child stdout (rbfc's visible "Waiting for build completion" buc_step never reached the console during dogfight — strong evidence it does). If captured, the heartbeat belongs engine-side (periodic "running <case> (Ns elapsed)"), not in rbfc — surface progress without un-quieting all child output.

## Wrap criterion
Running dogfight shows periodic build-phase progress on the console during the poll wait.

## Out of scope
Broader theurge trace overhaul; per-step structured progress. Heartbeat only.

### 2026-06-01 08:30 - ₢BBABL - n

Add temporary clobber-proof forensic capture of token-endpoint mint failures (TEMP-FORENSIC BBABL) to close the memo's Host-B capture gap before writing the ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID touchpoint-2 match. Reproduce-first per operator: the OAuth token-endpoint body for ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID was never captured, so the consumer-retry error-class extension must be written against the real shape, not a guess. Append-only /tmp log survives a later successful mint overwriting the per-invocation response file. Removed once shape is confirmed.

### 2026-06-01 08:20 - ₢BBABG - W

Pulled shellcheck off the hot path into release-prep only. Mount-time reconciliation found two of five docketed decisions already satisfied (the two-entry-point fold; Tools/ baseline already zero) and the coverage target a phantom (.buk/rbbc_constants.sh does not exist; its real namesake Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh is already linted under Tools/). Investigation showed rbq_qualify_fast was wired as a pre-command gate on rbw-cC charge and rbw-fO ordain, so the shellcheck binary was a routine-dev dependency. Resolution (operator-chosen Path B): new rbq_qualify_shellcheck owns the buq_shellcheck call; rbq_qualify_fast no longer runs it, cutting the shellcheck dependency from fast-qualify and the charge/ordain gate; rbq_qualify_release calls it explicitly; rblm_zero gains a lint gate before any mutation so the pristine baseline cannot be shellcheck-dirty. Parallelism (xargs -P) dropped — non-POSIX and outside the allowed-command set, and pulling shellcheck off the hot path removed its only motivation. BCG table and rbtdru_cupel comment synced. Verified: rbw-tq green with no shellcheck step; Tools/ baseline exit 0; rbw-MZ loads and aborts safely at tree-clean. Real coverage gap (sentry/pentacle/MBS scripts carry findings, sentry is the security boundary) carried forward to ₢BBABU with a required jailer risk-assessment + tadmor re-test contract.

### 2026-06-01 08:20 - ₢BBABG - n

Move shellcheck out of the fast-qualify / charge-ordain hot path into release-prep only. New rbq_qualify_shellcheck function owns the buq_shellcheck call; rbq_qualify_fast no longer runs it (so the charge/ordain workbench gate and rbw-tq no longer require the shellcheck binary), and rbq_qualify_release calls it explicitly. Add a marshal-zero lint gate: rblm_zero shells out to rbq_cli.sh rbq_qualify_shellcheck after the tree/upstream/pushed prechecks and before any mutation, so the pristine baseline cannot be shellcheck-dirty by construction. No parallelism: xargs -P was dropped (non-POSIX, absent from the BCG/theurge allowed-command set) because pulling shellcheck off the hot path removed the only motivation for it. Sync the BCG primitives table and the rbtdru_cupel shellcheck-clean comment to the new placement.

Three docket divergences handled by matching actual code, not the docket's claim: (1) RBSTB cited RBGC_RUBRIC_TRIGGER_PREFIX which never shipped (lived only in retired rbw-layout docs) — dead citation removed, reworded to vessel-sigil convention; agent flagged a deeper unaddressed drift (spec describes triggers.list-then-match but code POSTs directly to ZRBFC_TRIGGERS_URL) left for operator call. (2) RBSCIG 'GET+v3/409 everywhere' is a per-API split — IAM-mixin paths converged on 409/non-empty-etag/v3 but bucket/Storage correctly uses 412 (If-Match family) and method varies GET vs POST; spec written to preserve the nuance. (3) RBS0 item-8 invoker confirmed levy/unmake-only (the corrected state). Out-of-scope RBSRR/RBSAA/RBSAP untouched. Two minor mint gaps surfaced and left for operator: RBRV_EGRESS_MODE lacks an RBS0 quoin; RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX referenced as literal not minted. Parallel fan-out (8 agents); two agents wrote RBS0-SpecTop.adoc concurrently in disjoint regions — verified no corruption post-run.

### 2026-06-01 07:04 - ₢BBABR - W

Spec→code reconciliation of the six governor SA lifecycle specs to shipped rbgg_Governor.sh. Four invest/divest specs moved from singleton-per-role to identity-parameterized (required identity folio composing <role>-<identity>; divest synthesizes email and removes the role RBRA only on client_email match). Two roster specs moved from empty-value presence-as-fact to email-as-fact-content (buf_write_fact_multi, bare-identity file_root, .email-regex filter). Sonnet did the reconciliation; opus reviewed against code and fixed an undefined «SA_EMAIL» loop variable in both roster specs. Three minor cross-spec uniformity items (invest email-domain expansion vs RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL constant; divest Display-Result email-naming overstatement; cosmetic step-title drift) surfaced and left for operator call. Wrap criterion met: no singleton-per-role or empty-fact language remains.

### 2026-06-01 07:04 - ₢BBABR - n

Reconcile the six governor SA lifecycle specs to shipped rbgg_Governor.sh. Invest/divest (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSDD, RBSRD) corrected from the singleton-per-role model (no arg, identity derived as <role>-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}, RBRA always removed on divest) to the identity-parameterized model the code ships: required identity folio composing <role>-<identity>; divest synthesizes the email as <role>-<identity>@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL} and removes the role RBRA only when its client_email matches. Roster (RBSDR, RBSRL) corrected from empty-value presence-as-fact to email-as-fact-content via buf_write_fact_multi with bare-identity file_root and full SA email as value; filter is by .email regex, not .accountId prefix. Review fix: both roster emit loops now bind «SA_EMAIL» as the loop variable rather than passing it as an unstored value. No singleton-per-role or empty-fact language remains.

### 2026-06-01 06:52 - ₢BBABQ - W

Reconciled the two access-probe procedural specs to shipped rbgv_AccessProbe.sh. RBSAJ rewritten from all-iterations-must-pass to propagation-absorbing bounded retry (first 2xx wins; 403 retries within attempt budget then 'propagation budget exhausted' with jq body summary; all other codes incl 401 to one fail-fast catch-all; --count as max-attempts, --delay_ms between retries; rbgv defaults count=1/delay=0 with 60x3000ms budget at the dispatch call-site; 5xx retry relocated to the call step; Display-Summary replaced by loop-exhaustion guard). RBSAO drops the phantom HTTP 404 handler. Wrap criterion met: both specs describe shipped behavior, no all-pass/iteration or phantom-404 references remain.

### 2026-06-01 06:52 - ₢BBABQ - n

Reconcile access-probe specs to shipped rbgv_AccessProbe.sh. RBSAJ rewritten from the all-iterations-must-pass model to the propagation-absorbing bounded retry: first 2xx wins and returns; 403 retries within the attempt budget then dies 'propagation budget exhausted' with a jq body summary; all other codes including 401 fall to one fail-fast catch-all. --count reframed as max-attempts, --delay_ms as between-retries; rbgv defaults count=1/delay=0 with the 60x3000ms SA-role budget set at the dispatch call-site; 5xx transient retry relocated to the call step; Display-Summary replaced by the terminal loop-exhaustion guard. RBSAO drops the phantom HTTP 404 handler the code lacks (404 folds into the generic catch-all).
