### 2026-05-31 13:44 - ₢BBABM - W

Release-ceremony repair + BCG command-lint, landed green. The design-laden Track 2 (cupel) was already underway at mount; this pace finished it and the surrounding tracks. Cupel: narrowed to release kit roots via opt-in allowlist; asymmetric two-pass (corpus-wide function visibility, kit-root linting); inventory mode; GCB domain made supply-chain-precise (curated container-tool allowlist, no declared-dep inheritance, no eviction free-pass) — catches the 'no jq in GCB' discipline by construction. Declared-deps ratified and synced across BCG + RBS0 + cupel const: +tar +stat +cargo +tee, -tcpdump (re-homed as a container/VM dependency since Scry runs it via exec/ssh, never host command-position). 62 kit-bash findings driven to 0: a real latent bug caught (buc_warning -> buc_warn in rbgb error branches); gdate/uname/cat/tail(x7 shared helper)/diff/cmp eliminated to builtins; full eviction table (wc/tr/grep/head/awk) rewritten incl. the dense bujb_jurisdiction caparison cluster; rbndb depot tripwire moved to openssl-digest byte-match; rbo.observe.sh deleted as dead (superseded by Scry). Ceremony: Step 2 deleted (cupel carries it), renumbered + cross-refs fixed, README.consumer/index.html/.nojekyll retirement cleanup, rbw-tf->rbw-tq fix. Verified: 137 unit tests, 131 fast-suite cases, 179-file shellcheck, fast qualify all green; RCG-clean (named consts, magic strings removed from tests). Foundry/caparison semantic validation rides this heat's later service/crucible + caparison runs, flagged in every commit. 14 commits.

### 2026-05-31 13:42 - ₢BBABM - n

Repair the rbk-prep-release ceremony (Tracks 1-5). Track 2: delete Step 2 (manual external-command audit) — now carried automatically by the cupel command-dependency lint riding rbw-tr's complete suite at the pre-strip qualification step; renumbered Steps 2-12 and fixed all internal cross-references (Step 10->9, 9->8, 8->7, 4->3) plus the 9a-9f subsection labels; added a knowledge-preserving note to the pre-strip qualification step naming cupel as the audit's successor. ### 2026-05-31 07:43 - ₢BBABM - n

Fix two cupel lexer false-positive classes and exclude ABANDONED dead code. (1) Array literals NAME=( ... ) were treated as subshell command groups, recording bare flag elements (-o, -U) as commands; now detected after an assignment word and skipped as data via a new balanced-paren helper. (2) Nested parens inside $(( ... )) / (( ... )) arithmetic terminated the skip at the first )), leaking the remainder (0xFFFFFFFF) as commands; the helper now balances paren depth and skips quoted segments. (3) Per operator decision, the corpus walk now skips any ABANDONED* directory — dead code is not held to the live command-dependency discipline. Four new unit tests; all 134 theurge tests pass.

### 2026-05-31 07:33 - ₢BBABM - n

Add case…esac state tracking to the cupel lexer. The first discovery run produced ~400 false positives because case-branch patterns (glob *, HTTP status codes, mode/enum values, getopts flags) sit at command position and were mis-recorded as commands. A three-state per-case frame stack (subject/pattern/body) now suppresses pattern tokens, treats `|` as alternation inside patterns rather than a pipe, and scans only branch-body command lists — handling nested case and commands following esac. Six unit tests pin the behavior. All 131 theurge tests pass; builds clean under deny(warnings).

### 2026-05-31 07:21 - ₢BBABM - n

Add cupel BCG command-dependency lint fixture to theurge. New rbtdru_cupel.rs implements a two-pass function-aware command-position lexer over all Tools/ bash: pass 1 unions every locally-defined function across the corpus, pass 2 flags each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, BCG POSIX floor, RBS0 declared deps}, failing-with-replacement on BCG's enforced eviction table. Partitions kit-bash (strict) from GCB-bash (any Tools/rbk/rbgj* path — looser, evictions tolerated, gcloud et al. allowed) by path prefix rather than a hardcoded dir list, covering all seven rbgj* job groups. Two Independent cases (kit, gcb). Registered in manifest (empty required-colophons), lib.rs, the fixtures registry, and the six suites carrying the fast fixtures (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish) — riding rbw-ts.fast for the dev loop and the complete suite for the rbw-tr release gate. rbtdtu_cupel.rs adds 27 unit tests pinning the lexer (heredoc/here-string/arithmetic/case/quote/comment handling), assignment + keyword classification, function collection, and the kit-vs-gcb classify polarity. ### 2026-05-14 11:42 - ₢BBABI - n

Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene library module, wire it as a precondition into both kludge (rbfk_kludge) and conjure (rbfd_build) paths, fold BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in rbfk_kludge, remediate the rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization, mint the RBSFH spec subdoc as the canonical rule home, and synchronize RBSAK Step 6 + RBSAC Step 1 + CLAUDE.md acronym mappings. Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. CLI furnishings: both rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh now source rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (inserted between rbgu_Utility.sh and the module-specific source) and invoke zrbfh_kindle in their kindle chains; minimal kindle has no constant or scratch-file dependencies, so the addition is purely additive ordering. Callsite wiring — rbfk_kludge.sh: rbfh_dockerfile_check invoked after the anchored-misses guard completes and before the hallmark mint, in a buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene" block at the natural conceptual position (post slot-resolution, pre docker-build). rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: identical pattern inserted immediately after the existing test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" validation in rbfd_build (line 1198 → 1200 gap), before buc_info "Building vessel image" — fail-fast before any GAR/credential/Cloud Build cost incurs on a malformed Dockerfile. BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge.sh (BBABH debt fold-in): (a) ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX readonly kindle constant defined as "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_" plus mutable z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0 mutable kindle state in zrbfk_kindle; (b) new zrbfk_image_present_predicate sentinel'd wrapper around docker image inspect that increments the counter, routes stderr to ${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${counter} forensic temp file, and returns docker's exit status — three previous "docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null" sites (anchored-miss check, origin-miss check, origin re-inspect after pull) all routed through the predicate; (c) ten multi-var local declarations from the slot-resolution loop split one-per-line (z_slot, z_origin_var, z_anchor_var, z_origin, z_anchor, z_slot_ref, z_pkg_path, z_tag, z_miss, all separate local statements); (d) two `test ${#array[@]} -gt 0` sites converted to `(( ${#array[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom for build_args presence + anchored_misses presence. Vessel remediation — rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile: replaced the hardcoded `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` with the standard pattern `# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)\nARG RBF_IMAGE_1\nFROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching rbev-busybox and common-sentry-context style — the rbrv.env already had RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm set so the build-arg propagation was always wired; the Dockerfile just ignored it. Empirically verified by surveying all 7 conjure-mode vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge-context, common-sentry-context, jupyter, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether): every one now uses ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} or AS coda variants. Also tidied rbrv.env: removed a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived AFTER the `# eof` marker — pre-existing cleanup matching the shape of working conjure vessels like rbev-bottle-ccyolo which omit the empty ANCHOR entirely. Spec subdoc RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc minted in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/: follows RBSDS-dns_step shape (//axhob_operation header with quoin reference, prose intro establishing scope across kludge and conjure callsites, three //axhos_step blocks one per rule), MCM-annotated, uses linked terms {rbtgo_ark_kludge}, {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, {rbbc_fatal}, {rbrv_image_origin}, {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} all verified present in mapping section. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc updated in two places: (1) mapping section gained a new "// Foundry Internals (build-side hygiene contracts shared across modes)" block with the single quoin `:rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene: <<rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene,Dockerfile Hygiene>>` immediately after the Script Internals block (line ~313); (2) new top-level section `== Foundry Internals` inserted between `== Script Internals` and `== Trade Studies` (line ~4120), containing the anchor block `[[rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene]]` + `//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal` annotation + `=== {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` header + 5-line intro + `include::RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc[]`. RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Step 6 (Build Image Locally) reworked: replaced BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM stages being a residual risk with a new leading bullet calling out the hygiene validation as the precondition ("Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch...") and reframed the no-network paragraph to credit {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} for closing the residual hardcoded-FROM risk that --pull=never used to backstop. RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc Step 1 (Validate Input Parameters) gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet — "vessel Dockerfile passes {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} — any column-0 FROM line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch as its image token; hardcoded literals abort before {gcb_service} submission". Departures from docket: minor only — (a) docket said "verify field name [RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN] against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing", verified empirically (ccyolo, busybox, etc.) and the jupyter rbrv.env already had the correct field set so the field-name verification step was a no-op (the bypass was purely in the Dockerfile); (b) the rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` line removal in jupyter rbrv.env was opportunistic cleanup not explicitly docketed but matches the shape of working conjure vessels and was clearly broken (positioned after `# eof`). The hygiene gap (no defense against hardcoded FROM bypassing RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization) is documented in the spec and chivvied as ₢BBABI for project-owned Dockerfile syntax constraint + bash hygiene check. ### 2026-05-13 21:28 - ₢BBABH - n

Drop --pull=never from kludge's docker build invocation. Surfaced empirically when post-decoupling test got past the director gate and finally exercised the build step against a real buildx-enabled docker daemon: buildx's --pull flag is boolean-only ('strconv.ParseBool: parsing never: invalid syntax'), there is no --pull=never analog in modern docker. The flag was inherited from the classic-builder era when it was the explicit no-network assertion at build time. Empirically, the original ₢BBABH docket framed --pull=never as a load-bearing 'tag-drift defense backstop' constraint, but that assumed classic docker semantics this environment no longer provides. The actual tag-drift defense lives in the presence guard above the build (every slot verified by docker image inspect — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent); buildx's default behavior is to not re-pull base images, so combined with the presence guard the build runs entirely against the local cache without networking. Code change: removed the --pull=never \ line from the docker build invocation in rbfk_kludge.sh, updated the two comment blocks that referenced the flag — the resolve-base-images preamble now says 'docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image' as the tag-drift defense, and the build-step comment says 'Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks'. Spec sync at RBSAK Step 6 (Build Image Locally): replaced the prescriptive --pull=never prose with descriptive prose explaining the no-network property as an emergent consequence of the presence check + buildx's default behavior; documented the lost capability explicitly — 'Hardcoded FROM stages that bypass RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization remain a residual risk; buildx no longer exposes a --pull=never analog, so explicit assertion of the no-network contract at build time is no longer available.' Departure from docket: the original constraint '--pull=never backstop on the build stays as tag-drift defense' is invalidated by the empirical-test discovery that the flag was already broken on the current docker environment — the constraint preserved a flag that produced an error rather than the asserted defense. The substantive defense (presence guard + buildx-default) is preserved; only the explicit flag drops. Verification: bash -n clean on rbfk_kludge.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 98/98 (enrollment-validation 47 + regime-validation 27 + regime-smoke 9 + handbook-render 15) — handbook-render renders the spec including the updated Step 6 prose. Empirical end-to-end re-run pending after this notch (kludge requires clean tree).

### 2026-05-13 21:24 - ₢BBABH - n

Decouple kludge from the director credential chain by factoring it into its own BCG module (rbfk). Empirical test of ₢BBABH's auto-pull behavior surfaced a parasitic coupling: rbob_cli's differential kindle for kludge commands fires zrbfd_kindle, which gates on RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE at module-load time even though rbfd_kludge itself never sources or uses the credential (the four `source RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` sites in rbfd live in enshrine/build/mirror/graft — all cloud-build, none reached by kludge). Worse, zrbfd_kindle transitively calls zrbfv_kindle (line 90) because rbfd_ordain needs rbfv for the post-build vouch step; kludge has zero rbfv references but inherited rbfv's identical director gate too. The spec (RBSAK Limitations) already documented this contract violation: 'foundry kindle validates RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE exists, though kludge does not use credentials' — load-bearing-complexity test fails, so the load-bearing complexity gets factored out. Mint: rbfk (k for Kludge, semantically resonant with the kludge hallmark prefix). Terminal exclusivity preserved — rbf family already non-terminal with siblings rbfc/rbfd/rbfl/rbfr/rbfv; k unused; rbfk_kludge.sh + rbfk_cli.sh follow standard BCG implementation+CLI pair. New module rbfk_kludge.sh sources only rbfc_FoundryCore.sh (for ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST/PATH registry constants + zrbfc_resolve_vessel/zrbfc_load_vessel helpers — kludge's only non-builtin non-bash dependencies); zrbfk_kindle is minimal (delegate zrbfc_kindle, mark ZRBFK_KINDLED=1); zrbfk_sentinel delegates to zrbfc_sentinel plus its own KINDLED check. The rbfk_kludge function body is the unchanged ₢BBABH auto-pull implementation (origin auto-pull inline with re-inspect sanity gate, anchored accumulates for fatal-after-loop refusal) — moved verbatim from rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1336-1489, with zrbfd_sentinel → zrbfk_sentinel as the only edit. New CLI rbfk_cli.sh mirrors rbfd_cli.sh's furnish source/kindle chain identically except (a) sources rbfk_kludge.sh instead of rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, and (b) calls zrbfk_kindle instead of zrbfd_kindle — so the rbfd kindle's director RBRA gate never fires for kludge invocations. The CLI's executable bit is set per BCG's _cli.sh-must-be-executable rule. Five updated call sites: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh — kludge function block deleted (header comment + body, ~153 lines), module description on line 19 narrowed from 'ordain, conjure, enshrine, kludge, mirror, graft' to 'ordain, conjure, enshrine, mirror, graft'; rbfd's director gate at lines 43-44 stays load-bearing for its remaining credentialed operations and is intentionally untouched. rbob_bottle.sh — both kludge delegation sites (rbob_kludge at line 642, rbob_kludge_sentry at line 668) call rbfk_kludge instead of rbfd_kludge; companion comment at :641 updated to match. rbob_cli.sh — added `source rbfk_kludge.sh` after the existing rbfd source (no removal — rbob_ordain still needs rbfd), and split the differential kindle case so kludge commands (rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry) call zrbfk_kindle while rbob_ordain keeps zrbfd_kindle. rbz_zipper.sh — RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL enrollment retargeted: module `rbfd_cli.sh` → `rbfk_cli.sh`, function `rbfd_kludge` → `rbfk_kludge` (rbw-fk tabtarget invocation unchanged from operator's view). RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Limitations bullet — replaced the now-obsolete parenthetical ('foundry kindle validates RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE exists, though kludge does not use credentials') with accurate framing: GCP depot configuration is needed to construct GAR-formatted image tags (registry host/project/repository), but no credentials are consumed and no registry is contacted for hallmark publication — only origin-slot upstream pulls reach a registry, unauthenticated. Architecture-wide outcome: rbfd has uniform credentialed contract (every function in rbfd consumes director credentials); rbfk has uniform uncredentialed contract (no director, no Cloud Build, no OAuth); no exceptional per-function gating within either module; the documented spec quirk goes away. BCG compliance: rbfk_kludge.sh has standard SOURCED multiple-inclusion guard, sources only rbfc per BCG's 'module sources nothing except own sub-dependencies' pattern (matches rbfd's pattern of sourcing rbfc + rbfv); kindle uses readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1 as last statement; sentinel checks ZRBFK_KINDLED='1'; function body preserves all braced+quoted expansions and explicit || buc_die discipline from ₢BBABH; no inline shellcheck directives. CLI has set -euo pipefail, sources only buc_command.sh at header, all other deps in furnish after buc_doc_env_done gate. Verification: bash -n clean on all seven touched files (rbfk_kludge.sh, rbfk_cli.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbob_bottle.sh, rbob_cli.sh, rbz_zipper.sh new contents); tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 98/98 (enrollment-validation 47 + regime-validation 27 + regime-smoke 9 + handbook-render 15) — handbook-render exercises asciidoctor on RBSAK including the updated Limitations bullet. Empirical end-to-end exercise: tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor invoked against a completely-purged docker cache (zero images, zero containers, zero build cache after the earlier `docker rm -f ... && docker rmi -f ... && docker system prune -a --volumes -f` sweep) with no director RBRA installed. First post-fix invocation got past the director-credential-gate failure mode (which was 'GCB service env file not found: ../station-files/secrets/director/rbra.env' before the structural split) — now reaches rbfk_kludge's dirty-tree guard, confirming the kindle chain no longer requires director credentials. The dirty-tree failure (this commit's own uncommitted state) is the expected next gate; once notched, the kludge proceeds into the auto-pull path that ₢BBABH constructed.

### 2026-05-13 14:34 - ₢BBABF - W

Closed silent semantic gap between IAM-grant propagation classes by recognizing a third class — caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM cache lag on a freshly-empowered governor) — alongside the existing two member-visibility classes (HTTP 400 "does not exist" / "is not deleted"). zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate generalized to accept per-site (code, body-glob) tolerance pairs with empty-glob short-circuit semantics; RBGC_PROPAGATION_{INITIAL_DELAY,MAX_DELAY,DEADLINE}_SEC minted as the single shared budget for every IAM-grant retry site (3/20/420 — empirical 7-min ceiling for Google's "typically <2 min" propagation); (403, "") tolerance added to four resource-scope rbgi_* functions (repo, SA, bucket, secret) plus the inline GAR retry in rbgg_invest_director. Project-scope rbgi_add_project_iam_role keeps only the two 400 patterns — governor's roles/owner reaches project-scope IAM caches effectively instantly, so 403 there would slow real-denial diagnosis without absorbing any known race. Class C uses time-bound discrimination rather than body fingerprinting: the bound IS the discriminator (proven model from rbgv_jwt_sa_probe). Tier A specs: RBSCIP three-class taxonomy + constant-referenced backoff profile; RBSCIG new propagation-error catalog table (code/glob/scope/discriminator) + class C prose with 2026-05-13 empirical evidence (fresh-depot AR repo 403 against governor with ~30s-old roles/owner grant); RBSDK AR-step NOTE on class C absorption. Tier B coherence: RBSGM/RBSDE/RBSRK each distinguish project-scope-instant from resource-scope-laggy IAM cache propagation. Per docket, loop-skeleton extraction deferred (bash higher-order is ugly); Tier C drift deferred to ₢BBAAq's running-list mechanism. BCG audit pass on touched code: five `local z_prop_deadline=` upgraded to `local -r`; inline `# shellcheck disable=SC2254` removed (already globally suppressed in busc_shellcheckrc:62); `local -ra z_tolerance=(...)` readonly; comments WHY-only. Verification: bash -n clean ×3, shellcheck delta 0, fast suite 98/98 — handbook-render exercises asciidoctor rendering of all six touched specs. The wrap criterion of one-shot fresh-depot PayorMantle → InvestRetriever → InvestDirector remains operator-verified at next gauntlet run (deferred per ₣BB paddock's fast-suite reservation against ₣A_'s parallel-period burn-in).

### 2026-05-13 14:34 - ₢BBABF - n

Close class C propagation gap surfaced by ₣A_'s fresh-depot run on canest2bhm100003 (memo-20260513-iam-propagation-race-director-invest-gar): rbgg_invest_director's AR getIamPolicy failed HTTP 403 against a freshly-empowered governor (~90s after roles/owner grant), where the same script's earlier project-scope and SA-scope IAM operations succeeded against the same token. Generalize zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate to take per-site (code, body-glob) tolerance pairs (empty glob = match-any-body, short-circuits without loading the error message), mint RBGC_PROPAGATION_{INITIAL_DELAY,MAX_DELAY,DEADLINE}_SEC=3/20/420 as the single shared budget for every IAM-grant retry site, and add (403, "") tolerance to the four resource-scope rbgi_* functions (repo, SA, bucket, secret) plus the inline GAR retry in rbgg_invest_director. Project-scope rbgi_add_project_iam_role keeps only the two 400 member-visibility pairs — the governor's roles/owner grant reaches project-scope IAM caches effectively instantly, so spurious 403 tolerance there would slow real-denial diagnosis without absorbing any known race. Class C uses time-bound discrimination rather than body fingerprinting (proven model from rbgv_jwt_sa_probe): the bound IS the discriminator — real propagation succeeds within RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC, real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. Tier A spec sync: RBSCIP gains a three-class taxonomy at the top (A forward member-visibility / B backward member-visibility / C caller-recently-empowered) and replaces inline 3/20/420 backoff numbers in the Backoff Profile section with RBGC_PROPAGATION_* constant references plus a load-bearing note that class C is time-bounded only and that the shared budget propagates by editing the constants once; RBSCIG gains a 'Propagation Error Catalog' table covering all three classes (code / body glob / scope applicability / discriminator) followed by prose explaining class C's empirical evidence (2026-05-13 fresh-depot AR repo 403 against governor with ~30s-old roles/owner grant, cumulative additional propagation ~30s) and the project-vs-resource scope discriminator; RBSDK's AR step gains a NOTE about resource-scope cache lag and class C absorption with reference to RBSCIG. Tier B coherence: RBSGM notes that downstream resource-scope ops absorb the roles/owner propagation lag via class C tolerance; RBSDE notes that the existing artifactregistry-API preflight poll does NOT gate per-repository resource-scope cache state (in-band absorption in director-invest does); RBSRK notes that its project-scope grant doesn't need class C tolerance, distinguishing it from director-invest even though both run shortly after governor mantle. Departures from docket: loop-skeleton extraction deferred per docket (bash higher-order is ugly); Tier C drift (RBS0 mapping, RBSHR closure, RBSAJ cross-refs) deferred to BBAAq's running-list mechanism. BCG audit pass on touched code: five `local z_prop_deadline=` upgraded to `local -r` (assigned-once locals get readonly per BCG); inline `# shellcheck disable=SC2254` removed (SC2254 already globally suppressed in busc_shellcheckrc:62); `local -ra z_tolerance=(...)` arrays declared readonly; comments WHY-only (tolerance composition rationale, scope distinction). Verification: bash -n clean on rbgc_Constants.sh / rbgi_IAM.sh / rbgg_Governor.sh; shellcheck delta zero; fast suite 98/98 (enrollment-validation 47, regime-validation 27, regime-smoke 9, handbook-render 15) — handbook-render exercises asciidoctor rendering of all six touched specs.

### 2026-05-08 10:52 - ₢BBABE - n

Fix shape A: sentry takes ownership of its default route. Cross-platform empirical work confirmed BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced a latent fragility — both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x ignore compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` for default-route selection, picking the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (= enclave). Pre-rename the special `services.networks.default` path made compose pick the right gateway by accident; BBABC removed that luck. Implementation: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink-interface discovery now computes the uplink gateway as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention; verified arithmetic for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Compute uses POSIX sh arithmetic (script is `#!/bin/sh`): IFS=.read into octets via heredoc, bitwise mask via `0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1<<host_bits)-1)`, gateway = network_address + 1. Spec deltas extend the existing don't-trust-Docker discipline (already in place for IP-based interface naming, RBSSS step 2) to default-route ownership: RBS0's at_sentry_container narrative gains a sentence pointing at the `priority`-not-honored finding; RBSSS step 2 retitled `Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing` with two new sub-bullets. Compose YAML cleanup: removed the broken `priority: 1000` / `priority: 500` keys (empirically inert); rewrote the misleading topology header that claimed `eth0=transit priority:1000` (eth0 is empirically enclave on both platforms — that comment was wrong since the IP-discovery requirement was added); new comment names the engine versions tested. Bottle vessel unchanged (no rebuild needed); sentry vessel needs re-kludge since rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image at build time per RBS0:2416. Excludes Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh which is uncommitted from another officium per multi-officium discipline.

### 2026-05-05 13:58 - Heat - n

Capture spook surfaced during BBAAX first-run: add 'TabTarget Invocation Discipline' section to buk-claude-context.md, parallel to the existing 'Forbidden Shell Operations' (cd) rule. Forbids wrapping tabtarget invocations with tee/tail/head/grep/2>&1/any pipe, since tabtargets self-log to ../logs-buk/ AND zsh defaults pipefail OFF (so a 'tabtarget | tail' pipeline returns tail's exit code of 0 even when the tabtarget failed — a failing test reports as success). Section names the three log files (last.txt, same-{cmd}.txt, hist-{cmd}-{ts}.txt), gives wrong/right examples, and provides escape hatch (set -o pipefail or PIPESTATUS) for rare cases that need live filtering. Heat-affiliated rather than pace-affiliated since the rule benefits future Claude sessions broadly, not just BBAAX. Spook caught during BBAAX first-run when the agent's 'rbw-tP 2>&1 | tee /tmp/log | tail -80' invocation reported exit 0 despite moriah fixture failing — the failure was correctly caught by set -e through rbte_suite → rbq_qualify_pristine → rbw-tP, but the bash pipeline ate the non-zero exit.

### 2026-05-05 11:39 - ₢BBAA_ - W

base64-to-openssl sweep in Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh garrison step5 — three raw base64 invocations converted to the openssl-standardized form per commit 385589f4. Encode site (line 480) became 'openssl enc -base64 -A < BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE' replacing 'base64 < BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE'; both heredoc decode sites (line 497 in the b|w branch and line 507 in the c branch) became 'echo .. | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > ztmp' replacing 'echo .. | base64 -d > ztmp'. -A flag is load-bearing on encode (suppresses 76-column line-wrap so the heredoc-embedded blob is a single line) and on decode (tolerates absence of newlines on single-line input). Existing line-486 newline-strip becomes belt-and-suspenders rather than load-bearing under -A, left in place per docket scope. Function recap for future reference: garrison step5 plants the workload SSH privkey on the remote via an admin SSH heredoc; raw PEM cannot be embedded inline because of multi-line content + cross-shell quoting drift, so the local side base64-flattens it to a single-line [A-Za-z0-9+/=] blob, embeds in heredoc, and remote decodes back to byte-exact PEM into a tmp file before install/cp to the target path with workload ownership and 0600 mode. Acceptance signal is the live garrison ceremony's step6 SSH knock — round-trip byte corruption fails SSH key parse and the knock returns permission-denied, so no fixture was needed. Discovery sweep across Tools/buk and Tools/rbk surfaced 17 hits; 13 already openssl-form or comments/error-strings, 3 raw invocations (all in bujb_jurisdiction.sh garrison step5), 1 edge case in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228 missing -A flag (operator decision: skip — FUTURE/ is out of live tree). Validation: bash -n clean, shellcheck -S error clean, post-sweep recheck recipe surfaced only the descriptive error-message string at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1063 ('Failed to decode buildStepOutputs base64') which is prose not an invocation. Mid-pace observation: heat A- had a structural gap independent of BBAA_ — AAm builds fenestrate + garrison verbs and AAD/E/F practice environment install + fundus + Docker, but no pace explicitly walked buw-jpF + buw-jpG{c,w} against a real Windows node. Slated practice-jurisdiction-windows (₢A-AAr) between AAD and AAE to close that gap; the new pace incidentally validates the BBAA_ change via the garrison step6 knock, but its primary purpose is closing the implicit-dependency gap in the heat sequence.

### 2026-05-05 11:22 - ₢BBAA_ - n

base64-to-openssl sweep — convert the three raw base64 invocations in bujb_jurisdiction.sh garrison step5 to the openssl-standardized form per commit 385589f4. Encode site (line 480) becomes 'openssl enc -base64 -A < BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE' replacing 'base64 < BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE'; the two heredoc decode sites (line 497 in the b|w branch and line 507 in the c branch) become 'echo .. | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > ztmp' replacing 'echo .. | base64 -d > ztmp'. Function recap: garrison step5 plants the workload SSH privkey on the remote via an admin SSH heredoc; raw PEM cannot be embedded inline (multi-line, cross-shell quoting drift), so the local side base64-flattens it to a single-line [A-Za-z0-9+/=] blob, embeds it in the heredoc, and the remote decodes back to byte-exact PEM into a tmp file before install/cp to the target path with workload ownership and 0600 mode. The -A flag is load-bearing: it suppresses openssl's default 76-column line-wrap on encode (so the heredoc-embedded blob is a single line) and tolerates absence of newlines on decode (so single-line input works). Existing line-486 newline-strip (z_key_b64 //$'\n'/) becomes belt-and-suspenders rather than load-bearing under -A; left in place per docket scope (no incidental cleanup). Acceptance is the live garrison ceremony's step6 SSH knock — round-trip byte corruption fails SSH key parse and the knock returns permission-denied, so no fixture is needed. Discovery sweep across Tools/buk and Tools/rbk surfaced 17 hits; 13 already openssl-form or comments/error-strings, 3 raw invocations (all in bujb_jurisdiction.sh, all garrison step5), 1 edge case in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228 missing -A flag (operator decision: skip — FUTURE/ is out of live tree). Validation: bash -n clean, shellcheck -S error clean, post-sweep recheck recipe surfaces only the descriptive error-message string at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1063 ('Failed to decode buildStepOutputs base64') which is prose not an invocation.

Pool-stem derived from validated displayName + project_id (cloud_prefix extracted by trimming infix+moniker from project_id), so worker pool deletion works against arbitrary leftover canonicals, not just live RBRR. Tinder lift: 'd-' literal lifted to RBGC_depot_project_infix per BCG tinder discipline (top-of-module, lower_snake, pure string literal, no runtime dep) — first tinder in rbgc_Constants.sh; rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation now references the tinder. Cascading dependency: rbdc kindle requires the tinder at source-time, so four CLIs that source rbdc but not rbgc (rbrv_cli, rbrr_cli, rbro_cli, rbra_cli) now source rbgc_Constants.sh before rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh; three test scripts in rbtdrf_fast.rs (the rbtdrf_run_one_validation_test helper, rv_rbrv_all_vessels, and the vessel-discovery script in rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) get the same source addition. Zipper enrollment for RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT switches channel from '' to 'param1'; tabtarget rbw-dU stays single-file (no imprint variant); rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md regenerated via rbw-MG to reflect the channel change. RBS0 mints :rbgc_depot_project_infix: as the first rbgc_* linked term, BCG-cased (lower_snake suffix matches the tinder constant). ### 2026-05-04 16:03 - ₢BBAAX - n

Insert rbgu_poll_until_ok probe before LIST-keys preflight in zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key. Second BBAAX gauntlet attempt failed at rbtdrk_retriever_invest with HTTP 404 'Service account does not exist' on the LIST keys preflight (rbgg_Governor.sh:196-202), immediately after a successful SA create returning a uid. The post-create rbgu_poll_until_ok at line 193-194 confirmed the SA was visible at the by-email GET endpoint, but the keys subresource has its own eventual-consistency lag relative to the SA itself — already known and mitigated for keys.create at line 240-243 with retry-on-404, but the LIST-keys preflight one step earlier was single-shot. Fix is the two-call shape: rbgu_poll_until_ok against the keys subpath URL absorbs the propagation delay, then the existing rbgu_http_json + rbgu_http_require_ok pair captures the canonical response into ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS for downstream USER_MANAGED counting at line 209-210. New ZRBGG_INFIX_KEYS_PROBE='keys_probe' constant added next to ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS to avoid temp-file basename collision with ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY (which the by-email probe at line 194 consumes). Single-point-of-fix covers both invest paths — rbgg_invest_retriever and rbgg_invest_director both route through zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key. Operator question 'use existing helper or bespoke amendment' answered: rbgu_poll_until_ok is the right helper, already used parallel-shape at line 194 for the SA-by-email probe; bespoke retry-on-404 at keys.create is necessarily inline because writes can't safely poll-until-OK (idempotency would mint N keys on retry), but reads are exactly the helper's contract. Asymmetry observed: rbfc_FoundryCore.sh poll loop tolerates curl rc=28 timeouts with 3-consecutive policy, rbgu_poll_until_ok tolerates GET propagation 404s, but rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh wrest's docker login is single-shot — the broader transient-tolerance survey is slated as ₢BBAA8 docker-login-retry-shape for design conversation per operator's pre-implementation-discussion constraint. This commit is a tactical inline application of the existing helper to close one specific call-site asymmetry, not a structural change. shellcheck -S error clean. Validation deferred to clean-chat BBAAX retry per operator instruction.

### 2026-05-04 14:50 - ₢BBAA7 - n

Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean.

### 2026-05-04 14:34 - ₢BBAA7 - n

Fix step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle to match the actual rekon contract for abjured hallmarks. Initial assertion expected rekon to succeed (exit 0) and report all five ark basenames as not-yes. Live run surfaced the real contract: rbfl_rekon_hallmark calls buc_die with 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>' (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:616) when the hallmark's package subtree is absent from GAR, exiting non-zero. This is the cleaner contract — abjure either removes the entire registry entry or it didn't run; there's no 'partial state' for rekon to enumerate. Step 6 now positively asserts that exit-non-zero shape: invoke rekon, expect exit_code != 0 AND stderr contains 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>'. Three failure paths surfaced explicitly (succeeded when should have died, died with wrong message, invocation failure). Combines with step 7's audit-list-restored assertion as defense-in-depth: rekon checks the package-level lookup, audit checks the registry-level enumeration, both must confirm absence. Producer string is hardcoded with a comment naming rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh as the source of truth; future drift breaks this test, which is intentional coupling.

### 2026-05-04 14:25 - ₢BBAA7 - n

Replace stdout-parsing in hallmark-lifecycle with the fact-multi machine-contract path. The first live run of the fixture surfaced that rbw-iah's stdout includes BUW pre-banner, ANSI-coded buc_step logging, and table chrome alongside the actual hallmark rows; parsing that display stream is fragile and a code smell against the established BBAAO fact-multi foundation already used by SA roster (rbgg_Governor.sh) and depot enumeration (rbgp_Payor.sh). Producer side: rbfl_audit_hallmarks now calls buf_write_fact_multi inside its dedup-check block, emitting one <hallmark>.audit-hallmark file per discovered hallmark to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem-as-data-bus). The display printf stays unchanged — fact emission is purely additive. RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark='audit-hallmark' added to rbcc_Constants.sh's fact-extension tinder registry alongside RBCC_fact_ext_depot, RBCC_fact_ext_roster_*, etc. Consumer side: rbtdri_invocation.rs gains rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi(result, extension) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> — enumerates burv_output/current/ for files matching *.<extension>, returns sorted basename-without-extension list. rbtdrc_hallmark_lifecycle's three audit calls (baseline, post-ordain, final) now read structured facts via this enumerator instead of parsing display stdout. zrbtdrc_audit_parse and zrbtdrc_is_hallmark_id helpers deleted (no longer needed). Constant RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK mirrors the bash side. Architectural alignment: audit-hallmarks is now machine-consumable for any future tool, not just this test — same shape SA roster already publishes per-identity. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean. shellcheck clean on rbcc_Constants.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh.

### 2026-05-04 14:02 - ₢BBAA7 - n

Switch rbev-busybox from airgap to tether egress mode. The vessel was misconfigured as airgap with empty RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR — an inconsistent state where ordain has no source for the base image. The new airgap enshrine-preflight (added earlier in this pace) correctly fired on this and refused to ordain. Previous four-mode tests worked against the vessel only because the prior preflight silently skipped empty anchors, leaving the runtime build to fail or succeed depending on stale enshrinement state in GAR. tether mode is the correct shape for this vessel's role: a small fast cloud-built hallmark used by hallmark-lifecycle (ordain/abjure round-trip coverage) and batch-vouch (vouch state-planting). Neither consumer cares about egress mode; tether lets buildx fetch busybox:latest from Docker Hub at build time without enshrine prerequisite. Airgap-mode coverage stays in onboarding-sequence/ordain_airgap (rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap, properly enshrined via the airgap chain that lands in this pace's absorbed onboarding tails).

### 2026-05-03 15:12 - ₢BBAAX - n

First-run inline fix for ₢BBAAX gauntlet observability gap: emit depot project ID and Cloud Build console URL via buc_info + buc_link immediately after rbgu_http_json_lro_ok succeeds creating the depot project in rbgp_depot_levy. Operator (and agent reading the live gauntlet output stream) needs the depot's Cloud Build console URL to peek at in-flight cloud builds during the long onboarding-sequence fixture; previously the URL was only emitted per-build via the existing buc_link calls in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1021 and :1257 (after each builds.create response), leaving long quiet stretches between builds where the URL was not visible in the recent stream. Reconstructing the project ID by hand from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER yields the wrong identifier — RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID at rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:84 injects a 'd-' infix between the prefix and moniker (canc- + d- + canest2100004 = canc-d-canest2100004), and the agent missed this during BBAAX, sending the operator to a nonexistent project ID that produced the GCP 'You need additional access' error against canc-canest2100004 instead. Fix uses RBGC_CONSOLE_URL (already defined at rbgc_Constants.sh:78 as https://console.cloud.google.com/) suffixed with cloud-build/builds?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}, matching the buc_link signature already established in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ('Click to ', 'Open depot Cloud Build console', URL). Did not promote ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE from rbfc to rbgc — the constant lives in foundry-private namespace appropriately, and rbgp's URL composition pattern at depot-levy time is short enough to inline. Preceded the buc_link with a buc_info echoing RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID itself so the project ID is grep-able from the output stream even if the link rendering is consumed by terminal styling. Insertion point is between the LRO completion and the billing-link step — the project exists in GCP at this point, all subsequent depot-levy steps continue against it, so the URL is meaningful from this moment forward. shellcheck -S error clean.

### 2026-05-03 14:03 - ₢BBAAX - n

Add Mid-execution posture rule to JJK agent context, sourced from rules-of-engagement conversation during BBAAX gauntlet first-run. Captures recurring failure mode where mid-pace surprises trigger option-proliferation responses (multi-option triage menus, scope/cost weighing, slate-vs-inline frame, follow-up-pace recommendations) that consume operator context with segmentation work that's not the agent's territory. Rule prescribes mechanism + one specific repair as default response shape; explicit prohibition on proliferating options or weighing consequences across alternatives; reaffirms that pace scope and segmentation are operator territory. Placed after Paddock posture in the agent-behavior framing cluster (Sincerity / Docket posture / Slate-time / Stale-by-mount / Docket anti-patterns / Reference discipline / Paddock posture), before Identities vs Display Names where the structural vocabulary begins. Located in jjk-claude-context.md rather than top-level CLAUDE.md because the failure mode is JJK-vocabulary-induced — outside a mount the pace-language doesn't load and the segmentation temptation doesn't surface; killing the temptation at the JJK context level is cleaner than blacklisting symptoms in a general directive.

### 2026-05-03 13:51 - ₢BBAAX - n

First-run inline fix for pristine-lifecycle/rbtdrp_depot_tear_down: zrbgp_depot_state_emit's classification path was reading state directly from CRM v3 projects:search hits, which Google documents as eventually consistent and lags mutations — notably DELETE_REQUESTED transitions post-projects.delete. After a successful rbgp_depot_unmake (which polls projects.get up to 60s waiting for DELETE_REQUESTED at the strongly-consistent endpoint), a subsequent rbgp_depot_list call observed the unmade project still as state=ACTIVE in search and emitted COMPLETE in the fact file, failing the fixture's verify. Reshape: search remains for discovery (display-name-anchored project enumeration), but per-hit state classification now issues a one-shot GET against ${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${projectId} — the same URL pattern rbgp_depot_unmake itself uses for verify-deletion at line 1061. Reordered loop body so projectId/displayName/moniker validation runs before the GET (skip the HTTP round trip on non-domain projects). Three new per-iteration locals (z_get_url, z_get_infix, z_get_code) declared in the BCG exception-2 block. 200 -> classify on returned .state; 404 -> skip iteration silently (project completed deletion between search and GET, fixture's absent-fact-file path treats as success); other -> log and skip, mirroring existing unrecognized-state handling. No new helper minted: surveyed rbgu_poll_until_ok (wait-for-availability), rbgu_http_json_ok (die-on-non-2xx), rbgu_http_require_ok-with-warn-code (rbgg_Governor.sh:746-750 precedent for 4xx tolerance) — none was the right shape for one-shot-read-with-skip-on-404, and only one current call site needs this composition. Inlined three primitives (rbgu_http_json + rbgu_http_code_capture + rbgu_json_field_capture) at the use site, modeled on the codebase's established posture of inlining tolerant 4xx handling rather than abstracting it. shellcheck -S error clean. Cost: 1 search call + N GET calls per list invocation (was 1 search call); for typical 4-10 depots this adds 4-10 HTTP round trips — acceptable for dev/test scale infrastructure.

RCG named-string repair on the prior notch's BURE_CONFIRM bypass introduction. Operator flagged the 6 sites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs that read `&[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` as RCG-violating magic strings (per Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md the codebase requires named string constants for env-var keys and protocol values). Prior precedent already existed: rbtdrp_pristine.rs:106-107 had module-private `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` + `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` consumed at line 1030 — same primitive, but module-prefixed and not shared. Adding a second module's worth of magic strings would have created a duplicate-constants smell across rbtdrp_/rbtdrc_, so consolidated by extraction: defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` and `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` in rbtdri_invocation.rs (the natural home: rbtdri_invoke_global is the primitive that propagates env-pairs to tabtargets, and rbtdri_invocation.rs already exports RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR and RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY using the same pattern). Updated rbtdrc_crucible.rs (6 sites) and rbtdrp_pristine.rs (1 site at line 1025; was line 1030 before the dead-constant deletion) to import and use the new shared constants. Removed the rbtdrp_-private duplicates (4 lines including the docstring) since the shared rbtdri_ versions now serve as the single source of truth across the rbtd crate. Verification: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (0.51s, no warnings); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/this-officium-prior-notch baseline; grep `\"BURE_CONFIRM\"\|\"skip\"` confirms no remaining magic-string sites of these values in src/. Out of scope this notch (would expand the patch surface beyond what the operator review identified): broader audit of other env-var literals in rbtd/src/ such as RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY-pattern strings used elsewhere, and possible pull-up of rbtdri_invocation.rs's BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR alongside other dispatch-side conventions (BURD_BUK_DIR, BURD_TOOLS_DIR keys consumed by spawned tabtargets) into a unified env-key constants table. Discovery recipe for residual magic strings: `grep -rn '\\"[A-Z_]\\+\\"' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/ | grep -v '//\\|test_'` to enumerate uppercase string literals that look like env keys or protocol tokens, then evaluate per RCG whether each warrants extraction — deferred since today's empirical loop is the active priority.

Two orthogonal fixes for the BBAAs onboarding-sequence empirical loop, landed together. (A) Three-line nudge in rbob_bottle.sh: at the three internal callsites (rbob_kludge:503, rbob_kludge_sentry:534, rbob_ordain:565) replace `rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"` with `BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_*_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge` (and the ordain analog). Drops the now-dead `local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_*_VESSEL}"` plus its `test -d` guard at each site (existence check moves into rbfd's own `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, single source of truth for vessel resolution diagnostics). Solves kludge-tadmor blocker observed in today's empirical run: trace `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh: line 185: rbrv_list_capture: command not found` was symptom of `zrbfc_resolve_vessel(BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor")` falling through to listing branch because `tadmor` is the nameplate moniker (inherited from rbw-cKS dispatch into rbob_cli.sh process), not a vessel sigil — rbfd_kludge expected sigil/path. Bash assignment-prefix `VAR=value func` is the language's primitive for scoped env override and is symmetric with the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec` (buz_zipper.sh:280) — same primitive at finer scope. Preserves BBAAs's universal-BUZ_FOLIO-contract design intent (handlers read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly across all rb*_, jjfp_, buhw_, rbgg_ modules; cli.sh furnishes also read BUZ_FOLIO for kindle); the rbob → rbfd internal calls just needed to honor the contract by setting BUZ_FOLIO to rbob's natural-derivative (RBRN_*_VESSEL sigil) for the inner call. After this patch, BUZ_FOLIO usage is uniform: dispatcher writes once via exec, callers may scope-override via assignment-prefix, all consumers read. Zero handler/dispatcher edits needed. (B) Strip --force flag from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure plus their RBSIJ/RBSAA specs and 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs — origin trace: docket revision 260128-2116 for ₢APAAT (silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`) introduced 'Confirm before deletion or require --force flag' as freelance docket text never operator-authorized; subsequent reslating preserved it; alignment commit 525b7fac (`:A:` Feb 9 2026) pulled `(add confirmation/force)` into the spec; bd344f92 (Haiku sub-agent, same day) implemented z_force/z_skip_confirm/--force doc-param mechanically; 388dd454 carried it through the rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh extraction; 7167b795 (BBAAs) included `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because of --force. Removed: `local z_force="${1:-}"`, `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`, `local z_skip_confirm=false` + `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch, and the `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` — `buc_require` runs unconditionally (single coherent path). The codebase already had a non-load-bearing-correct skip mechanism (`BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env var checked at buc_require:335-338, buc_command.sh) — --force was a redundant duplicate variant. Updated all 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 2603, 2688, 2720, 2820, 2948, 3003) from `&[&hallmark, "--force"], &[]` to `&[&hallmark], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` — fixtures continue to auto-confirm via the existing env-var bypass; --force literal disappears from the entire rbk tree (verified via grep). Spec edits: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:51-59 collapses 'Unless --force flag is provided' nested-step into unconditional 'Confirm Jettison' step; RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc:84-92 same shape. Verification: bash -n green on rbob_bottle.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile, 1.76s, no Rust warnings); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh green (tabtargets, RBW colophon registrations, generated context freshness all OK); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing) — no new failures introduced. Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Deferred follow-ups (separate notches when relevant): (i) source rbrv_regime.sh in zrbob_furnish to make rbrv_list_capture available in failure-listing branches reached from rbob processes (currently latent, surfaces only on resolution-failure paths and obscures real diagnostics); (ii) audit other Rust orchestrator/handbook callers passing positional args to BUZ_FOLIO-reading handlers — discovery recipe `grep -rn "rbtdri_invoke_global\|rbtdri_invoke_global_imprint" Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/` cross-referenced against zipper channel column for any handler with internal bash callers in rb*.sh modules. Caveat: scoped to the empirical failure trace and the unauthorized-flag scrub; if any handler outside this bug class has a similar internal-caller mismatch, will surface on the next empirical run as a new failure to diagnose.

### 2026-05-02 13:06 - ₢BBAAs - n

Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

### 2026-05-02 07:48 - ₢BBAAs - n

fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.

### 2026-05-01 10:56 - ₢BBAAs - n

BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch.

### 2026-05-01 09:57 - ₢BBAAs - n

fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. Verification: bash -n green on both edited files; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat in clean pace remount.

New step script Tools/rbk/rbgjr/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh runs on the alpine reliquary tool image with busybox-wget HEAD-checks against 5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo); alpine implicitly self-validated by Cloud Build pulling it. All four entry points carry uniform _RBGR_GAR_HOST/_RBGR_GAR_PATH/_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT/_RBGR_RELIQUARY substitutions plus three _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n slots; conjure populates the locator slots from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR, the others pass empty strings. ### 2026-04-30 08:25 - ₢BBAAs - n

enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). Block comment + docstring updated to remove skip-if-set framing. Commit-message string in source: removed coronet prefix from new commit string per directive (coronets should not appear in source). Pre-existing coronet-prefixed commit strings elsewhere in this file (BBAA0/BBAAu) are left as-is; broader sweep is follow-up work. Spec/handbook drift remains: RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc still describes skip-if-set classifier; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor linked-term descriptions still describe skip-if-set semantics; rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env head comment still says 'enshrine HEAD-validates'. These are documentation drift, deferred to a follow-up notch per operator pause. BCG audit on edited bash: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, one declaration per line, mutable locals for synthesized values (Exception 2), no bash 4+ features, no heredocs, no inline shellcheck. Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; theurge build green (rbtdro_onboarding.rs compiles); empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount. This notch parallels f365fe89's revert (03a2a17a) — net effect from BBAA0 baseline: BBAA0's enshrine-overload pattern reverted, orchestration's pin-validation step removed, spec/comments drift TBD.

### 2026-04-30 08:19 - ₢BBAAs - n

BBAAs revert f365fe89 (enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate). The four-way classifier patch added more branching to a switch that exists only because BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be both 'mirror upstream + write back' (forge case) and 'HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark' (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch is wrong-direction; the right move is to remove the overload. Operator-driven reframing: the classifier's HEAD-200 'skip' path is not an optimization — it actively breaks enshrine's contract. Enshrine's job is to compute the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream; HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest forever, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's commit 14242011 records empirically: 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac when upstream digest moved). The next notch implements the simplification: drop the entire classifier, make rbfd_enshrine always submit the CB job, remove the orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call (the hallmark-pin's existence is established by ordain-forge's success, not by a separate validation step), partial-revert BBAA0 spec prose accordingly. This notch restores rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh and RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc to their pre-f365fe89 state via git revert --no-commit. bash -n green; theurge build green (no Rust changes).

### 2026-04-30 08:08 - ₢BBAAs - n

BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated. Surfaced empirically in BBAAs onboarding-sequence run on a fresh canonical-established depot — the committed forge ANCHOR rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac was depot-stale (no enshrinement in the new GAR), and the classifier fatal-died before the regenerable CB path could run. Fix branches the 404 case on ORIGIN: with ORIGIN set, write 'enshrine' classification (extract step overwrites the stale value with the freshly-computed locator); with ORIGIN empty, keep the existing fatal die. Pipeline downstream of the classifier needs no changes — submit already passes ORIGIN substitutions only for to-enshrine slots, and extract already overwrites existing ANCHOR lines via in-place line replacement. Spec sweep: RBSAE head NOTE rewritten to describe the four-way semantics (skip / regenerate / fatal-pin / fresh-enshrine / empty); Classify Slots step expanded from 'pre-populated vs to-enshrine' to the explicit 404+ORIGIN-set branch; Extract step's 'pre-populated slots not touched' updated to 'skip slots not touched, stale-cache reclassifications written through'. BCG audit: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, mutable locals for per-iteration synthesized values (Exception 2), comment justifying non-obvious WHY (asymmetric ORIGIN-set vs ORIGIN-empty handling on 404), continue inside for loop, no new bash 4+ features, no heredocs. Verification: bash -n green, theurge build green, 0/0 unit tests pass, fast suite 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA0/BBAA2/BBAAz baseline). Empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount.

### 2026-04-29 11:31 - ₢BBAAs - n

Narrow the prior commit's enforce-skip list to drop rbob_ordain. The split that made my prior arm too wide: ordain has TWO entry points and they live in different CLIs. rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain is vessel-keyed (foundry-direct), goes through rbfd_cli.sh, never loads a nameplate, never runs zrbrn_enforce — and it is the entry point onboarding-sequence actually invokes (RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ORDAIN = "rbw-fO" at rbtdrm_manifest.rs:30). rbw-tO → rbob_ordain is nameplate-keyed (Theurge OrdainCycle, imprint channel), goes through rbob_cli.sh, IS the gate where zrbrn_enforce earns its keep — it is the natural pre-cloud-spend regime-validity check on a nameplate-affiliated build. Putting rbob_ordain into the skip list achieved nothing for the immediate failure (the failing path is rbw-cKS → rbob_kludge_sentry, not any rbob_ordain) and silently weakened the rbw-tO validation surface. Final skip list: rbob_kludge, rbob_kludge_bottle, rbob_kludge_sentry — all three are nameplate-keyed kludge entry points that write RBRN_*_HALLMARK and self-veto on marshal-zero blank-by-design state. rbob_kludge stays in the list because it is reachable two ways: directly via rbw-tK (Theurge KludgeCycle) and indirectly via rbob_kludge_bottle's body (rbob_bottle.sh:481-484 is just zrbob_sentinel; rbob_kludge); ${z_command} is set in furnish from the zipper-registered name for the dispatched tabtarget, so all three matter. The differential foundry-kindle block at the tail of zrbob_furnish keeps rbob_ordain — ordain still needs zrbfd_kindle to perform the Cloud Build; only the enforce dispatch drops it. Build syntax-check green (bash -n); 91/91 rbtd unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged from BBAAs; regime-smoke skipped under suite-level fail-fast as expected).
