### 2026-04-28 11:34 - ₢BBAAc - n

JWT SA access-probe absorbs IAM-policy-read propagation race via outer-loop semantic flip — under fresh marshal-zero, retriever_invest's post-grant probe failed iteration 1 with HTTP 403 (`Permission 'artifactregistry.packages.list' denied on resource ... (or it may not exist).`) ~14s after both IAM bindings were granted; gcloud verified the bindings landed in the project IAM policy minutes later, so the 403 was the post-grant policy-replication window, not a real denial. Body shape (`IAM_PERMISSION_DENIED` + `(or it may not exist)`) is identical for race and real-denial cases, so the discriminator is bounded budget — same principle as BBAAp's consumer-side OAuth retry, applied one layer up at the resource API call. Producer-side gating won't work here (BBAAp's lesson: producer's verifying read != next consumer's read under Google's eventual consistency), so absorption lands on the consumer path inside `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe`. Outer loop semantic flips: was 'N successful samples in a row, every iteration must pass' (stability sampling); now 'up to N attempts, first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries with delay, anything else dies with body summary'. `zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once` becomes pure observation — renamed `_capture`, prints HTTP code via stdout, populates the response/code/stderr files, no in-function policy decisions, buc_dies only on infrastructure failures (curl, file I/O). Verdict logic moves into the outer loop where loop context lives. New `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture(resp_file, stderr_file)` helper extracts `.error.message` for both die paths (budget-exhausted on 403, unexpected-HTTP on else); helper captures jq stderr to the paired stderr file (no `2>/dev/null` suppression — fixes the BCG violation that was burying parse failures). The `401|403)` lump is gone; 401 now falls naturally into `*)` and gets the rich body-summary diagnostic that real auth anomalies deserve, instead of the bare `(HTTP 401)` the lump produced. Param locals on `zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture` and `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe` upgraded `local` → `local -r` per BCG default; `iteration` vocabulary renamed to `attempt` to reflect the new contract; defensive `buc_die` on loop-exit-without-verdict guards against zero-count and other invalid configurations. Call-site tuning in `rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe` per role: governor/director/retriever get 60 attempts × 3000ms (≈180s propagation budget — generous upper bound for IAM replication on fresh projects, exits early on first 2xx); payor branch defaults stay at 5 × 1500ms with a comment noting its semantic is unchanged (different race, different domain). The `local z_iterations`/`local z_delay_ms` are now `local -r` and live inside their case branches so the two branches' values can't accidentally cross-contaminate. Stack composes with BBAAp: layer 1 (`rbgo_get_token_capture`) absorbs the JWT-signature mint race with its own 90s budget; layer 2 (`rbgv_jwt_sa_probe`) absorbs the IAM-policy-read race with its own 180s budget; each layer recognizes only its own 'not yet' signature. Stability sampling is intentionally lost — once the AR endpoint returns 2xx in a region from a JWT-bearer'd request, the binding is durable from that client; flap-after-success isn't a real mode in the lifecycle the probe inhabits. Spec sync deferred to BBAAq per docket discipline; sync-point note appended to BBAAq's docket naming RBSAJ sections needing rewrite (Execute Probe Iterations / Display Summary / top NOTE / Validate Input Parameters) and the companion rbte_engine call-site change. Verification: bash -n clean on rbgv_AccessProbe.sh and rbte_engine.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Live re-run from the same fresh marshal-zero is the next operator gesture (folds into BBAAc's continuing walkthrough). Out of scope per the surprise-discriminated principle: Payor probe BCG nits (same `2>/dev/null` jq, same `401|403)` lump) are surfaced-as-itch — not load-bearing for this race; touching them adds risk for no observed benefit, capture if it bites.

OAuth token-mint retry on JWT-propagation race — shifted absorption from producer-side probe to consumer-side retry inside rbgo_get_token_capture. The post-write race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA key now manifests at every RBRA consumer (not just SA-creation) as `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature.`; surfaced by BBAAc walkthrough where canonical-establish's retriever_invest case failed with that exact signature ~zero seconds after governor_mantle's BBAAn probe succeeded — same race, different consumer. rbgo_get_token_capture (rbgo_OAuth.sh:181) rewritten with discriminated retry: jq-parses ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE for `error == invalid_grant` AND `error_description == "Invalid JWT Signature."`; only that signature retries (2s initial, 2x growth, 15s cap, 90s budget locked per pristine-tier contract); any other error shape returns 1 immediately (fail-fast preserved). JWT rebuilt each attempt (refreshes iat/exp). On timeout: buc_warn + return 1 (cf. prior buc_die in probe) — failure propagates through caller's existing `|| return 1` chain naturally. Body captured to ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE before timeout for forensic inspection. Progress logging via buc_step on each retry attempt — operator visibility during retry window, no silent 90s pause. rbgo_probe_jwt_bearer_propagation deleted (45 lines); both call sites deleted (rbgg_Governor.sh:294 in zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key, rbgp_Payor.sh:1398 in rbgp_governor_mantle). Three RBGC constants renamed RBGC_SA_KEY_PROBE_* → RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_* (paired-constant family alongside RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_*); ZRBGO_PROBE_STDERR_FILE renamed ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE (now stores response JSON for jq parsing, not stderr — different role). Constants comment block reframed around consumer-side absorption. Verification: bash -n clean on all four touched files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Wrap-criterion items 1-5 code-complete; item 6 (live canonical-establish from fresh marshal-zero) is operator-driven post-landing, folds into BBAAc's retry path. No spec doc touches required (no behavioral shift visible at procedural-spec level, per docket out-of-scope). Fix CRM v3 project displayName HTTP 400 surfaced by canonical-establish run from marshal-zero. RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON' → 'RBGC-DEPOT': underscores violate CRM v3 displayName allowed-character set (letters/digits/hyphen/space/single-quote/double-quote/exclamation per Google docs), and the resulting 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON canest2100000' was 31 chars exceeding the 30-char displayName ceiling. Either alone produces 'Request contains an invalid argument'. Single-constant fix flows to all five usage sites (depot project create, Mason SA create, Governor SA create, search anchor query, anchor-strip in moniker derivation) — load-bearing constant pattern earned. Mason/Governor SA displayNames go through IAM API (100-char limit, underscores allowed) so they wouldn't have failed; only the CRM v3 depot-project-create path surfaces the constraint. Updated the inline comment in zrbgp_depot_state_emit to reflect the new search query string. Specs reference the constant by name (RBSDE/RBSGM/RBSDL all use «RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX» placeholder) — no spec updates required. Verification: bash -n clean on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Surprise captured per pristine-walkthrough wrap criterion: silent first-build assumption (BBAAm threaded the constant without exercising the actual CRM v3 create path), inline-fixed because single-constant scope, no follow-up pace required.

### 2026-04-28 07:17 - ₢BBAAR - n

Segment 2 of BBAAR handbook cult-verb sweep: rbhogw_governor_wrapper.sh step 1 producer-output rewrite. New prose (5 lines) names this flow: 'mantle command writes the key file to its output directory and prints two cp commands — one for production install, one for assay (test) mode. Run the production line to place the file at the path derived from your RBRR configuration.' Decisions: (1) Mentioned both cp options so the operator isn't surprised by two printed lines — narrative primes recognition; (2) Did not re-yelp RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR in inline prose — already rendered via buh_tt two lines earlier, 'mantle command' is a clear referent and re-yelping adds visual noise; (3) Kept ${RBYC_RBRR} interpolation — the operator opens that file, the link is useful; (4) Dropped 'check RBRR_SECRETS_DIR for the location' — no longer needed since mantle prints the literal cp line. BCG-clean: pure text within buh_line, RBYC kindle constant interpolated directly per yawp-discipline §891, no structural changes. The six BBAAZ specs are not the only inline-style NOTE sites in the corpus (gold-standard RBSDE itself has L51 `{rbtgo_depot_unmake}` mid-line in NOTE prose), but those are wrapped in backticks treating them as inline literals; the conversion target is the linked-term-as-prose-anchor pattern, not all `{...}` occurrences. Per-file scope as planned: RBSDK (drift 4 only — opener + 2 step-internal NOTEs), RBSRK (drifts 1+4 — opener + step 3 reshape), RBSDD (drifts 2+3+4 — opener + step 4 + completion), RBSRD (drifts 2+3+4 — opener + step 4 + completion), RBSDR (drift 4 only — opener), RBSRL (drift 4 only — opener). All repairs are pure .adoc — no code, no test, no behavioral change beyond what already lives in the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts and BBAAY's depot-identity-collapse vocabulary. Per-file changes: (RBSDK-director_invest) opener converted from prose-style to NOTE: block matching RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard shape, with {rbtr_governor} as actor and the singleton-per-role + fatal-on-existing semantics re-stated; the bare //axhos_step 'Write Credential to Assay Output Slot' folded into a //axhoot_typed_output {rbtgi_rbra_file} block to restore the typed-output declaration that the orphan had and the Stage-2 rewrite had inadvertently dropped — one //axhoot_typed_output block now exists, anchored on the RBRA file as the operation's typed output. (RBSRK-retriever_invest) same NOTE-block conversion + same //axhoot_typed_output restoration; PLUS dropped a stray //axhoq_precondition block (regime-kindled assertion) that was non-load-bearing and not present in any sibling spec — the regime-kindled assumption is universal for governor-authenticated operations and doesn't earn a callout. (RBSDD-director_divest) opener converted to NOTE: block AND fixed the wrong-actor bug at line 5 — the prose said 'The {rbtr_director} divests the {rbtr_director}...' which was self-divest semantics; corrected to {rbtr_governor} as the actor since divest is governor-authenticated per BBAAQ contract. Replaced «DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» placeholder + 2-line 'derived as RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), consistent with {rbtgi_depot_project}' parenthetical with the inline {rbtgi_depot_project} linked term, matching RBSRD's endpoint form. Step 3 heading aligned to cult-verb level: 'Divest Service Account' → 'Divest Director Service Account' (RBSRD parallels with 'Divest Retriever Service Account'). Dropped the redundant post-DELETE {rbbc_require} 'preceding DELETE succeeded (HTTP 200 or 404)' since the prior step's gate already established that contract. (RBSRD-retriever_divest) step 3 heading 'Delete Retriever Service Account' → 'Divest Retriever Service Account' — cult-verb is divest, REST API verb is DELETE; step heading should match the cult-verb level. (RBSDR-director_roster) opener converted to NOTE: block with the defined plural attribute {giam_service_account_s} (was English-suffix plural). Dropped the empty-result early-exit 'No director SAs found / Return success' branch — non-load-bearing scaffolding since the presence-as-fact convention naturally degrades to zero emissions and the final 'Report count' step shows that. The {buf_write_fact_multi} call description rewritten from ambiguous 'with file root, extension constant ..., and basename «IDENTITY»' to explicit three-positional-argument form: <file_root> = <roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY», <ext> = RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director (resolves at source time to roster-director), <value> = '' (empty content per RBS0:1081 presence-as-fact). //axhoc_completion replaced with parallel template matching RBSRL's. (RBSRL-retriever_roster) line 4 plural attribute: {giam_service_account}s → {giam_service_account_s}. The {buf_write_fact_multi} call description had a contract-violating shape — said 'value «IDENTITY»' which would put identity into file content, contradicting RBS0:1119's 'fact content is empty, presence-as-fact'. Rewritten to explicit three-positional-argument form with empty value, mirroring RBSDR. //axhoc_completion replaced with the parallel template ('Successful execution rosters {rbtr_<role>} service accounts via per-SA fact emission — one `<ext-value>` fact file per discovered identity. The operation is read-only; no service accounts are created or modified.'). (RBS0-SpecTop) two sites updated from English-suffix plural to defined plural attribute: line 1086 (rbtgo_director_roster brief) and line 1121 (rbtgo_retriever_roster brief), {giam_service_account}s → {giam_service_account_s} — the mapping at RBS0:200 defines :giam_service_account_s: as the plural form. Inline follow-up after the seven parallel agents returned: surfaced during review that the linked term {rbtoe_rbra_remove} appeared in RBSDD:44 and RBSRD:42 but was not defined in the RBS0 mapping section (rbtoe_* operation entries at RBS0:155-217 do not include rbra_remove). Since the existence of a centralized rbgu_* helper for RBRA file removal is not confirmed in the codebase, replaced both linked-term references with inline prose ('Remove the role's RBRA file at {rbrr_<role>_rbra_file} from disk' / 'to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse') rather than minting a dangling linked-term that points at no anchor or implementation. The previous {rbbc_fatal} on file-removal failure (RBSDD) and {rbbc_require} success-or-absent (RBSRD) gates remain. Verification: grep -rn 'rbtoe_rbra_remove' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/ returns zero hits; the 12 originally-listed repair items all show as landed (NOTE-block opener consistent across 6 files, //axhoot_typed_output present in both invest specs, plural attribute used everywhere it should be, three-positional-argument form documented for both roster specs, cult-verb step headings aligned, RBSDD wrong-actor fixed, RBSDD endpoint inline, RBSRK stray precondition dropped, redundant requires dropped). Residual drift items judged non-significant for this pace (deferred): invest step-3 shape drift between RBSDK ('Preflight: Confirm SA Does Not Exist' with {rbbc_require}) and RBSRK ('Probe for Pre-existing Service Account' with prose 'Continue only on HTTP 404'); divest //axhoc_completion prose drift between RBSDD (11 lines, 2 paragraphs) and RBSRD (5 lines + 'Proceed to invest' hint); divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift; NOTE-block style inline-vs-line-broken across the 6 new specs vs. RBSDE/RBSDV (the new specs are internally consistent; AsciiDoc renders both forms identically). These are stylistic / cosmetic drift, not load-bearing or contract-violating; left for a future spec-uniformity sweep when other procedural-spec drift is being addressed.

Helper-function extraction (Option C: shared helper between mantle's pre-cleanup and depot_unmake's terminal cleanup) considered and rejected at mount time — mantle's pre-cleanup (rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270) is structurally coupled to its surrounding flow (it precedes governor regen, uses different scratch/infix discipline, and uses the same BCG-non-compliant `jq -r ... | head -N` pattern as line 341 of rbgg_Governor.sh that I surfaced for separate scope in Step B), and refactoring it would extend BBAAN scope into a tangentially-related cleanup. The success-line at line 1100 ('All infrastructure (Mason SA, repository, bucket) will be automatically removed') was left as-is since the explicit governor cleanup runs BEFORE project deletion; project deletion still handles Mason / repository / bucket / etc. via implicit cleanup. Why before lien removal: governor SA cleanup needs the project ACTIVE state for the IAM list/delete API calls; lien removal and billing unlink are subsequent prep-for-project-delete operations, so SA cleanup belongs first in the prep sequence. Why before billing unlink: billing-unlink is the first irreversible step (releases quota immediately), so any cleanup that needs the project quota-active should run earlier. No-demantle-verb posture preserved: there is no rbgg_demantle / equivalent function; depot_unmake is the sole governor-SA-cleanup consumer. bash -n clean. Regime-smoke regression check after this commit: rs_rbrr / rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix / rs_rbrp / rs_burc / rs_burs / rs_burd all PASS; rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv still fail with 'RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK / RBRV_RELIQUARY must not be empty' — same marshal-zero workspace-state failure carried forward from earlier (commit 972e1dc3, pre-BBAAN), unrelated to depot_unmake. Step F regenerates the tabtarget context next.

### 2026-04-27 14:18 - ₢BBAAN - n

Repair BCG tinder discipline: replace the over-strict 'no variable expansion' prohibition with the load-bearing invariant 'no runtime dependency'. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is now permitted — a tinder value may interpolate ${«PREFIX»_lower_name} for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves source-time availability. The original rule conflated the desired property (no runtime coupling) with a stricter mechanism (no ${} characters anywhere), forcing literal duplication of common substrings (role names, verb names) across tinder values that had no semantic justification. Four sites updated: (1) line 131 definition paragraph reshaped to declare the no-runtime-dependency invariant explicitly and call out tinder-on-tinder as permitted composition; (2) line 280 module template comment updated; (3) lines 363-377 'choosing correctly' example block expanded with a positive tinder-on-tinder example and a new ❌ kindle-constant-expansion example to mark the boundary precisely (kindle constants are unavailable at source time so their expansion still violates tinder discipline even though they're internal to the module); the existing BURD_TEMP_DIR ❌ example reframed to clarify that BURD_TEMP_DIR's runtime nature is the violation, not the ${} syntax per se; (4) line 1618 checklist item rewritten to match. Net effect: tinder constants can factor common substrings without losing source-time availability; downstream pace BBAAN's RBCC tinder enrichment (verb tinder family, mason role move, fact-ext composition) lands in the next commit consuming this rule shift.

Confirmed retired RBRR attributes (rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem) already absent from RBS0 mappings — BBAAQ Surface 4 had previously scrubbed them. The require step '«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» matches pattern rbw-*-[0-9]{12}' references the OLD timestamped project-ID shape from before BBAAM's depot-identity-collapse — under the new ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER} shape (where CLOUD_PREFIX is ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ and MONIKER is ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26), the rbw-*-[0-9]{12} pattern can never match. Replaced lines 26-27 with a NOTE following RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc:38 precedent ('Joint length ... is enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation') — replacement reads 'NOTE: Format and joint length of ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + d- + ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} are enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no shape validation'. Choice rationale: deleting outright would remove documentation value; updating regex to new shape would re-create stale-by-construction risk and re-validate already-validated upstream input; NOTE preserves documentation while matching established precedent in BBAAQ Surface 4's mint vocabulary. Verifications all green: grep for rbrr_depot_project_id|rbrr_gar_repository|rbrr_gcb_pool_stem in RBSRR returns zero hits; grep for rbrr_depot_moniker in RBS0 returns line 327 with new mapping; grep for rbw-*-[0-9]{12} in RBSGM returns zero hits. ### 2026-04-27 13:14 - ₢BBAAM - n

Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. Verification deferred to retry-after-notch in a fresh chat (operator request: prompt for clean-chat resumption to be issued).

Repair: insert 'source ${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh' into zrbgp_furnish (rbgp_cli.sh:37, after buh_handbook.sh, before rbgc_Constants.sh) — mirrors sibling-CLI source ordering. One-line addition. Skipping zrbrr_enforce is correct (depot-list doesn't need depot-moniker validation — BBAAS's stated decoupling intent); skipping zrbdc_kindle is wrong because zrbdc_kindle defines RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE (line 77 of rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh: '${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}'), which the depot-list path immediately needs for Payor OAuth. This is the identical over-gate I almost made in Cycle 1's rbte_cli.sh first-pass; operator counterpoint ('is the issue that a kindle needs to be guarded, or should we be simply guarding _enforce?') pulled me back to gate-only-enforce for that file; the same counterpoint applies symmetrically to BBAAS's existing gate. Why BBAAT didn't catch: BBAAT's verification was build + fast suite + enrollment-validation only — BBAAS's commit message (4d810db7) explicitly deferred service-tier verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 to BBAAM cycle resumption, and BBAAT's notch (9af836de) repeated the deferral. The deferral is exactly what this cycle is exercising. Repair: move zrbdc_kindle out of the gated if-block in zrbgp_furnish (rbgp_cli.sh:56-60) so it runs unconditionally, leaving only zrbrr_enforce gated on z_command != rbgp_depot_list. Same shape as Cycle 1's rbte_cli.sh fix — zrbdc_kindle does no validation, just readonly string composition; with empty RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX it produces harmless nonsense for RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID / RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY / RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM / RBDC_GCS_BUCKET that depot_list never reads. Verification deferred to retry-after-notch.

### 2026-04-27 12:39 - ₢BBAAM - n

Extend BBAAS's depot-agnostic-Payor pattern from rbgp_cli.sh into the theurge probe furnish path. Cycle 1 of BBAAM verification uncovered tt/rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.payor.sh failing at zrbte_furnish_probe with 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' against blank-template RBRR (post-marshal-zero state). The Payor probe is depot-agnostic by design — it targets RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID via OAuth + CRM projects.get (rbgv_AccessProbe.sh:232-283), exercising upstream-credential health on the Payor's own GCP project; never references RBDC depot identity. But zrbte_furnish_probe (Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_cli.sh:47-73) was a single all-or-nothing furnish path used for all four probe roles (governor/director/retriever/payor), unconditionally calling zrbrr_enforce which mandates RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER per the BBAAM Step 1 enrollment in rbrr_regime.sh:50 (1-26 mandatory) and format check at rbrr_regime.sh:94-95. Coupling existed pre-BBAAM in shape but didn't bite because pre-BBAAM enrollment of the moniker was permissive; BBAAM's mandate exposed it. BBAAS (commit 4d810db7, ₢BBAAS) had already established the architectural pattern — Payor-tier operations that don't need a depot are decoupled from RBRR enforcement — at rbgp_cli.sh:56-60 for rbgp_depot_list (skip zrbrr_enforce + zrbdc_kindle). BBAAS's scope was the rbgp Payor CLI; it didn't propagate to the theurge probe path. Repair extends the same shape to zrbte_furnish_probe: read BURD_TOKEN_3 (role imprint), gate zrbrr_enforce on role != 'payor', leave zrbdc_kindle unconditional. The unconditional zrbdc_kindle differs from rbgp_cli.sh's stricter form deliberately — zrbdc_kindle does no validation, just readonly string composition (rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:73-91). Lines 73-78 derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR + role names (RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE is exactly what the Payor probe's OAuth flow needs); lines 84-91 compose depot identity from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER but produce harmless nonsense ('d-', '-gar', '-pool', 'b-') when those are empty, and the Payor probe never reads them. So leaving zrbdc_kindle unconditional both feeds the Payor probe what it needs (rbro file path) and avoids cascading kindle skips into downstream modules. Verification: tt/rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.payor.sh passes 5/5 iterations with HTTP 200 against blank-template RBRR. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 24/27 — 3 failures (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) match exactly the BBAAT/BBAAS baseline flagged in their notch commits as 'pre-existing post-marshal-zero blank-template state', none exercise probe code, not regressions from this edit. Initial first-pass also gated zrbdc_kindle on role!=payor; operator counterpoint surfaced over-gating (the user's question: 'is the issue that a kindle needs to be guarded, or should we be simply guarding _enforce?') — second pass relaxed to gate only zrbrr_enforce, which is the actual validation point. SA probes (governor/director/retriever) continue through the full enforcement path unchanged. Cycle 1 of BBAAM verification advanced one step: Payor health probe step is now reachable on blank-template RBRR; next cycle continues from this state into marshal-zero + commit and pristine-lifecycle fixture cases.

### 2026-04-27 12:06 - ₢BBAAT - n

BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list — design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list → CRM lookup → lifecycle gate → SA list → Mason scan → emit} replacing {Billing list → billing-enabled filter → SA list → Mason scan → CRM lookup → emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup — replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE → COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED → DELETE_REQUESTED, else → skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh — Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit — BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction — BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA → moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs → 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly — same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

### 2026-04-27 11:34 - ₢BBAAS - n

Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is no longer emitted — Mason SA presence is the discriminator, so a project missing a Mason SA is not classified as a depot at all. State machine collapses to {COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED}; the BROKEN constant remains in rbgc_Constants.sh for backward compatibility but no code path emits it. New constant RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT='depot-project' added next to RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT — emit now writes a sidecar fact file '<moniker>.depot-project' carrying the canonical project_id since project_id can no longer be composed from ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${moniker}; rbgp_depot_list render reads both fact files keyed by moniker. RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX is fully removed from the depot_list code path. zrbgp_furnish in rbgp_cli.sh gets differential dispatch via local -r z_command='${1:-}' — when the command is rbgp_depot_list, zrbrr_enforce and zrbdc_kindle are skipped (zrbrr_kindle still runs to source rbrr.env into env, which is harmless when the file is absent or fields are blank); levy/unmake/install/governor-mantle keep the strict path. Spec sweep: RBSDL-depot_list.adoc fully rewritten to describe the new Billing v1 → IAM SA list → Mason classifier → CRM lifecycle → dual-fact-file flow, BROKEN state removed, COMPLETE/DELETE_REQUESTED only in the display summary; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbtoe_depot_list_update pattern updated to the same new flow (Billing v1 enumeration, Mason SA classifier, CRM lifecycle lookup, dual fact-file emission), dropping the old prose about CRM filter 'projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE' and the rbw-[name]-[timestamp] pattern validation. RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc audited — no listing-semantics prose to update; left untouched. Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

Doc comment updated to describe the autodetect+zero-arg lifecycle. Reordered: 'Authenticate as Payor' becomes step 1 with the single 'local z_token' capture, 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' becomes step 2 reusing that token. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.

### 2026-04-27 08:11 - ₢BBAAM - n

Step 1 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: kindle infrastructure plus mechanical sweep. RBRR drops RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM and adds RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (format ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26). RBDC adds RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID (${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}), RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar), RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool), and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET (${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}); type markers and pool/tether/airgap suffixes are literal strings inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering (existing furnishers run rbdc-then-rbgc in most modules, rbgc-then-rbdc in others — uniformity left for a future cleanup). RBGC drops obsolete RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX and RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX (project_id and bucket are now installation-prefix-anchored, not timestamp-suffixed) and gains depot-state vocabulary tinder constants (RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED) plus RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT for the per-moniker fact-file shape coming in Step 4. RBGD switches RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID throughout, drops the prefix-strip-and-timestamp depot-name extraction logic (RBGD_DEPOT_NAME is now just RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), and reshapes RBGD_GCS_BUCKET via RBDC_GCS_BUCKET. Marshal-zero (rblm_cli.sh) banner copy and sed cases adjusted to the new field set. Compose bridge: rbob_bottle.sh writes a temp env file (rbob_rbdc_compose.env) at zrbob_kindle with RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, added to the docker compose --env-file list; .rbk/rbob_compose.yml image refs switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY to the RBDC equivalents. Operator's .rbk/rbrr.env reshaped to the new field set with RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=depot10041 placeholder. Mechanical RBRR_X -> RBDC_X sweep across all consumers: rbgg_Governor, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbgp_Payor (depot-list regex sites replaced with string-prefix match against ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-, post-levy 'Update RBRR configuration' banner replaced with informational 'Depot resources created' summary, governor-mantle docstring and error message rewritten around the new derivation), rbgp_cli (differential-furnish comment annotated Step-2-pending), rbgb_Buckets, rbga_ArtifactRegistry, rbob_bottle (GAR ref via RBDC), rbrn_cli, rbfv_FoundryVerify, rbfc_FoundryCore, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbfl_FoundryLedger, rbhpq_quota_build (also RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM -> RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM), rbhpb_base. Onboarding probes that file-scan rbrr.env to detect 'depot configured' switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: rbhocc_crash_course, rbhodf_director_first_build, rbhoda_director_airgap, rbhodb_director_bind, rbhodg_director_graft. Theurge minimal-touch: rbtdrp_pristine.rs RBTDRP_RBRR_BLANK_FIELDS shrinks to {CLOUD_PREFIX, RUNTIME_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER}; helper rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id renamed to rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker (case 3 call site updated, but the ordering is service-tier-broken until Step 5 reshapes case 3). rbtdrf_fast.rs negative tests rbrr-missing-project-id -> rbrr-missing-moniker (unset RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) and rbrr-bad-pool-stem -> rbrr-bad-moniker (export RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=BAD-MONIKER triggers format check). Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58 unit tests), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97 across enrollment-validation/regime-validation/regime-smoke/handbook-render), vow-b. Step 2 (rbgp_depot_levy reshape — drop timestamp, drop CLI arg, use RBDC, drop post-levy banner) is the next pace; rbgp_depot_levy in this state is left functionally intact with its old CLI-arg flow but its post-creation values can no longer be 'pasted into RBRR' (they're derived now), so the function is effectively unusable until Step 2 lands.
